run_query: SELECT o.ticker, o.foreign_pct FROM ownership o JOIN summary s USING(ticker) WHERE s.market_cap>5e13 ORDER BY o.foreign_pct DESC -- identifikasi nama yang paling rawan capital outflow saat risk-off

picks.rationale: Consumer staple makanan-minuman, revenue ~100% rupiah/domestik -> safe-haven IDR; sudah -29% 1y (murah, P/B ~1,7) jadi downside terbatas, defensif inti yang dipertahankan.
Jamu/herbal, ROE ~37% tertinggi di kesehatan, neraca nyaris tanpa utang & bahan baku lokal -> kebal tarif & IDR lemah, tambah sebagai defensif berkualitas.
Induk ICBP, P/B ~0,49 paling murah di staples, yield solid; diversifikasi (Bogasari kena impor gandum dolar tapi punya pricing power) -> hold sebagai value-defensive.
Batu bara, revenue USD biaya rupiah -> diuntungkan rupiah lemah; yield ~12,5% jadi bantalan saat risk-off, hedge alami yang ditambahkan ke porto.
CPO/sawit, revenue ekspor dolar + biaya rupiah, yield ~8,9% & ROE ~34% -> diuntungkan IDR lemah, hedge komoditas selain batu bara.
Inti manufaktur porto yang paling rawan dipangkas: otomotif sensitif daya beli + risiko margin bila IDR lemah naikkan biaya komponen impor; trim, jangan add.

answers.risk: Thesis batal kalau tarif justru memicu BI MEMOTONG suku bunga (bukan menahan) untuk dukung pertumbuhan, dan/atau pemerintah deal dengan AS sehingga tarif efektif Indonesia turun di bawah 25% — itu membalik arah: bank & properti/tech rate-sensitive (BBCA, CTRA, GOTO) malah rally dan rotasi ke staples/komoditas jadi underperform. Risiko kedua: China stimulus besar -> permintaan batu bara/CPO melonjak membuat ADRO/TAPG outperform staples (skenario ini menguatkan picks komoditas tapi melemahkan staples). Pemicu invalidasi konkret: IDR menguat menembus level kunci + foreign net inflow balik positif ke BBCA/BBRI selama beberapa minggu beruntun.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia is a net crude importer (Pertamina fuel-subsidy bill) but a structural net energy exporter via thermal coal and oil-linked LNG, so a Hormuz-closure/$120 Brent shock is fiscally negative on oil yet positive for the IDX-listed energy complex, which is ~90% coal/gas, not E&P — pure oil names like MEDC aren't even in the brief, so the real "oil hedge" on IDX is the coal/gas dividend complex (ADRO, PTBA, PGAS, BYAN) whose USD revenue re-rates on oil-coal substitution and oil-indexed LNG contracts. The second-order transmission is FX/rates: a wider oil-import bill pressures IDR, forcing BI to stay hawkish to defend the rupiah and cap imported inflation, which simultaneously fattens USD-revenue exporters and crushes IDR-cost, rate-sensitive importers. Victims are clear and balance-sheet-deep: GIAA (jet fuel ~35% of opex plus USD leases/debt on an already distressed carrier) and cement (SMGR/INTP — coal/diesel kiln energy is a huge COGS line while property demand weakens as BI holds), so the trade is long coal-dividend/gas vs short fuel-and-FX-exposed cyclicals. The hedge is real but partial: coal's correlation to Brent is via the thermal-coal/LNG channel, not 1:1, and the coal complex is already a crowded high-yield trade (BYAN down 46% trailing shows it can de-rate on China demand independent of oil). Net: a tilt into IDX coal-gas dividend names is a sensible, cash-generative hedge against an oil-supply shock, but size it as a coal/LNG-beta trade, not a pure-oil play.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, company_name, sector, market_cap, dividend_yield, price_to_book, return_on_equity, beta FROM latest WHERE sector = 'Energi' AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC;

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y, r.ret_6m FROM latest l JOIN returns r ON r.ticker = l.ticker WHERE l.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','PGAS','BYAN','BSSR','GEMS','AADI','ITMG') ORDER BY r.ret_6m DESC;
SELECT ticker, sector, price_to_book, return_on_equity, beta FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('SMGR','INTP','GIAA','JSMR') OR (sector = 'Barang Baku' AND company_name ILIKE '%semen%');
SELECT ticker, date, broker_code, net_value FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','PGAS','BYAN') AND broker_code IN ('BK','AK','KZ') ORDER BY date DESC, net_value DESC LIMIT 40;

picks.rationale: Cleanest oil-shock hedge: USD-denominated coal revenue, 12.5% yield, balance-sheet cash to defend dividend; coal re-rates on oil-coal substitution and a weaker IDR lifts rupiah earnings.
The closest IDX gas play: oil-indexed LNG/gas contracts and USD revenue benefit directly from $120 Brent, 9.9% yield, defensive infra cash flow — the truest 'oil & gas' proxy in the brief.
State-backed thermal coal, 10.7% yield, domestic+export mix; gains on coal price lift and IDR weakness, lower China-demand beta than the premium names.
Largest IDX coal heavyweight (mcap 373tn, ROE 31%) down 46% trailing — highest-torque re-rating candidate if oil drags coal up, but the volatility/illiquid-float warning sits here.
Highest coal dividend in the brief (15.4%), small-cap USD coal exporter — maximum carry while you wait for the oil-coal correlation to play out.

Avoid / funding short: cheapest P/B (0.40) is a value trap in this scenario — coal/diesel kiln energy is a major COGS line and property demand weakens as BI stays hawkish, the cement 'victim' leg of the trade.

answers.risk: The hedge breaks if the oil spike is transient (Hormuz reopens / SPR + OPEC spare capacity caps Brent) or if China thermal-coal demand and seaborne coal prices roll over independently of oil — BYAN's -46% trailing shows coal can de-rate on its own cycle, decoupling the 'oil hedge.' Compounding risk: a sustained oil shock that forces BI to hike hard and Jakarta to widen fuel subsidies could trigger broad foreign outflows from IDX (bank-heavy index), and a coal windfall/export-levy or DMO policy response would claw back exporter upside — so the long coal-dividend / short cement-airline spread is the more robust expression than an outright IDX energy beta bet.

answers.reasoning: Transmission: CNY is the anchor for EM Asia FX, so a retaliatory yuan devaluation drags IDR down with it; BI defends with FX intervention and a higher-for-longer rate stance, which simultaneously triggers foreign outflows from IDX (banks, the most-liquid and most foreign-held large caps like BBCA/BMRI/BBNI, absorb the selling) and inflates the IDR cost of servicing USD debt. The emiten that get hurt are USD-debt-heavy, IDR-revenue names — property (PANI, BSDE, CTRA, LPKR) and rate-sensitive tech (GOTO, BUKA, DCII) — because their liabilities reprice in USD while their cash flows stay in weakening rupiah. The natural shelters are dollar earners and net-cash balance sheets: coal/energy exporters (BYAN, PTBA, ITMG, UNTR) price and settle in USD, so a weaker IDR is a tailwind to translated earnings, and the Indonesian coal majors carry little-to-no USD debt and sit on net cash, decoupling them from the FX-debt squeeze. SIDO adds a domestic, zero-USD-debt consumer-staple hedge that is insulated from both the currency translation and the foreign-flow rotation. Net: own USD revenue + net cash, avoid IDR revenue + USD debt.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Energi','Perindustrian') AND dividend_yield > 6 AND return_on_equity > 15 AND price_to_book < 3 -- USD-revenue exporters with strong cash returns as FX hedge candidates

SELECT f.ticker, f.total_debt, f.total_cash, f.total_debt/NULLIF(f.total_equity,0) AS de, f.usd_debt_pct FROM fundamentals f JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE c.sector IN ('Energi','Perindustrian','Kesehatan') AND (f.total_cash >= f.total_debt OR f.usd_debt_pct < 0.2) ORDER BY de ASC -- net-cash / low-USD-leverage filter

SELECT s.ticker, s.return_on_equity, s.dividend_yield, s.beta, s.price_to_book FROM summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('BYAN','PTBA','ITMG','UNTR','SIDO') -- verify low beta, valuation and yield cushion on the shortlist

SELECT ticker, ROUND(100*(ret_1y),1) AS ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','PTBA','ITMG','UNTR','SIDO','PANI','BSDE','GOTO','DCII') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC -- contrast resilient exporters vs rate/debt-sensitive losers

picks.rationale: State-owned coal, USD-linked pricing with a net-cash balance sheet and negligible USD debt; 10.7% yield cushions FX volatility while weaker IDR lifts translated earnings.
USD coal revenue, persistently net cash with no meaningful USD borrowings — a pure FX-resilient exporter that benefits from rupiah depreciation rather than suffering from it.
Coal sold in USD with low-leverage, near net-cash balance sheet (ROE 31%); IDR weakness inflates IDR-translated earnings, offsetting any China-demand drag on the commodity.
Diversified heavy-equipment, mining-contracting and gold with partial USD revenue, low net gearing and strong cash generation; 6.7% yield and conservative balance sheet weather both FX and outflows.
Domestic herbal staple, essentially zero USD debt and net cash (ROE 37%); demand is IDR-domestic and defensive, insulated from currency translation and foreign-flow rotation.

answers.risk: The thesis is really a coal/commodity bet wearing an FX hedge. It breaks if a China devaluation comes bundled with a hard China demand slowdown that craters coal and thermal volumes/prices (a real risk — BYAN is already -46% 1y) — then USD-revenue resilience is swamped by a lower USD price, and the export tailwind reverses. It also weakens if BI out-hikes the shock and IDR stabilizes fast, removing the translation tailwind, or if a coal windfall/export levy or DMO change taxes away the FX upside. SIDO is the cleanest hedge against that commodity tail but carries the least upside.

answers.reasoning: Net ini risiko dulu, peluang belakangan. Shock chip Taiwan memicu resesi manufaktur global sehingga permintaan baja nirkarat (penyerap sekitar 70 persen nikel dunia) dan EV anjlok, sementara harga nikel LME yang sudah tertekan banjir pasok kelas-2 Indonesia makin jatuh, dan karena nikel serta batu bara IDX diekspor dalam USD top-line emiten kena langsung. Transmisi makro: risk-off global memicu outflow asing dari IDX (ANTM dan BRMS yang paling likuid jadi keluar duluan), IDR melemah ke kisaran 16.5 sampai 17 ribu, BI menahan atau menaikkan rate demi menjaga rupiah, dan multiple seluruh pasar tertekan bukan cuma tambang. Hilirisasi EV memang struktural lewat smelter dan larangan ekspor bijih, tapi itu tesis 3 sampai 5 tahun yang justru menderita di siklus turun karena emiten nikel Indonesia high-capex dan high-debt sehingga sensitif ke biaya pendanaan saat rate tinggi. Posisi terbaik bukan all-in nikel melainkan barbell: nama logam dengan neraca terkuat untuk eksposur struktural, diimbangi emas safe-haven yang naik saat risk-off plus dividend yield batu bara tinggi sebagai bantalan arus kas saat harga logam industri melemah.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, market_cap, roe, dividend_yield, price_to_book FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','NICL','BRMS','MDKA','EMAS','CITA') ORDER BY market_cap DESC

SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_60d FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','BRMS','AMMN','MDKA','NICL') AND date >= current_date - INTERVAL 60 DAY GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign_60d
SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ret_3m, ret_1m FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','BRMS','AMMN','MDKA','EMAS','PTBA','ADRO','BSSR') ORDER BY ret_3m
screen_stocks sector=Barang Baku min_market_cap=5e12 sort_by=dividend_yield order=desc, plus sector=Energi min_dividend_yield=10 for coal hedge leg

picks.rationale: Proxy nikel plus emas paling likuid di IDX 496 rp bn per hari; kaki emas hedge alami saat risk-off tapi paling rentan jadi pintu keluar outflow asing, inti barbell bukan all-in
Eksposur emas; di shock Taiwan emas naik sebagai safe-haven sementara nikel jatuh, kaki defensif yang membiayai taruhan struktural EV
Satu-satunya nama nikel di brief dengan ROE tinggi 42 persen; kualitas neraca terbaik untuk pegang tesis hilirisasi melewati siklus turun bukan smelter pembakar kas
Tembaga dan emas Barang Baku 375 tn bukan murni nikel sehingga lebih terikat ke listrik dan grid global; ROE 5 persen high-capex paling sensitif ke rate tinggi, kecilkan bobot dan beli kelemahan
Kaki bantalan arus kas; yield sekitar 10.7 persen batu bara mendanai portofolio saat harga logam industri melemah, korelasi rendah ke siklus EV
Yield sekitar 12.5 persen dengan neraca kuat; hedging dividen energi menahan drawdown sementara menunggu re-akselerasi permintaan nikel dan EV

answers.risk: Tesis barbell batal kalau shock pendek dan dangkal sehingga rebound demand membuat beta nikel dan EV menang dan kaki emas plus batu bara cuma jadi pemberat; atau kalau pasok kelas-2 Indonesia terus membanjiri pasar sehingga harga nikel LME tetap tertekan secara struktural meski demand pulih, membuat emiten nikel kalah ke emas dan batu bara di skenario makro apa pun. Invalidator konkret: net_foreign di eod_summary untuk ANTM dan BRMS berbalik positif dan ret_3m di view returns berbalik naik sementara nikel LME pulih di atas biaya tunai smelter, saat itu tambah bobot ANTM dan NICL dan kurangi kaki hedging. Risiko kebijakan: pelonggaran kuota ekspor bijih atau pajak ekspor baru bisa mengubah ekonomi smelter mendadak. Catatan data: INCO dan MBMA yang disebut di pertanyaan tidak ada di brief jadi tidak dipakai sebagai pick sampai diverifikasi via search_tickers.

answers.reasoning: Skenario "higher-for-longer" Fed + oil/tariff inflation menekan EM via dua kanal Indonesia-spesifik: (1) carry/yield differential menyempit, USD/IDR melemah, dan BI terpaksa menahan BI-rate tinggi untuk membela rupiah, yang menghantam multiple bank dan saham rate-sensitive (properti, tech). (2) Foreign net sell di IDX secara mekanis terkonsentrasi di nama paling likuid dan paling besar bobot indeksnya — yaitu trio bank BBCA/BBRI/BMRI — karena asing menjual yang BISA dijual, bukan yang ingin dijual. Data trading 2025 membuktikannya: di 10 hari foreign-outflow terberat, BBCA/BBRI/BMRI menyerap net sell asing Rp5.5tn/2.8tn/2.5tn dan justru bank BUMN paling babak belur (BBRI -2.5%, BBNI -2.3%, BMRI -2.2% rata-rata per hari stres), sementara nama komoditas float-rendah dan consumer staples nyaris flat. Catatan kunci: hipotesis "ownership asing kecil = resilient" benar arahnya tapi salah ukur — tabel ownership meremehkan eksposur asing bank (blok pengendali tercatat lokal/kustodian); metrik yang benar adalah foreign trading float, dan justru itu yang absen pada staples/komoditas low-float. Oil shock juga mengubah tanda: ANTM (emas, safe-haven) dan batu bara (BYAN/PTBA/BSSR) malah mendapat tailwind harga komoditas saat inflasi global naik.
tool_queries: WITH fn AS (SELECT date, SUM(CASE WHEN side='S' THEN value ELSE -value END) AS foreign_net_sell FROM broker_activity WHERE domicile='FOREIGN' GROUP BY date) SELECT date, foreign_net_sell/1e9 net_sell_rp_bn FROM fn ORDER BY foreign_net_sell DESC LIMIT 15  -- isolate the real foreign-outflow stress days in 2025

WITH stress AS (SELECT date FROM (SELECT date, SUM(CASE WHEN side='S' THEN value ELSE -value END) fns FROM broker_activity WHERE domicile='FOREIGN' GROUP BY date) ORDER BY fns DESC LIMIT 10), px AS (SELECT ticker, date, close/lag(close) OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date)-1 ret FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','ANTM','BYAN','PTBA','ICBP','INDF','SIDO','UNVR')) SELECT ticker, avg(ret)*100 avg_stressday_ret_pct FROM px JOIN stress USING(date) GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY 2 DESC  -- rank big-caps by mean same-day return on the 10 worst outflow days

SELECT ticker, SUM(CASE WHEN side='S' THEN value ELSE -value END)/1e9 foreign_net_sell_rp_bn FROM broker_activity WHERE domicile='FOREIGN' AND date IN (<stress_days>) GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY 2 DESC  -- show WHICH names foreigners actually dumped (banks) vs barely touched (ANTM/BYAN/PTBA)

SELECT ticker, sum(CASE WHEN local_foreign='FOREIGN' THEN percentage ELSE 0 END) registered_foreign_pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','ASII','BYAN','ANTM','ICBP','UNVR') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY 2 DESC  -- cross-check registered foreign ownership vs trading-float exposure (proves the ownership table understates bank foreign risk)

picks.rationale: Paling resilient di hari stres (rata-rata +0.08%) dengan foreign net sell minim; emas = safe-haven yang justru diuntungkan oil/inflation shock, hedging alami terhadap pelemahan IDR.

Flat (+0.08%) saat outflow puncak dan asing nyaris tidak menjual (net -Rp1bn); float publik sangat tipis sehingga tidak ikut dalam keranjang jual asing, plus tailwind harga batu bara saat energy shock.
Staple defensif terbaik (-0.49%) dengan foreign trading-float kecil; demand mi instan inelastis, pendapatan domestik IDR, paling tahan saat asing kabur.
Tahan banting (-0.75%) dengan foreign net sell hanya Rp67bn; yield ~10.7% jadi bantalan, dan batu bara dapat tailwind harga di oil shock — pegangan dividen yang tidak diburu asing.

Diserap paling banyak oleh jual asing (Rp5.5tn) TAPI tetap turun paling sedikit di antara bank besar (-1.62% vs BUMN -2.2..-2.5%); premium kualitas/CASA membuatnya jadi bank paling layak HOLD jika harus punya bank, bukan SOE.
Asing malah net beli tipis (-Rp22bn) saat stres dan turun moderat (-1.16%); holdco staple murah dengan eksposur asing rendah, melengkapi keranjang defensif domestik.

answers.risk: Tesis ini gugur jika oil/inflation shock berbalik jadi commodity-DEMAND bust (resesi global): ANTM/BYAN/PTBA kehilangan tailwind harga sekaligus, dan korelasi safe-haven emas bisa putus jika real yield AS melonjak. Risiko kedua: ukuran "low foreign ownership = aman" menyesatkan — resiliensi sebenarnya berasal dari trading-float/likuiditas rendah, jadi nama low-float ini ILIKUID; saat panic-sell berlanjut, justru sulit keluar dan bisa gap down tajam (bid kosong), kebalikan dari likuiditas dalam pada BBCA. Ketiga, jika BI memangkas rate lebih cepat dari ekspektasi (IDR stabil, outflow reda), bank BUMN yang paling tertekan (BBRI/BMRI) akan rebound paling kencang — keranjang defensif ini akan underperform dramatis di skenario risk-on. Terakhir, data hanya 2025 (1 tahun, 10 hari stres) — sampel kecil, bukan siklus penuh.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia's nickel hilirisasi is not ore-constrained, it is China-input-constrained: the HPAL/RKEF smelter technology, the sulphur/reagent chemistry, the bulk of the project equity, and the battery-grade offtake all sit with Chinese partners (Lygend, Tsingshan, CATL/Brunp, Huayou/GEM, Jinsheng). A harder US tech embargo that pushes Beijing to weaponize nickel-processing and rare-earth exports cuts both ways for Jakarta: it could lift LME/MHP and Class-1 prices (positive for ANTM/INCO ore + matte) but simultaneously chokes the capital and equipment pipeline that the new HPAL plants in Obi/Morowali depend on, and risks Indonesian projects being caught in secondary-sanction / dual-use screening, which would freeze the Chinese-funded capex that drives these stocks' growth narrative. The macro backdrop amplifies the equity risk: with BI holding rates to defend a soft IDR and foreign flows thin, these are high-beta (NCKL beta ~1.5), low-yield growth names that have already run 70-270% in twelve months on flawless-execution assumptions, so any China-capital disruption hits them through both an earnings channel (stalled smelter ramp, offtake reprice) and a multiple channel (momentum/foreign-flow unwind). The most dangerous exposure is therefore concentrated where Chinese strategic equity and Chinese-tech HPAL JVs are the load-bearing asset and the balance sheet is private rather than state-backed (DKFT, NCKL, MBMA), while MIND ID-controlled ANTM and Vale/Sumitomo-controlled INCO are comparatively insulated on ownership even if their share prices still carry the most momentum to give back.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Barang Baku', keyword_in_background IN ['nikel','smelter','HPAL']) to enumerate the downstream nickel universe and their sub_sector/market_cap

run_query: SELECT ticker, name, type, classification, local_foreign, percentage FROM read_parquet('ownership/latest.parquet') WHERE ticker IN ('NCKL','MBMA','INCO','ANTM','DKFT','NICE','IFSH','NICL') AND percentage>=4.5 ORDER BY ticker, percentage DESC  -- isolate Chinese-controlled stakes (Jinsheng, Huayong, Lygend)

run_query trailing-1y return from prices via ASOF: WITH last AS (SELECT ticker, last(close ORDER BY date) c, max(date) d FROM prices GROUP BY ticker) ... join price ~365d prior, compute ret_1y for the nickel tickers to size how much momentum is at risk

run_query: SELECT ticker, market_cap, price_to_book, return_on_equity, dividend_yield, beta, recommendation_key FROM read_parquet('summary/latest.parquet') WHERE ticker IN (nickel set) -- gauge valuation/leverage/beta and analyst posture (treat yf ratios directionally per the unreliable-ratio caveat)

picks.rationale: Most direct China-capital casualty: 61.6% controlled by PT Jinsheng Mining (Chinese), tiny ~Rp3.8tn cap, up ~183% in 1y on the smelter narrative; a Chinese-capital/offtake freeze hits the controlling shareholder itself.

Harita's HPAL flagship is the showcase of Chinese (Ningbo Lygend) processing tech at the asset level; high-beta (1.54), strong_buy crowd, +70% and growth-priced, so a China-tech/capex disruption reprices both earnings ramp and multiple.

Battery-materials growth story leans entirely on Chinese JVs (Huayong HK on the register 7.6% plus CATL/Brunp and Tsingshan HPAL partners); +143% with no dividend and nosebleed implied multiples, maximum sensitivity to a China-capital pullback.

State (MIND ID 65%) ownership insulates the balance sheet, but its battery downstream rides Chinese (Ningbo/CBL) JVs and the stock is +134% in 1y, so it carries the most momentum to unwind on a flow/sentiment shock even if ownership risk is lower.

Contrast/relative-haven pick: controlled by Vale Canada + Sumitomo (foreign but non-Chinese, 48% foreign), so China exposure is offtake/HPAL-partner not equity; better positioned if Beijing weaponizes processing while LME nickel rallies.

answers.risk: Thesis inverts if Chinese export controls tighten the global supply of refined/Class-1 nickel and MHP enough that LME and battery-grade prices spike: that windfalls the Indonesian downstream margin and the very names flagged as fragile (NCKL, MBMA, INCO) could re-rate higher despite the capital risk. It also assumes Chinese partners actually pull or freeze capital rather than double down on Indonesian capacity to bypass US-facing supply chains, and that Jakarta does not backstop the JVs (state banks/DHE rules) to keep hilirisasi flagship projects ramping. The yf valuation ratios for these names are unreliable (PB/PE wildly distorted), so position sizing should lean on the ownership-structure and 1y-momentum evidence, not the reported multiples; if the China stakes turn out to be passive/minority at the listco level (e.g., true HPAL equity sits in unlisted opcos), the listed-equity blast radius is smaller than the headline implies.

answers.reasoning: An export-restriction-driven CPO spike is a price shock, not a demand boom: Indonesia would be cutting volumes via DMO/levy/quota, so realized upside accrues to upstream nucleus-estate planters who keep selling at higher domestic-linked prices, NOT to refiner-exporters whose European volume gets sanctioned away. The transmission is FX-amplified — CPO is USD-priced and IDR sits weak versus a still-restrictive BI rate, so rupiah revenue per ton inflates even before the price move, which is why high-ROE, low-leverage upstream names (SSMS ROE 41, NSSS ROE 47, TAPG ROE 34) convert the spike to cash fastest while heavily-indebted downstream/refining names see margin compression as feedstock costs rise and the EU offtake disappears. But this is a laggard sector, not a momentum trade: Barang Konsumen Primer averages only ~96% trailing-1y vs Energi 120% / Perindustrian 128%, and AALI/LSIP don't appear in any winners or top-ROE screen — the market is not paying up for palm, which means you are buying an event, and if the restriction is jawboning rather than a hard quota the catalyst evaporates. Foreign flows won't carry these names (palm has weak foreign sponsorship and ESG/deforestation screening actively pushes EU funds out), so this is a domestic-liquidity, dividend-cushioned tactical trade timed to the policy headline, not a re-rating you hold through a demand air-pocket. Size it as an event trade with a hard invalidation, lean upstream/low-leverage, and use the 8-9% yields (TAPG 8.9) as the floor that pays you to wait.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Barang Konsumen Primer', tickers IN ('TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','AALI','LSIP','SIMP','DSNG'), fields: roe, pb, de_ratio, div_yield, mcap) — rank the palm cluster by ROE and inverse leverage to separate upstream cash compounders from indebted downstream

SELECT l.ticker, l.roe, l.pb, l.div_yield, f.debt_to_equity, f.ebitda_margin FROM latest l JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) WHERE l.ticker IN ('TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','AALI','LSIP','SIMP','DSNG') ORDER BY f.debt_to_equity ASC — confirm DSNG/AALI carry the highest gearing (downstream/refining risk) vs SSMS/NSSS net-cash upstream

SELECT ticker, ret_1m, ret_3m, ret_1y, ann_vol FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','AALI','LSIP','SIMP','DSNG') ORDER BY ret_3m DESC — check whether a CPO move is already priced in (momentum) or these are still laggards (entry edge)

SELECT ticker, foreign_pct, foreign_chg_3m FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','AALI','LSIP','SIMP','DSNG') — gauge foreign positioning/ESG-driven outflow that caps the EU-demand-loss leg of the thesis

picks.rationale: Best risk/reward of the named four: ROE 34 + 8.9% yield among the highest of any palm name, upstream-tilted so a domestic-linked CPO spike drops to cash while the dividend pays you to hold through EU-demand noise.
Pure upstream nucleus-estate planter, ROE 41 and low refining/EU exposure — the cleanest beneficiary of a restriction-driven price shock, minimal sanction risk on the demand side.
Highest ROE in the cluster (47), small/efficient upstream operator — highest operating leverage to a CPO price melt-up, the aggressive sleeve of the trade.

Largest, most liquid palm proxy and the institutional vehicle for the theme, but more refined-export/EU exposed and a trailing laggard (absent from all winners screens) — own it as the index expression, not the alpha.
Cash-rich, low-debt upstream Salim/Astra name — defensive way to play the spike with balance-sheet ballast if the EU-demand-loss leg bites and prices give back.

Highest-risk pick: integrated upstream+downstream with the most leverage, so it has the largest beta to a CPO up-move but compresses worst if EU offtake vanishes and feedstock costs rise — trade only if the restriction is confirmed hard policy.

answers.risk: The thesis dies if the export restriction is political jawboning rather than a hard quota/levy — then the CPO spike fails to materialize and you're left holding a laggard sector with no catalyst. The second invalidation is the demand leg actually biting: losing EU offtake (Indonesia's deforestation-sanction counterparty) without a price-restriction offset turns this into a volume-loss story, hitting the refiner/downstream-exposed names (DSNG, AALI) hardest as inventories build and CPO rolls over. Compounding both: ESG/EUDR screening structurally suppresses foreign sponsorship of palm, so there's no flow tailwind to re-rate the group, and a stronger IDR or a BI cut that revives non-commodity sectors would rotate domestic liquidity away. Net: this is a tactical, dividend-cushioned event trade with a hard stop on the policy headline — not a buy-and-hold.

answers.reasoning: A trade-war + oil-shock recession hits Indonesia through three transmission channels: (1) commodity terms-of-trade — coal/nickel/CPO export receipts collapse, which is ~half of IDX's earnings weight and the fiscal swing factor, dragging energy/materials and the banks that lend to them; (2) the IDR — global risk-off plus a current-account hit forces BI to defend the rupiah by holding rates high even into a downturn, which compresses property/tech multiples and slows the rate-cut relief banks need; (3) foreign flows — IDX large caps (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/TLKM) are the offshore liquidity valve, so foreigners sell the index leaders first, mechanically over-correcting the highest-quality defensives below fair value. The actual data confirms the playbook: the realized beta field cleanly separates true defensives (ICBP, AMRT, INDF, KLBF, JSMR, TLKM, BBCA all sit at beta ≤0.15, several negative) from cyclicals, while clean P/B shows the cheap optionality (BSDE 0.37, SMGR 0.40, CTRA 0.55, BBNI 0.80). With a 5-year horizon the right structure is to overweight cash now (30-40%) as dry powder, hold low-beta consumer-staples/telco/healthcare for the core, and stage limit-buy tranches into oversold cyclical quality (banks at trough P/B, coal dividend payers, ASII as the conglomerate proxy) only after the 20%+ index drawdown actually prints.
tool_queries: SELECT s.ticker, c.sector, s.beta, s.price_to_book, s.dividend_yield, s.recommendation_key FROM summary s JOIN companies c ON s.ticker=c.ticker WHERE c.sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Infrastruktur','Kesehatan') AND s.beta < 0.2 AND s.market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY s.beta ASC -- find low-beta defensives that fall least in a 20% IHSG drawdown

SELECT ticker, sector, price_to_book, beta, market_cap FROM summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE price_to_book < 1.0 AND price_to_book > 0 AND market_cap > 10e12 ORDER BY price_to_book ASC -- cheap-by-book cyclicals (banks/property/materials) for the 'blood in the streets' tranche
screen_stocks(sector='Energi', min_dividend_yield=0.09, min_market_cap=5e12, sort='dividend_yield desc') -- coal dividend payers (PTBA, ADRO, BSSR) as cash-flow cushion if oil/coal whipsaws back

SELECT t.ticker, v.ret_1y, b.foreign_net_5d FROM returns v JOIN broker_net b USING(ticker) WHERE v.ret_1y < -0.25 AND b.foreign_net_5d > 0 -- oversold large caps where foreign selling is exhausting / turning to net buy = entry trigger

picks.rationale: Core defensive: realized beta -0.06, instant-noodle/staples demand is recession-proof, and the brief shows it already -29% trailing-1y so much of the de-rating is done — first-tranche buy.
Defensive telco/infra with beta 0.15 and a real ~6.8% yield; as an index heavyweight it gets oversold by foreign outflows, giving a high-quality entry on the 20% IHSG dip.
Healthcare anchor: beta ~0.0, strong_buy consensus, IDR-resilient domestic pharma demand — exactly the sector that holds when consumer discretionary and cyclicals roll over.
Coal dividend cushion: beta -0.20 and double-digit yield (10.7% in brief); a state-owned low-debt payer that survives an oil/coal shock and pays you to wait — staged buys, not all at once.

Cyclical-quality watchlist: cheapest big bank on book (P/B 0.80, brief shows 0.49) with beta 0.25; banks lead the IHSG down on foreign selling, so this is a limit-buy at trough valuation for the rate-cut recovery leg.

The conglomerate cyclical proxy: beta 0.13, P/B ~1.07, ~6.7% yield, and embeds the UNTR/coal-equipment cycle — a single liquid name to add staged exposure to the eventual recovery without single-commodity risk.

answers.risk: Main risk is a stagflationary IDR spiral: if the oil shock keeps inflation high while growth craters, BI is forced to hold or hike rates instead of cutting, which (a) breaks the bank-recovery thesis (BBNI/ASII stay cheap for years, not quarters) and (b) crushes the coal-dividend assumption if global demand destruction caves coal prices, turning PTBA/ADRO payouts into value traps. The thesis also assumes the 20%+ drawdown is cyclical, not structural — a permanent loss of foreign-investor access to IDX (capital controls, sovereign downgrade) would mean the heavyweights (BBCA/TLKM) keep falling rather than mean-revert. Invalidation trigger: IDR breaking past ~17,500/USD with BI hiking into the recession, or coal/CPO export prices making new multi-year lows while inventories build — at that point shift further into cash/USD and pause the cyclical tranches.

answers.reasoning: Catatan jujur dulu: SMDR/TPMA/HUMI tidak ada di data brief ini, jadi aku tidak bisa verifikasi ketiganya dan tidak akan mengarangnya — di IDX yang punya likuiditas + cashflow nyata untuk thesis "freight naik 2-3x" justru rantai bulk-export, bukan pure shipping. Transmisi: spike freight seaborne dan gangguan jalur LCS menaikkan harga realisasi FOB/CFR komoditas curah Indonesia (batu bara, nikel, tembaga) sekaligus melemahkan IDR karena premi risiko + lonjakan tagihan impor energi/pangan, dan IDR lemah justru menambah margin emiten yang revenue-nya USD tapi cost rupiah (ADRO, PTBA, ITMG, ANTM, AMMN) — itu kaki long yang dibayar dividen 10-15% jadi ada carry sambil nunggu. Sisi sebaliknya: importir bahan baku (ICBP/INDF impor gandum-kedelai, UNVR/MYOR input impor) kena double hit dari freight + IDR, dan kalau BI tahan/naikkan rate untuk bela rupiah, sektor rate-sensitive berutang tinggi (properti CTRA/BSDE/SMRA) ikut tertekan — itu kaki yang kamu under-weight/funding, bukan ditambah. Cara "tidak over-expose satu arah" = struktur barbell berpasangan: long beneficiary curah-ekspor, danai dari/under-weight importir & properti rate-sensitive, lalu ballast dengan defensif domestik non-impor (TLKM, PGAS, POWR) yang cashflow-nya rupiah dan tahan terhadap arah freight maupun IDR; net beta thesis ditekan tapi spread-nya tetap kebuka.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector IN ('Energi'), mcap_tn > 10, yield_pct > 9, order by yield_pct desc) -- pin the paid-to-wait coal/bulk-export long leg (ADRO, PTBA, ITMG, BSSR, GEMS, AADI)

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.mcap_tn, r.ret_1y, f.yield_pct, f.roe_pct FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) LEFT JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) WHERE l.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','ITMG','BSSR','GEMS','BYAN','UNTR','ANTM','AMMN') ORDER BY r.ret_1y DESC -- confirm export-leg carry + momentum (note BYAN -46% 1y = idiosyncratic, screen it out)

SELECT ticker, sector, ret_1y, pe, mcap_tn FROM latest JOIN returns USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','UNVR','CTRA','BSDE','SMRA') -- size the import-cost / rate-sensitive funding leg (ICBP/AMRT already -29% 1y, validate squeeze direction)

SELECT bn.ticker, SUM(bn.net_value) AS net_foreign_5d FROM broker_net bn JOIN brokers b USING(broker_code) WHERE b.firm IN ('UBS','J.P.Morgan','CLSA','Maybank') AND bn.date >= current_date - INTERVAL 5 DAY AND bn.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','ANTM','AMMN','ICBP','INDF') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC -- read foreign-broker net flow to see if smart money already leans the same way

picks.rationale: Long leg: thermal-coal bulk exporter, revenue USD/cost IDR, freight+IDR spike lifts realisasi; dibayar 12.5% yield jadi carry sambil nunggu thesis main out.
Long leg: coal exporter SOE, 10.7% yield, leverage rendah; beneficiary harga seaborne naik tanpa risiko balance-sheet, anchor dividen kaki long.
Long leg + likuiditas (Rp496bn/hari): nikel/emas USD-priced, naik kalau IDR lemah dan jalur ekspor mineral ketat; emas juga safe-haven kalau eskalasi LCS jadi risk-off.
Long leg: tembaga, output USD, terbantu IDR lemah + premi gangguan supply; mcap besar & likuid (Rp245bn/hari) buat sizing serius.
Funding/hedge leg (under-weight/pair-short): importir gandum-kedelai-dairy, kena freight + IDR lemah langsung di COGS; sudah -29% 1y, arah squeeze terkonfirmasi.
Ballast defensif: revenue 100% rupiah-domestik, nyaris zero import/freight beta, yield 6.8% — netralkan arah supaya portofolio nggak over-expose satu sisi.

answers.risk: Kaki long ini sebetulnya proxy commodity-export + IDR, BUKAN pure freight — jadi thesis batal kalau (1) eskalasi LCS justru menekan harga komoditas via demand China yang melemah (resesi/risk-off), sehingga long batubara-nikel turun bareng importir, korelasi naik ke +1 dan pair-trade jadi rugi dua arah; (2) BI memotong rate / IDR menguat tak terduga, mencabut tailwind FX dan justru menghidupkan kembali properti & importir yang kamu under-weight; atau (3) gangguan freight cuma sebentar (de-escalation) sehingga premi normalisasi sebelum carry dividen menutup. Mitigasi: ukur kaki long ke export-beta murni, jangan over-size emiten dengan cerita idiosinkratik seperti BYAN (-46% 1y), dan jaga ballast TLKM/PGAS/POWR cukup besar agar net beta thesis terkendali. Reminder eksekusi: SMDR/TPMA/HUMI tidak terverifikasi di dataset ini — validasi terpisah sebelum dipakai.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia is a structural net importer of wheat (feed + flour) and a price-taker on globally-traded urea/phosphate, so a prolonged Russia-Ukraine war keeps the imported-input bill elevated and that cost is paid in USD — meaning the real squeeze is FX, not just commodity price: a weak IDR (BI holding rate high to defend the rupiah against Fed/foreign-flow pressure) inflates COGS faster than rupiah selling prices can follow. The transmission runs hardest through poultry (CPIN, JPFA): broiler/feed is ~70% corn+soymeal+wheat-bran, demand is price-elastic protein, and DOC/broiler ASPs are administratively sensitive, so margin is the shock absorber — these are the most cost-kepukul. Branded consumer staples (ICBP/INDF, MYOR) have pricing power and SKU/gram-down levers to pass-through over 1-2 quarters, but the lag plus already-soft volumes (ICBP -29% 1y in the brief) compress near-term margin even though they recover it. The genuine natural hedge sits upstream in CPO (TAPG, SSMS, NSSS): palm is a substitute cooking oil and exportable in USD, so a war-driven edible-oil/biofuel bid actually lifts their revenue while everyone else eats cost — and INDF/SIMP's plantation arm partially offsets its own food-division input pain. Net: poultry absorbs, branded packaged passes through with a lag, CPO benefits.
tool_queries: SELECT s.ticker, s.sector, s.mcap_tn, s.roe_pct, f.gross_margin_pct, f.net_margin_pct FROM summary s JOIN fundamentals f ON f.ticker=s.ticker WHERE s.ticker IN ('CPIN','JPFA','ICBP','INDF','SIMP','MYOR','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS') ORDER BY f.gross_margin_pct ASC
SELECT ticker, ret_1y_pct, ret_3m_pct FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('CPIN','JPFA','ICBP','INDF','MYOR','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','SIMP') ORDER BY ret_3m_pct ASC
screen_stocks(sector IN ['Barang Konsumen Primer'], mcap_tn>5, sort=ret_1y_pct asc, limit=20)  -- isolate the names whose price already discounts the cost shock vs CPO names that ran

SELECT bn.ticker, SUM(bn.net_value_rp) AS net_foreign_60d FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','CPIN','JPFA','TAPG') AND bn.date >= date('now','-60 day') GROUP BY bn.ticker ORDER BY net_foreign_60d ASC  -- confirm whether foreign flows are already exiting the cost-squeezed names

picks.rationale: Largest poultry/feed integrator; corn+wheat-bran+soymeal-heavy COGS in USD means margin is the shock absorber — most cost-kepukul, weak pass-through on elastic broiler demand.
Same feed-cost transmission as CPIN with thinner balance sheet and more commodity-DOC exposure; highest margin sensitivity to a sustained wheat/feed bid.
Imported-wheat noodles but strong brand pricing power; already -29% 1y so the cost shock is partly priced — can pass-through over 1-2 quarters, near-term margin lag.
Parent blends ICBP's wheat-cost pain with Bogasari flour and a plantation (SIMP) arm — partial internal hedge; best risk-adjusted way to play the chain.
CPO play (34% ROE, 8.9% yield) — palm is the substitute cooking oil; a war-driven edible-oil/fertilizer-scarcity bid lifts its USD revenue, the natural beneficiary, not victim.
Pure-play palm (41% ROE) leveraged to the same CPO upcycle; hedges the basket against the food/poultry cost squeeze.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the war de-escalates or Black Sea grain/fertilizer corridors normalize and global wheat/urea prices roll over, collapsing the cost shock — then poultry (CPIN/JPFA) margins snap back fastest while the CPO hedge (TAPG/SSMS) loses its scarcity premium. Equally invalidating: a sharp IDR rally (BI cuts behind dovish Fed, foreign inflows return) removes the FX leg of the squeeze, or a soft Indonesian consumer / govt price caps (HET) block the staples pass-through, hurting ICBP/INDF even as input costs ease.

answers.category: FX-regime / de-dollarization scenario positioning (USD-revenue exporters vs USD-cost importers)
answers.reasoning: IDR ke 17.000 (dari ~16.200, depresiasi ~5%) memaksa fungsi reaksi BI: tahan suku bunga tinggi/intervensi FX bakar cadangan, yang menekan sektor rate-sensitive (properti & tech leverage tinggi) dan memicu outflow asing dari large-cap likuid yang kepemilikan asingnya tebal (BBCA, BBRI). Mekanisme transmisi intinya: eksportir batu bara/nikel/emas/CPO punya revenue USD tapi cost rupiah (upah, royalti, logistik domestik) sehingga IDR lemah = ekspansi margin translasi murni, sementara komoditas-nya sendiri dipatok USD; ini natural FX hedge. BRICS settlement non-USD justru meredakan friksi lane dagang ke China (pembeli terbesar batu bara/nikel/CPO Indonesia), dan emas adalah hedge de-dollarisasi paling bersih karena bid diversifikasi cadangan bank sentral. Sisi kalah: importir & USD-debt-heavy (properti developer, capex telco/infra, GOTO/tech) plus consumer staples dengan input impor (ICBP/INDF gandum-susu, UNVR) yang margin-nya tergerus saat COGS USD naik tapi harga jual rupiah lengket. Bank domestik-funded relatif terlindung dan kasih ballast yield tinggi (BBRI 10.4%, BBNI 9.4%), tapi pilih yang foreign-ownership-nya lebih rendah untuk batasi blast radius outflow.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND mcap_tn>5 AND div_yield>3 ORDER BY div_yield DESC -- tangkap eksportir batu bara/nikel/emas USD-revenue dengan ballast dividen (ADRO, PTBA, BSSR, GEMS, ANTM)

run_query: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, f.roe, f.div_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND f.roe>15 ORDER BY f.div_yield DESC -- konfirmasi kualitas margin eksportir (GEMS ROE 38, BYAN ROE 31 tapi turun -46% jadi entry, vs PTBA yield 10.7)

run_query: SELECT o.ticker, l.sector, o.foreign_pct, f.div_yield FROM ownership o JOIN latest l USING(ticker) JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector='Keuangan' AND l.mcap_tn>50 ORDER BY o.foreign_pct ASC -- pilih bank yield tinggi tapi foreign-ownership rendah buat batasi risiko outflow saat IDR 17rb

run_query: SELECT r.ticker, l.sector, r.ret_1y FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE r.ticker IN ('GOTO','BUKA','ICBP','AMRT','BSDE','CTRA','PANI','LPKR','TLKM') -- identifikasi USD-cost/USD-debt/rate-sensitive losers buat di-underweight (GOTO -36, ICBP -29, AMRT -29)

picks.rationale: Emas + nikel BUMN, hedge de-dollarisasi paling bersih (bid emas dari diversifikasi cadangan bank sentral BRICS) plus revenue USD/cost IDR; salah satu paling likuid (496 rp bn) jadi gampang masuk-keluar.
Batu bara USD-revenue, yield 10.7% kasih ballast saat BI tahan rate tinggi; pembeli China lebih lancar di lane settlement non-USD.
Eksportir batu bara ROE 38 + yield 14.7%, margin translasi melebar saat IDR ke 17rb karena cost-nya rupiah.
Batu bara ROE 31 yield 3.7% tapi udah turun -46% setahun, valuasi murah jadi entry asimetris ke skenario IDR lemah/komoditas USD.
CPO play, USD-revenue ekspor sawit + cost rupiah; yield 8.9% ROE 34, sektor Konsumen Primer yang justru pemenang FX bukan korban impor.
Bank domestik-funded sebagai ballast IDR book; yield 9.4% dan foreign-ownership lebih moderat dibanding BBCA/BBRI jadi blast radius outflow asing lebih kecil.

answers.risk: Tesis batal kalau de-dollarisasi/IDR 17rb gagal terwujud dan IDR justru rebound (BI hawkish berhasil atau Fed dovish), itu balikin trade: exporter margin translasi mengempis sementara importir/properti/tech rate-sensitive yang udah jatuh (GOTO -36, properti P/B <0.5) malah rally. Risiko kedua: harga komoditas USD (batu bara/nikel) anjlok bareng demand China melemah, yang menetralkan tailwind FX, IDR lemah nggak nolong kalau ASP komoditas turun lebih kencang, jadi ini sebenarnya joint bet FX lemah DAN komoditas stabil/naik, bukan FX murni. Ketiga, batu bara/CPO punya risiko intervensi kebijakan domestik (DMO, pajak ekspor, larangan ekspor nikel ore) yang bisa sita upside FX itu ke fiskal negara.

answers.reasoning: China+1 relocation is a real-economy capex story before it's an earnings story, so the cleanest IDX expression is industrial-estate developers who book land sales (marketing sales / pre-sales) the moment a multinational signs — DMAS (Kota Deltamas, anchored by Greenland/Sojitz, with auto + data-center tenants), BEST (Kota Bekasi Industrial Estate), and SSIA (Suryacita / Subang Smartpolitan). The transmission chain is: tenant signs -> land plot booked as revenue -> recurring estate/utility/water income -> later property tax & dumptruck/logistics demand; DMAS's 43.9% trailing yield is literally land-sale cash being paid out, the single best tell that plots are actually moving. Banks (BMRI, BBRI, BBCA) are a second-derivative play — relocation lifts working-capital and project loan demand, and BMRI/BBNI are the corporate-FDI lenders — but bank loan growth lags estate land sales by 4-8 quarters and is diluted by the rest of the loan book, so it's a weaker pure-play. Macro tailwind: a softening BI rate path lowers the property sector's high debt cost and the IDR weakness post-trade-war makes Indonesian labor/land cheaper in USD, reinforcing the relocation math; the risk is that the nickel/EV downstream (smelters, ANTM/AMMN orbit) is Chinese-FDI heavy, which is the relocation INTO Indonesia but can reverse if commodity prices or Chinese capital controls turn. Net: own the estates (DMAS/BEST/SSIA) for the direct beta, add one mega-bank (BMRI) for the loan-growth derivative, and verify with land-sales-driven dividends + announcements, not headlines.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, name, sector, mcap_tn, dividend_yield, pb, roe FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('DMAS','BEST','SSIA','KIJA','PANI','BSDE','CTRA','SMRA') ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC; -- confirm which estate names are actually paying out land-sale cash (DMAS 43.9% yield is the tell)

SELECT a.ticker, a.date, a.title FROM announcements a WHERE a.ticker IN ('DMAS','BEST','SSIA','KIJA') AND a.date >= '2024-01-01' AND (lower(a.title) LIKE '%marketing sales%' OR lower(a.title) LIKE '%penjualan lahan%' OR lower(a.title) LIKE '%land%' OR lower(a.title) LIKE '%tenant%' OR lower(a.title) LIKE '%industrial%' OR lower(a.title) LIKE '%relokasi%') ORDER BY a.date DESC; -- did real land/tenant transactions get disclosed, not just narrative

SELECT f.ticker, f.period, f.revenue, f.revenue_growth_yoy, f.net_income, f.roe FROM fundamentals f WHERE f.ticker IN ('DMAS','BEST','SSIA','BMRI','BBRI') ORDER BY f.ticker, f.period DESC LIMIT 40; -- check whether estate revenue (land sales) is actually re-accelerating and whether bank loan/NII growth follows

SELECT bn.ticker, SUM(bn.foreign_net) AS net_foreign_flow FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.date >= date('now','-90 day') AND bn.ticker IN ('DMAS','BEST','SSIA','BMRI','BBRI','BBCA') GROUP BY bn.ticker ORDER BY net_foreign_flow DESC; -- is smart/foreign money already accumulating the thematic names (flow confirmation vs narrative)

picks.rationale: Purest China+1 play: Kota Deltamas estate, land-sale revenue paid straight out as a 43.9% trailing yield — the strongest data signal that plots are actually being sold to relocating tenants.
Subang Smartpolitan industrial estate plus the contractor (NRCA) arm — direct exposure to both land sales and the construction capex when factories get built.
Bekasi industrial estate developer levered directly to manufacturing-tenant land demand; high-beta way to play accelerating relocation absorption.
Top corporate/FDI lender (419tn mcap, 19% ROE) — second-derivative beneficiary as relocation drives project finance and working-capital loan growth.
Largest, most liquid bank with 10.4% yield; captures the downstream SME/logistics credit demand around new industrial zones while paying you to wait.
Heavy-equipment and construction-machinery proxy (6.7% yield, part of ASII) — picks up earthworks/logistics demand as estates build out factory sites.

answers.risk: Thesis is invalidated if the relocation stays narrative: watch the data, not headlines. Specifically — (1) estate land/marketing sales fail to re-accelerate in fundamentals and announcements show no new tenant/land deals, meaning DMAS's high yield is a one-off liquidation not recurring absorption; (2) the FDI flowing in is mostly Chinese nickel/EV downstream capital (ANTM/AMMN orbit) rather than genuine China+1 diversification, so it reverses with commodity prices or Beijing capital controls; (3) a hawkish BI / weak-IDR shock raises funding cost for these high-debt property names and stalls capex. If foreign flow (broker_net) and land-sale disclosures both stay flat, treat the theme as priced-in narrative and avoid.

answers.category: single-sector deep-dive (thermal coal: ADRO vs ITMG vs PTBA)
answers.reasoning: Newcastle at ~$110/t off its >$400 peak is the binding variable: it compresses export-realized ASPs while Indonesia's DMO floor (PLN buys at a capped ~$70/t for power-grade) protects only the domestic slice, so the right pick depends on export beta, not headline yield. PTBA (state-owned PT Bukit Asam) is the most DMO-anchored and IDR-resilient — its costs and a large captive PLN offtake are domestic, so a soft seaborne price and a weak rupiah (BI holding ~5.75-6% to defend IDR against Fed-driven outflows) hurt it least, but it carries political/fiscal overhang as an SOE that the government can lean on for cheap coal and dividends. ITMG is a pure-export, zero-net-debt cash machine with the highest cash cost per ton, so it has the most operating leverage to a falling Newcastle and the thinnest moat in a structural China renewables demand-taper — high yield is a melting ice cube if prices keep sliding. ADRO is the trickiest: the listed parent has been de-thermalized after spinning the coal arm into AADI (9.9% yield in the brief) and pivoting toward Adaro Minerals/aluminium-smelting/energy transition, so "ADRO" today is a metals+power transition story, not a clean thermal-coal proxy — for a 2-3y horizon that diversification is a feature against the renewables thesis, not a bug. Foreign flows are leaving Energi on the price cycle (BYAN -46% trailing 1y in the brief), so entry should favor the names whose cash flows least depend on seaborne spot.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Energi', min_mcap=5e12, order_by='dividend_yield DESC') to rank ADRO/ITMG/PTBA/AADI/BSSR/GEMS/BYAN by yield and market cap from the same snapshot

run_query: SELECT ticker, market_cap, latest_dividend, latest_dividend_year FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','AADI') -- pull div-per-share + mcap to recompute yield and detect the ADRO->AADI thermal spin-off

run_query: SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy) fb, SUM(foreign_sell) fs, SUM(foreign_buy-foreign_sell) net_foreign FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','PTBA') AND date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 90 DAY GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign -- proxy foreign positioning on the coal-price slide
run_query: SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','BYAN','AADI') ORDER BY ret_1y -- confirm which names already de-rated with Newcastle vs which still carry downside

picks.rationale: Best 2-3y hold of the three: state-owned, DMO/PLN-anchored domestic offtake insulates cash flows from a falling Newcastle, with a still-fat 10.7% yield in the brief.

Hold but understand it: post-AADI spin-off the listed entity is now a metals/aluminium/energy-transition play, which is the cleanest hedge against the China-renewables structural thesis; 12.5% headline yield flatters a name that is no longer pure thermal coal.
The actual pure-thermal ADRO successor (9.9% yield) — own this instead of ADRO only if you specifically want maximum coal-dividend beta and can stomach the export-price downside.

Highest cash-cost, pure-export, zero-net-debt name — most operating leverage to Newcastle, so the highest near-term dividend but the weakest structural case for a 2-3y hold as prices and China demand taper.
Reference/avoid: -46% trailing 1y (worst big-cap in the brief) shows how hard the export-levered, low-float coal names de-rate on the price cycle — a warning, not a buy for this horizon.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if Newcastle re-rates upward (China stimulus, El Nino power demand, or supply discipline from Indonesia/Australia) — then the highest-beta export names (ITMG, AADI/BYAN) outrun DMO-anchored PTBA and the defensive ranking inverts. The other invalidator is policy/data: PTBA's SOE status cuts both ways — Jakarta can mandate below-market DMO pricing or raid dividends for the budget, capping the very downside protection the pick relies on. Verify the ADRO->AADI corporate action and each name's true export/DMO split and cash cost per ton from filings before sizing; the brief's yields are trailing and yf-ratio reliability is flagged as directional, so treat the headline yields as signals, not underwriting numbers.

answers.reasoning: Hilirisasi is a fiscal-and-trade strategy, not a price tailwind: by forcing ore downstream and adding RKEF+HPAL capacity, Indonesia now supplies ~50%+ of global mined nickel, so the LME price the whole complex is levered to is being capped by Indonesia's own volume — higher royalty (3-19% sliding scale on HPV) plus a new-smelter moratorium are margin headwinds at the unit level even as they tighten future supply. That split means you must separate price exposure from volume/cost-curve exposure: ANTM is a low-cost SOE with a gold leg and 65% MIND ID control (verified: zero foreign in the >1% holder list, the only genuinely clean P/B ~4.3 and best ROE in the group), so it is the defensive ballast, not a swap-out. INCO is the most "globally priced" leg (48% foreign, Vale/Sumitomo matte) and therefore the most LME-beta name to trim if you fear oversupply. The actual pure HPAL/EV-battery-materials plays are MBMA (50% Merdeka + Huayou/CATL chain, full RKEF+HPAL+matte) and NCKL (Harita + Glencore, Halmahera HPAL) — but my queries show they already ran (MBMA +135%, NCKL +64% trailing-12m) and rolled over hard in the last month (-14% / -27%), and NCKL carries a liquidity/free-float penalty (ADV ~Rp98bn, only ~12% float in the >1% holder set vs ANTM's ~Rp550bn). Macro backdrop matters: with BI holding and IDR soft, USD nickel revenue is an FX hedge, but coal/CPO cash-cow flows and bank dominance mean foreign money treats these as high-beta satellites, so they sell first in a risk-off — which is exactly the 1-month drawdown the data already shows.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Barang Baku', sub_sector contains 'nickel/metals', sort_by=price_to_book asc) to isolate the nickel/battery-materials cohort and rank cheapness — flagging yf ratios as unreliable per data contract

run_query: WITH p AS (SELECT ticker,date,close,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date DESC) rn FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA','TINS','BRMS','AMMN')) SELECT ticker, max(CASE WHEN rn=1 THEN close END)/max(CASE WHEN rn=22 THEN close END)-1 AS r_1m, .../max(CASE WHEN rn=252 THEN close END)-1 AS r_12m FROM p GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY r_12m DESC -- confirm the complex is up huge on 12m but rolling over on 1m

run_query over ownership: SELECT ticker, sum(CASE WHEN local_foreign='FOREIGN' THEN percentage END) AS foreign_pct, max(percentage) AS top1, count(*) AS holders_ge1pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA') GROUP BY ticker -- separate SOE/strategic-controlled (ANTM 65% MIND ID, MBMA 50% Merdeka) from foreign-heavy LME-beta (INCO 48%)

run_query over eod_summary: SELECT ticker, avg(value)/1e9 AS adv_bn, sum(foreign_buy-foreign_sell)/1e9 AS fnet_bn FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN (...) AND date > current_date-90 GROUP BY ticker -- liquidity gate (NCKL ~98bn vs ANTM ~550bn) before sizing any rotation

picks.rationale: Keep as core — low-cost SOE (65% MIND ID, no foreign in the >1% book) with a gold leg that offsets nickel-price risk; best/most-believable ROE and P/B in the group, defensive against LME oversupply.

The cleanest cheap HPAL/EV pure play to add (Harita + Glencore, Halmahera/Obi): only credible low P/B ~1.97 in the cohort and strong_buy, but size small given thin float/liquidity (ADV ~Rp98bn) and -27% 1m drawdown.

The purest battery-materials chain (50% Merdeka + Huayou/CATL link, RKEF+HPAL+matte) — best 12m return ex-tin (+135%); add only as a high-beta satellite, not a wholesale swap, after the -14% 1m reset.

Better risk-adjusted way to own the Merdeka nickel story than MBMA alone — copper+gold+nickel diversification (owns 50% of MBMA), deepest 3m momentum (+48%) and strong_buy, less single-commodity LME exposure.
Trim, don't add — the most globally-priced/foreign-owned leg (48% foreign, Vale/Sumitomo matte), highest LME beta and the name to lighten if the oversupply/price-cap thesis plays out.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if LME nickel re-rates higher — driven by an Indonesian supply discipline shock (the smelter moratorium actually biting, RKAB ore-quota cuts, or Indonesia formalizing an OPEC-style nickel cartel) or a US/EU IRA-style demand pull that absorbs the surplus. In that regime the pure HPAL/EV names (MBMA, NCKL) have the most upside operating leverage and trimming INCO/holding ANTM would be the wrong tilt. Secondary risks: yf valuation ratios in the data are demonstrably broken (IDR/USD mix — P/B of 48,000x), so the rotation must be confirmed against audited filings, not the screen; and NCKL's thin free float/liquidity makes it hard to exit in a risk-off, while a stronger-than-expected royalty hike compresses unit margins across all of them regardless of LME.

answers.reasoning: Investor diagnosis is correct: MDKA is a polymetallic/holdco story (copper, gold, plus the AMMN stake) so its beta to spot gold is diluted by copper and Amman's porphyry economics — AMMN itself (ROE 5, mcap 375tn) trades on a multi-year copper-gold capex narrative, not on $/oz. For IDX-listed pure-ish gold beta, the cleanest equity proxies are ANTM (Aneka Tambang, state gold refiner/miner with a domestic LM bullion franchise) and the gold-focused juniors BRMS/CITA, but note all of them carry equity-specific noise: ANTM revenue is split with nickel, and BRMS/CITA add operational and balance-sheet risk that amplifies, not cleans, the gold signal. Macro tailwind is real for IDX gold names because BI has limited room to cut while the Fed eases — a softer USD/firmer DXY-reversal supports IDR-translated gold revenue, and ANTM's Logam Mulia retail bullion arm gets a direct volume kick from domestic safe-haven demand as Indonesian retail rotates out of low-yield deposits. The genuinely cleanest pure-gold-price exposure, though, is physical/digital gold (LM bullion, or Pegadaian/fintech digital-gold accounts priced off LBMA in IDR): zero equity beta, zero operational/dilution/dividend-policy risk, but you give up leverage to the price move, dividend yield, and pay a buy/sell spread plus storage. The honest answer: if you want clean exposure, buy IDR gold directly; if you want leveraged equity beta and accept mining risk, ANTM is the most liquid listed proxy, with BRMS/CITA as higher-octane satellites.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, name, sector FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','BRMS','CITA','NICL','MDKA','EMAS','AMMN') -- map the brief's flagged metals/gold names to actual listings and confirm sector=Barang Baku

SELECT s.ticker, f.revenue_segment, f.gold_pct_revenue, f.copper_pct_revenue, f.nickel_pct_revenue FROM fundamentals f JOIN summary s USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('ANTM','MDKA','AMMN','BRMS','CITA') -- quantify how much of each issuer's revenue is actually gold vs copper/nickel to rank purity

SELECT ticker, avg_value_rp_bn FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','BRMS','MDKA','AMMN','CITA','NICL') ORDER BY avg_value_rp_bn DESC -- liquidity screen; ANTM 496 and BRMS 441 are the only metals names with deep daily turnover for a clean entry/exit

screen_stocks(sector='Barang Baku', subindustry LIKE '%gold%' OR keyword IN ['emas','gold','logam mulia'], min_mcap_tn=1, sort='trailing_1y_return desc') -- surface any gold-specific listings (incl. EMAS) plus check 1y momentum vs the spot gold move

picks.rationale: Cleanest liquid IDX gold proxy: state miner with the Logam Mulia bullion franchise; deep turnover (~496rp bn/day) and direct retail safe-haven volume kick, though nickel dilutes the beta.
Higher-octane gold satellite — pure gold/mineral junior, very liquid (~441rp bn/day), so it amplifies the spot move but adds operational/balance-sheet risk.
Gold-leaning Barang Baku name with strong ROE (~40); a leveraged satellite for the up-cycle, smaller and less liquid than ANTM.
Explicitly NOT the clean pick — copper + gold + the AMMN stake dilute gold beta; include only if you want the polymetallic/copper-plus-gold blend, not pure $/oz.
Avoid for pure gold: 375tn copper-gold porphyry trading on multi-year capex/grade narrative (ROE 5), not on spot gold; explains why MDKA's look-through is muddied.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if gold rolls over — a faster-than-expected Fed cut that re-risks markets (or a geopolitical de-escalation) sends capital back into risk assets and pressures gold, and equity proxies fall harder than spot because of operational leverage. ANTM/BRMS/CITA also carry idiosyncratic equity risk (nickel price drag on ANTM, dilution/execution risk on the juniors, IDR translation) that can decouple them from the gold move in either direction. Net: if you truly want pure, clean gold-price exposure with no equity noise, physical/digital IDR gold is the right instrument and no listed IDX equity fully replaces it — the trade-off is you sacrifice operational leverage, dividends, and pay spread/storage.

answers.category: Commodity FX-translation beneficiaries vs USD-debt-burdened losers (IDR @ 17,000/USD)
answers.reasoning: Transmission: a capital-outflow-driven IDR move to 17,000 is a terms-of-trade gift for IDX commodity exporters because coal (Newcastle/ICI), nickel, and CPO all clear in USD while a large share of cash costs (domestic labor, royalties, IDR opex) stay in rupiah — every weak-IDR rupiah of USD revenue drops to a fatter IDR margin. But the same outflow forces BI to defend the currency (rate hikes / FX intervention), which raises the IDR cost of servicing USD debt and triggers a non-cash FX revaluation loss on the USD-denominated debt stack, so the net winner is decided by balance-sheet currency-matching, not the commodity print. The cleanest beneficiaries are net-cash, high-payout coal names (BYAN, PTBA, ITMG, BSSR, GEMS) that report/earn in USD and carry little hard-currency debt — the FX gain flows to dividends (the brief shows 10-15% yields on BSSR/GEMS/PTBA/ADRO). The losers are the leveraged/empire-building energy and nickel names (CUAN/PTRO group, AMMN, BRMS) whose USD project-debt and capex stacks mean the FX revaluation and higher IDR debt service offset or exceed the translation tailwind. CPO is a hedged middle case: TAPG/SSMS get the USD-price translation but face the export-levy/DMO drag and are less dollarized than coal.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') with sub_sector 'Minyak, Gas & Batu Bara' or 'Barang Baku', sort by dividend_yield desc — isolate the coal/metal exporters and rank cash-return capacity

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, s.market_cap, s.total_debt, s.total_cash, s.debt_to_equity, s.return_on_equity, s.dividend_yield FROM summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE c.sub_sector IN ('Minyak, Gas & Batu Bara','Barang Baku') AND c.sub_sector='Makanan & Minuman' ORDER BY s.debt_to_equity DESC — split net-cash beneficiaries from high-leverage names

run_query: SELECT ticker, financial_report_currency FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','PTBA','ITMG','BSSR','GEMS','ADRO','AADI','CUAN','PTRO','AMMN','BRMS','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS') — confirm which names actually report in USD (full natural FX hedge) vs IDR-only

run_query: SELECT ticker, (total_debt-total_cash) AS net_debt, (total_debt-total_cash)/NULLIF(market_cap,0) AS net_debt_to_mcap FROM summary WHERE ticker IN (commodity set) ORDER BY net_debt_to_mcap DESC — rank USD-debt blast radius for the FX-revaluation hit

picks.rationale: Net beneficiary: USD-priced coal exporter, 31% ROE and net-cash balance sheet — pure FX translation upside with minimal USD-debt drag; -46% trailing means cheap entry.
Net beneficiary: domestic-low-cost coal, USD revenue, net-cash and 10.7% yield — weak IDR fattens the IDR margin and feeds the dividend, no hard-currency debt to revalue.
Net beneficiary: small, debt-light USD coal exporter paying 15.4% yield — the FX windfall passes almost fully through to cash returns.
Net beneficiary: 38% ROE, 14.7% yield coal name with light leverage — high asset turn means the translation gain compounds into payout.

Debt-burdened loser: copper/gold smelter mid-build with a heavy USD project-debt and capex stack; 5% ROE — FX revaluation of debt and higher IDR debt service swamp any USD-revenue translation until the smelter ramps.

Debt-burdened / fragile: leveraged Prajogo energy vehicle (with PTRO) trading on expansion, not net cash — USD/funding-cost sensitivity makes it a casualty of the outflow-and-rate-defense leg, not a clean translation winner.
Hedged middle case: CPO exporter gets USD-price translation and 8.9% yield, but Indonesia's export levy/DMO caps the FX pass-through — softer beneficiary than net-cash coal.

answers.risk: The thesis inverts if the IDR move comes WITH a commodity-price collapse: a global risk-off that drives outflows often also tanks coal/nickel/CPO in USD terms, so the translation gain is dwarfed by lower realized USD prices (Newcastle/nickel already soft). It also breaks if BI's defense is aggressive enough to crush domestic demand and lift IDR funding costs across the board, or if the supposed net-cash names actually carry undisclosed USD shareholder/parent loans — must verify total_debt/total_cash and financial_report_currency per name on the live MCP, since the dev mirror's summary table is trimmed and lacks the debt fields. AMMN/CUAN turn into winners, not losers, if smelter/project ramp converts the capex into USD cash flow faster than the FX revaluation bites.

answers.category: IDX CPO / palm oil sector positioning
answers.reasoning: Transmission: CPO ~RM4,000/t with EUDR overhang plus the B40 mandate is a relative-value setup, not a sector call — EUDR hits export-skewed, traceability-light upstream pure-plays hardest (LSIP/SIMP earn a price, not a spread), while the B40 biodiesel mandate is a domestic, price-insensitive offtake that converts a glut into a guaranteed downstream margin, favoring integrated/biodiesel names. With BI holding rate elevated and IDR soft, exporters get a translation tailwind on USD-CPO receipts, but that cuts both ways: a weak rupiah also raises the local cost of the diesel that POME/biodiesel displaces, which is exactly why Jakarta keeps escalating the mandate (B35 to B40) — it's a fiscal/FX-defensive policy, so domestic biodiesel demand is structurally underwritten regardless of where spot CPO trades. The cycle logic mirrors coal: when the commodity price rolls over, the resilient names are the ones with a regulated/contracted domestic floor and refining spread (TBLA, DSNG, TAPG) rather than the cheapest book-value upstream beta (SIMP at 0.49 P/B, LSIP at 0.72). Foreign flows are not the swing factor here — most of these (TAPG, AALI, DSNG, LSIP) are domestically held, so they are insulated from the EM-outflow/EUDR-headline selling that would hit foreign-heavy names. Net: barbell toward integrated + biodiesel for the "price down, demand up" scenario, keep LSIP as the quality-upstream optionality if CPO holds.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector='Barang Konsumen Primer' OR ticker IN ('AALI','LSIP','SIMP','SMAR','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','DSNG','TBLA','SGRO','ANJT','BWPT') — return mcap, price_to_book, trailing_pe, dividend_yield, return_on_equity, profit_margins, recommendation_key

SELECT ticker, name, return_on_equity, profit_margins, price_to_book, dividend_yield, recommendation_key FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('LSIP','SIMP','AALI','SMAR','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','DSNG','TBLA','SGRO') ORDER BY profit_margins DESC -- isolate downstream/biodiesel margin vs upstream pure-play

SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ret_3m FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('LSIP','SIMP','AALI','SMAR','TAPG','DSNG','TBLA','SSMS','NSSS') ORDER BY ret_3m DESC -- recent momentum to confirm which names are already pricing the CPO/EUDR scenario

SELECT ticker, local_foreign, SUM(percentage) pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('TAPG','AALI','DSNG','LSIP','SIMP','TBLA','SSMS') GROUP BY 1,2 -- foreign-ownership exposure to gauge EUDR-headline outflow risk

picks.rationale: Cleanest pure-biodiesel/domestic-demand hedge: large B40-feeding biodiesel plant gives a contracted local offtake; 0.46 P/B, ~5x PE and a 12.4% yield make it the most direct 'CPO down, B40 up' winner.

Integrated with explicit biodiesel/renewable (POME, bioenergy) exposure; best-in-class 14.9% net margin and 17% ROE, +9% over 3m even as the sector cooled — refining spread cushions a spot-CPO correction.

Triputra's downstream-integrated play: top-tier 32.5% margin and 33.5% ROE with an 8.9% yield and mostly local ownership — earnings lean on volume/spread, not just CPO price, so it holds up if demand rises.

Integrated upstream+refining at a deep 0.64 P/B, but thin 5.1% margin and a 'hold' rec — own it as the cheap integrated optionality, not the resilience pick; refining doesn't rescue weak plantation economics.

Quality upstream pure-play (34% margin, 14% ROE, strong_buy, net cash) at 0.72 P/B — keep as the high-beta upside name IF CPO holds RM4,000+, but it's the most exposed to a price correction and EUDR export drag.

Cheapest pure-upstream beta on the board at 0.49 P/B (Salim/Indofood-linked, partly integrated via Bimoli refining) — deep-value but lowest-quality of the set; a CPO leg-down hits it hardest, so size small.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the B40 mandate slips or biodiesel subsidy/CPO-fund (BPDPKS) funding is cut — that strips the domestic-demand floor and re-couples even TBLA/DSNG/TAPG to spot CPO. The mirror risk is a CPO rally back toward RM4,500+ (La Nina supply shock or Indonesian export-levy hike): that would invert the call and make the cheap upstream beta (SIMP/LSIP) the outperformers while compressing refiner/biodiesel spreads. Secondary: a weaker IDR raises feedstock-vs-fossil-diesel economics and squeezes biodiesel blend margins, and integrated names' refining segments lag plantations when CPO is cheap. Data caveat — fundamentals table carries no leverage/debt field, so balance-sheet stress (notably TBLA's historically higher gearing) isn't verifiable here and must be checked on filings before sizing.

answers.reasoning: Transmission: a 2026 global recession crushes nickel/copper via China construction + EV demand, hitting IDX base-metal names (AMMN, NICL, BRMS) and coal volumes, while gold rallies on Fed cuts + safe-haven flows. The killer second-order effect is IDR — a risk-off recession typically weakens the rupiah vs USD, which is a tailwind for IDX commodity exporters whose costs are in IDR but revenue in USD, partially cushioning the demand shock and inflating gold's IDR price. The natural-hedge story for MDKA/AMMN is structurally weak: AMMN is overwhelmingly copper-and-by-product gold (ROE just 5 in the brief, no dividend, capex-heavy Phase 7 smelter), so it's net long the cyclical leg you're trying to hedge, not a balanced gold offset; MDKA carries real leverage and thin margins, so in a downturn the copper P&L and refinancing risk dominate the gold optionality. A barbell is cleaner: pair a pure-ish gold beneficiary (ANTM — Antam gold, by far the most liquid metals name at 496 rp bn/day, so you can size and exit) against a defensive cash-return anchor in coal (PTBA, 10.7% yield, state-backed, low-cost domestic DMO buffer) — but note coal is itself cyclical, so the true defensive ballast should lean on dividend/telco/staples (TLKM 6.8%, UNVR, SIDO) rather than expecting PTBA's payout to survive a deep price collapse. Foreign-flow context: in risk-off, foreigners dump the liquid large caps first (banks, BBCA/BBRI), so high-foreign-ownership cyclicals de-rate hardest, which argues for the gold + domestic-yield barbell over a single "two-metal" name.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe_pct, yield_pct, pb FROM fundamentals f JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('AMMN','MDKA','EMAS','ANTM','NICL','BRMS','PTBA','BYAN','ITMG','ADRO','GEMS','BSSR') ORDER BY yield_pct DESC;
SELECT ticker, AVG(value_rp_bn) AS adv FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','MDKA','BRMS','PTBA','BUMI','ADRO') AND date >= date('now','-60 day') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY adv DESC;
SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ret_3m FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','MDKA','PTBA','TLKM','UNVR','SIDO','BYAN') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC;
SELECT * FROM screen_stocks WHERE sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku','Infrastruktur') AND yield_pct >= 6 AND mcap_tn >= 10 ORDER BY yield_pct DESC;

picks.rationale: Cleanest liquid gold expression on IDX (Antam gold) and most-traded metals name at 496 rp bn/day, so you can build and exit the safe-haven leg without slippage; this is the gold side of the barbell.
Defensive cash-return anchor: 10.7% yield, state-backed (MIND ID), low-cost domestic DMO volumes buffer a price collapse better than seaborne coal — the income ballast leg.
Non-commodity defensive with 6.8% yield and IDR-domestic revenue; survives a recession far better than coal payouts and dampens the portfolio's cyclical beta.
Hold/underweight, NOT a natural hedge — brief shows ROE 5 and no dividend; it's net-long copper with capex risk, so it amplifies the cyclical leg you're hedging against, not offsetting it.
Avoid as the 'gold+copper hedge' — leverage and thin margins mean copper downside + refinancing risk dominate the gold optionality in a downturn; the dual-metal story breaks exactly when you need it.
Optional defensive sweetener: ROE 37, consumer-health staple with resilient IDR demand and a clean balance sheet, adds non-cyclical ballast to the defensive end of the barbell.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if there's no real recession — a soft-landing / China stimulus reflation sends nickel and copper higher, and AMMN/MDKA's cyclical leverage then crushes the defensive barbell. Second invalidator: if gold rallies but IDR weakens sharply and BI is forced to hike to defend the rupiah, high-yield coal/dividend names de-rate on the higher discount rate and PTBA's payout gets cut on volume/price pressure, so the 'safe' income leg fails too. ANTM is also not pure gold — its nickel exposure means it sells off with the cyclical leg in the early risk-off phase before gold dominates.

answers.reasoning: The copper structural-bull thesis is real but IDX has only two genuine copper proxies — AMMN (Batu Hijau/Elang porphyry) and MDKA (Tujuh Bukit + Wetar) — so the trade is name-selection, not sector beta. The Indonesia-specific tailwind is twofold: the ore-export ban and hilirisasi mandate force AMMN/MDKA to build domestic smelters, converting concentrate exporters into refined-cathode sellers that capture treatment/refining margin and rerate earnings — AMMN's trailing PE ~73x collapses to a forward PE ~11x precisely because Phase-7 plus smelter ramp drives earnings into 2026-27; and copper is USD-priced while costs are partly IDR, so a still-soft IDR and a BI rate that is finally easing lower the discount rate on these long-duration, capex-heavy reserve assets. Critically the wait-for-a-correction premise is mostly already satisfied — AMMN is down ~13% trailing-1y and sits ~4,700 near its 52w low of 4,500 (49% below the 9,225 high) while every other metals name ran (ANTM +134%, MDKA +128%, BRMS +121%, INCO +139%, TINS +274%); AMMN has de-rated, not run. Foreign flow corroborates MDKA's structural read: CATL/Brunp sits on the register beside the Saratoga/Thohir control block, tying it to the EV battery chain, not just spot copper.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker,name,market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn,trailing_pe,forward_pe,price_to_book,return_on_equity,target_mean_price,recommendation_key FROM read_parquet('data/summary/latest.parquet') WHERE ticker IN ('AMMN','MDKA','BRMS','ANTM','CITA','INCO','TINS') ORDER BY market_cap DESC

WITH px AS (SELECT ticker,date,close FROM read_parquet('data/prices/**/*.parquet',hive_partitioning=true) WHERE ticker IN ('AMMN','MDKA','BRMS','ANTM','CITA','INCO','TINS')), l AS (SELECT ticker,last(close ORDER BY date) c_now,max(date) d FROM px GROUP BY ticker), y AS (SELECT p.ticker,last(p.close ORDER BY p.date) c_yago FROM px p,l WHERE p.ticker=l.ticker AND p.date<=l.d-INTERVAL '1 year' GROUP BY p.ticker) SELECT l.ticker,(l.c_now/y.c_yago-1)*100 AS ret_1y,l.c_now FROM l JOIN y USING(ticker) ORDER BY ret_1y DESC
SELECT ticker,week_low_52,week_high_52,target_mean_price,beta FROM read_parquet('data/summary/latest.parquet') WHERE ticker IN ('AMMN','MDKA','ANTM','BRMS')
SELECT ticker,name,local_foreign,percentage FROM read_parquet('data/ownership/latest.parquet') WHERE ticker IN ('AMMN','MDKA') ORDER BY percentage DESC

picks.rationale: Best pure-copper exposure; already corrected ~49% from 9,225 to ~4,700 (near 52w low, -13% 1y vs peers +120-270%), and the premium is only on trailing PE ~73x; forward PE ~11x as the smelter/Phase-7 ramp lands, so entering near current levels is reasonable rather than waiting for a dip that has largely happened.

The diversified copper play (Tujuh Bukit + Wetar) with battery-chain optionality; CATL/Brunp and Saratoga/Thohir on the register; strong_buy, +128% 1y, target 3,624 vs ~3,260 so less margin of safety than AMMN but cleaner structural story.

Cheapest credible valuation in the cluster (trailing PE ~12x, P/B ~4.3x look real, not yf-corrupted), SOE-backed nickel/gold diversifier; a lower-beta way to hold the hilirisasi/metals theme alongside a copper bet.

Gold-copper (Citra Palu) and one of the most liquid metals names (avg rp441bn/day); momentum proxy +121% 1y, buy rated; a higher-beta satellite for the copper-adjacent tape, not a fundamentals anchor.
Genuinely cheap optical valuation (trailing PE ~6.9x, P/B ~1.98x) bauxite/alumina downstream play; not copper but the same hilirisasi mechanism; a value hedge against paying up in the copper names.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the copper structural bid fades; a China property/grid demand air-pocket or faster mine supply (DRC/Latin America) closing the gap compresses LME copper and de-rates both names regardless of forward PE. AMMN-specific: its forward multiple is entirely back-loaded on smelter commissioning and Phase-7 grades hitting schedule; any cost overrun, commissioning slip, or hilirisasi export/royalty policy shift turns the cheap forward PE illusory, and the thin controlled float (beta ~0.03) means the stock can gap on low volume. In a broad IDR/foreign-outflow risk-off, high-beta metals (BRMS, TINS) sell off first and hardest. Treat all yf P/B and ROE figures as corrupted (AMMN P/B printed as 63,513x); only trailing/forward PE and price-vs-range are load-bearing here.

answers.category: IDX commodity portfolio — diversified coal / nickel / gold / CPO, 3-5yr cycle, Rp500jt
answers.reasoning: Posisi siklus tiap komoditas beda tajam, jadi weight harus ikut siklus, bukan dibagi rata.
