Batu bara ada di fase late-cycle: harga normalisasi dari puncak 2022, tapi neraca emiten masih bersih dan payout besar — transmisinya lewat dividen kas tinggi (BSSR/PTBA/ADRO 10-15% yield di brief) yang jadi bantalan saat BI rate masih ~5.5-6% menahan IDR; ini income, bukan growth. Emas justru early/mid-cycle struktural: USD/real-yield turun + bank sentral EM (termasuk BI) akumulasi emas sebagai lindung fiskal dan defisit transaksi berjalan, sehingga produser emas IDX dapat tailwind harga jangka panjang. Nikel paling kontroversial — oversupply Indonesia sendiri (RKAB jor-joran + smelter HPAL China) menekan harga LME, tapi justru di sinilah hilirisasi struktural dan kebijakan fiskal pro-EV memberi opsi 3-5 tahun, jadi diambil kecil dan harus emiten berkualitas neraca, bukan yang bakar kas. CPO mid-cycle defensif: mandat B40/B50 menyerap suplai domestik dan menaut harga ke kebijakan fiskal energi, ROE perkebunan tinggi (TAPG 34%, SSMS 41% di brief) dengan yield layak — ini growth-plus-income yang relatif lepas dari arah suku bunga. Foreign flow dan dominasi bank besar bukan tema di sini; commodity book ini sengaja underweight rate-sensitive (properti/tech) dan memakai dividen energi sebagai mesin carry sambil emas+CPO membawa upside siklus.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku','Barang Konsumen Primer'), mcap_tn > 5, sort by dividend_yield desc — to confirm the coal/CPO income tier (expect BSSR, GEMS, ADRO, PTBA, AADI, TAPG) and metals names (ANTM, NICL) surface

run_query: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, f.roe, f.debt_to_equity, f.net_debt, f.current_ratio, f.dividend_yield, f.pb FROM latest l JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) WHERE l.ticker IN ('PTBA','ADRO','BSSR','ITMG','ANTM','AMMN','NICL','MDKA','TAPG','SSMS','AALI') ORDER BY f.net_debt ASC — rank balance-sheet quality (net cash first, low D/E, current_ratio>1) per sector

run_query: SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ann_vol FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('PTBA','ANTM','AMMN','TAPG','BSSR','ADRO') — cagr_3y is NULL on this 2.25y mirror, so use ret_1y + realized vol to gauge where each commodity sits vs its own trailing trend before sizing

run_query: SELECT ticker, AVG(value)/1e9 AS adv_rp_bn FROM eod_summary WHERE date >= current_date - INTERVAL 90 DAY AND ticker IN ('ANTM','PTBA','ADRO','AMMN','TAPG','BSSR','NICL') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY adv_rp_bn DESC — confirm liquidity for a Rp500jt book (ANTM ~496, PTBA/ADRO deep; NICL likely thin → cap weight)

picks.rationale: Coal income anchor: net-cash BUMN, ~10.7% yield in brief, late-cycle so held for carry not re-rating — the portfolio's cash engine.
Coal diversifier with ~12.5% yield and strongest free-cash conversion among thermal names; pairs with PTBA so coal sleeve isn't single-name BUMN risk.
Gold + nickel hybrid and most-liquid metals name (ADV ~496 rp bn); state-backed balance sheet gives the early/mid-cycle gold upside with daily exit liquidity.
Gold-copper structural growth (Batu Hijau/Elang); low 5% ROE today reflects ramp/capex, sized small as the multi-year copper-gold call option, not income.
CPO quality pick: 34% ROE and ~8.9% yield in brief, B40/B50 demand floor — the mid-cycle growth-plus-income leg least tied to BI rate.
Second CPO name, top-decile 41% ROE plantation; diversifies palm sleeve beyond TAPG against single-estate/weather risk.

answers.risk: Suggested sizing: coal 40% (PTBA 22 / ADRO 18) as the carry engine, CPO 30% (TAPG 18 / SSMS 12), gold/metals 30% (ANTM 20 / AMMN 10) — weight follows cycle, with late-cycle coal taken for cash flow not appreciation. Main risk: a global growth/China hard-landing hits all four commodities at once (correlation goes to 1 in a risk-off), and the coal dividend thesis specifically breaks if China import curbs or a faster RE buildout collapse thermal prices below cash cost — that would cut payouts, the very thing the 40% weight relies on. Secondary invalidators: nickel oversupply from Indonesia's own RKAB stays structural and drags ANTM/AMMN's metals leg; a sharp BI cut + IDR rally would rotate flows back into the rate-sensitive sectors this book deliberately avoids, causing relative underperformance. Sizing caveat: NICL-type small caps were excluded for liquidity — verify ADV before any name >15% of a Rp500jt book.

answers.reasoning: Trump's tariff + "drill baby drill" + anti-EV combo splits IDX's two metals/energy complexes in opposite directions. The anti-EV and US-IRA rollback leg is structurally bearish for Indonesian nickel: the whole 60bn+ HPAL/RKEF buildout (ANTM, NCKL, MDKA) was underwritten by the EV battery thesis, and an LME nickel price already crushed by Indonesian oversupply gets no demand rescue — so nickel names stay margin-squeezed with low/no yield (ANTM ROE thin, NCKL no payout). Coal is the cleaner trade but for a subtle reason: "drill baby drill" depresses oil/gas, not seaborne thermal coal, whose demand is China/India power, not the US — so ADRO/ITMG/PTBA keep printing cash and pay it out (ADRO 12.5%, PTBA 10.7%, BSSR 15.4%, GEMS 14.7%), which is exactly what a strong-USD/weak-IDR regime rewards since BI must hold rates high to defend the rupiah, making 10%+ rupiah dividend yields the dominant carry trade for both locals and the foreign flows that fund our current-account-sensitive market. The tariff leg is a wash-to-negative for both (global growth/China demand risk hits coal volumes and nickel price alike), but coal's valuation already discounts a down-cycle (ADRO/ITMG/PTBA trade like terminal-value plays) while nickel still carries growth-capex risk — so on a 1-2y risk-adjusted basis, dividend-coal outperforms growth-nickel. Caveat: this is a total-return/income call, not a momentum call — BYAN is down 46% and the Energi sector's +120% trailing return is high-coal-price base effect that can mean-revert.
tool_queries: SELECT s.ticker, s.sector, s.mcap_tn, s.roe, s.div_yield, r.ret_1y FROM summary s JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','BSSR','GEMS','AADI','BYAN','ANTM','NCKL','MDKA','NICL','BRMS') ORDER BY s.div_yield DESC;
SELECT ticker, AVG(net_value) AS avg_foreign_net FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','ANTM','NCKL') AND date >= date('now','-90 day') GROUP BY ticker;
screen_stocks(sector='Energi', subsector LIKE '%coal%', div_yield_min=8, mcap_tn_min=10, sort='div_yield desc')

SELECT ticker, MAX(close) FILTER(WHERE date>=date('now','-252 day')) AS hi, last_close, last_close/MAX(close) AS pct_off_high FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','ANTM','NCKL','BYAN') GROUP BY ticker;

picks.rationale: Core long: 12.5% rupiah yield, thermal-coal cash machine insulated from 'drill baby drill' (oil/gas, not seaborne coal); the high-carry name foreign flows want in a weak-IDR/high-BI-rate regime.
Net-cash, high-payout pure thermal coal; defensive total-return play that already discounts a down-cycle, low capex risk vs nickel growth names.
10.7% yield, domestic-PLN demand floor and state-backed offtake make volumes resilient to global tariff-driven growth slowdown.
Highest coal yield in the brief (15.4%); small-cap income proxy that screens top of the dividend-coal basket if you want maximum carry.
Underweight/avoid (the funded short side of the pair): nickel+gold name most exposed to anti-EV demand destruction and Indonesian oversupply, thin ROE and minimal yield offer no down-cycle cushion.
Underweight: pure HPAL/nickel growth story underwritten by the EV battery thesis Trump policy directly undercuts; no dividend support if LME nickel stays depressed.

answers.risk: Thesis inverts if China stimulates hard and floods battery/EV demand (rescuing LME nickel) while simultaneously cutting coal imports on its decarbonization/safety push — that flips the pair toward nickel. Equally, a Fed pivot / sharp USD weakening would let BI cut rates, compress the rupiah-yield carry advantage that makes coal dividends attractive, and re-rate the rate-sensitive growth/nickel complex. Watch coal's high trailing-1y base (Energi +120%, BYAN -46%): if seaborne thermal prices roll over on warm-winter/global-growth weakness, coal earnings and the dividends underpinning this call get cut, since payout ratios are high on already-elevated commodity prices.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia's DMO forces miners to sell a volume quota to PLN at a capped USD 70/ton (subbituminous reference), so PTBA — with the largest domestic/PLN skew — runs a structurally low blended ASP that barely moves when global Newcastle prices spike; its earnings beta to the coal cycle is muted while its ~10.7% yield is partly an SOE-payout artifact, not cycle leverage. ITMG and ADRO route far more tonnage to export (China/India seaborne), so in a high-price regime their realized ASP tracks the global curve and free cash flow inflects hard, which is exactly why ADRO already carries a 12.5% trailing yield (windfall payout) and ITMG historically runs a fortress net-cash balance sheet that converts price upside straight into special dividends. The macro frame matters: a high coal price is mildly IDR-supportive via the trade surplus and gives BI room to hold rates, but coal is a price-taker on a structurally declining seaborne demand path, and the same export leverage that makes ITMG/ADRO attractive at the top turns into the sharpest downside in a crash. At USD 80 Newcastle the math inverts — domestic DMO at ~USD 70 becomes a near-floor that protects PTBA's margin while export names compress toward marginal cost, so the attractiveness ranking flips. Net: high-price regime favors export leverage (ITMG > ADRO > PTBA); a crash to USD 80 favors the domestic floor and balance-sheet quality (ITMG > PTBA > ADRO).
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Energi', keywords=['coal','batubara'], min_mcap_tn=5, fields=['ticker','mcap_tn','roe','div_yield','pb','pe','net_cash'])  -- pull the full thermal-coal cohort: PTBA, ADRO, ITMG, BYAN, GEMS, BSSR, AADI

run_query("SELECT f.ticker, f.roe, f.dividend_yield, f.pb, f.payout_ratio, f.net_debt_to_equity FROM fundamentals f WHERE f.ticker IN ('PTBA','ITMG','ADRO','BYAN') ORDER BY f.dividend_yield DESC")  -- balance-sheet + payout to gauge windfall capacity vs DMO drag

run_query("SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ret_3m FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('PTBA','ITMG','ADRO','BYAN','GEMS') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC")  -- relative price action to see what the market already discounts (BYAN -46% in brief)
get_company('ITMG')  -- confirm export vs domestic revenue mix and net-cash position before ranking it #1 in both regimes

picks.rationale: High-price regime: top pick. Heaviest export skew of the three plus a net-cash balance sheet, so spot upside converts directly to special dividends; also the most defensive in a crash because no leverage to service.

High-price regime: #2. Export-levered with a 12.5% windfall yield in the brief, max cash-flow upside at the top — but the same operating leverage makes it the weakest of the three if Newcastle crashes to USD 80.

High-price regime: #3 (laggard) but the crash hedge. DMO/PLN domestic skew caps its ASP near USD 70 so it barely participates in a spike, yet that same floor protects its ~10.7% SOE-backed yield and margin when export peers compress.

Read-across comp, not a core buy. Premium ROE (31) and mcap 373tn but already -46% trailing-1y and a thin 3.7% yield — illiquid free float and rich valuation make it the high-beta way to express the cycle, not the risk-adjusted one.

Export-cohort confirmation: ROE 38 and 14.7% yield in the brief validate that seaborne-levered names carry the windfall payouts; supports the thesis that export skew = cycle attractiveness at the top.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if DMO policy changes — a higher domestic price cap (toward HBA/market) or a tighter export quota would erase PTBA's relative drag and gut the export premium, flipping the ranking. Other invalidators: a sustained China/India seaborne demand collapse that drives global below DMO USD 70 (then even ITMG/ADRO sell into a stranded export market with no domestic floor), a sharp IDR move altering USD-revenue translation, and the fact that the high trailing yields (ADRO 12.5%, PTBA 10.7%) are partly non-recurring windfall payouts that will normalize down as prices mean-revert — so don't underwrite them as a steady-state coupon.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia's nickel complex is a cost-curve story, not an EV-demand story: with LME nickel deep in the cost curve, the swing factor is Indonesian ore (saprolite/limonite) cost, RKAB ore quotas, and the HPM domestic ore price formula — names with captive low-grade ore + captive coal-fired power (Indonesia's grid and IMIP/IWIP smelters run on cheap domestic thermal coal, so a weak coal cycle actually subsidizes smelter cash costs) sit bottom-quartile and stay cash-positive while merchant RKEF/HPAL converters bleed. The macro overlay matters two ways: a weak IDR is a tailwind because nickel revenue is USD while ore, labor and coal power are IDR-denominated, lifting rupiah margins even as LME falls; but BI's still-elevated rate and the government's resource-nationalism shift (export levy/royalty hikes, DMO-style ore policy, the downstream "hilirisasi" mandate) raise the cost of the heavy capex these integrated builds require, punishing balance-sheet-stretched names. Foreign flows are the bubble vector — ANTM and BRMS are among the most liquid Barang Baku names (ANTM ~496rp bn ADV) and trade as EV/foreign-flow proxies, so their multiples detach from realized smelter economics, whereas the discipline test is whether ROE and operating cash flow survive a flat-to-down LME. Bottom line: differentiate on integration depth and unit cash cost, not on the EV narrative — captive-power, captive-ore, equity-method-into-finished-nickel names are sustainable; merchant smelters and pure ore/hype proxies are the bubble.
tool_queries: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn, l.return_on_equity AS roe, l.profit_margins, l.price_to_book, l.dividend_yield FROM latest l WHERE l.ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','NCKL','MBMA','NICL','BRMS','MDKA','AMMN') ORDER BY roe DESC

SELECT ticker, broker_code, SUM(net_value) net FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','BRMS') AND date >= '2026-03-01' GROUP BY ticker, broker_code ORDER BY ticker, net DESC LIMIT 40

picks.rationale: Highest-ROE confirmed nickel/metals name in the brief (BarangBaku ROE 42%) — survives the framework's cash-cost test; quality bucket, not bubble.
Most-liquid nickel proxy (ADV ~496rp bn) and state-backed integrated miner with gold optionality, but its valuation is the prime foreign-flow/EV-hype vector — own the asset base, not the multiple.

Integrated metals heavyweight (mcap 375tn) but ROE only ~5% with heavy smelter/downstream capex drag — textbook 'integration is expensive' name; quality of assets, low current returns, balance-sheet watch.
Second-most-liquid Barang Baku name (ADV ~441rp bn) trading as a metals momentum/flow proxy — highest bubble risk in the cluster, position only on broker-accumulation evidence.
Confirmed high-ROE metals name (BarangBaku ROE 40%) as a low-cost integrated benchmark for the 'sustainable' bucket against which to rank the hype names.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if LME nickel re-rates up (China stimulus or an Indonesian RKAB ore-quota squeeze tightening global supply) — that lifts even merchant/high-cost smelters and rewards the hype names, so the low-cost/integrated edge stops mattering and the cheap-discipline trade underperforms the momentum trade. Second invalidator: Yahoo-derived ROE/P/B in the brief are flagged unreliable for IDX names (INCO/NCKL/MBMA aren't even in the brief's heavyweight/liquidity tables), so if run_query against fundamentals shows the cost-curve and net-debt picture diverging from the snapshot ratios, the integration ranking must be rebuilt from realized unit cash cost and operating cash flow, not the screen.

answers.reasoning: Yield trap risk di coal IDX nyata: PTBA/ITMG/BSSR yield 10-15% itu trailing, dihitung dari laba 2022-23 saat harga Newcastle ~$300-400/ton; sekarang thermal coal sudah turun ke ~$110-130 area dan kontrak ekspor RI mayoritas mengikuti HBA + diskon, jadi earnings dan payout 2025-26 hampir pasti turun — payout ratio coal sering 75-100%, jadi dividen bergerak lockstep dengan harga batubara, bukan smoothed. Transmisi makro: BI rate masih tinggi (~6%) menahan IDR, dan emiten coal punya beban biaya USD (royalti naik via skema baru + DMO) sementara revenue USD tertekan; ketika The Fed cut dan DXY melemah, emas justru countercyclical — naik saat real yield turun, persis kebalikan siklus coal. ANTM/MDKA(EMAS) memberi eksposur emas tapi yield-nya tipis dan ANTM lebih ke volume/harga daripada dividen stabil, jadi emas itu hedge growth/harga, bukan income stream. Untuk passive income STABIL, jawabannya bukan overweight coal di puncak siklus maupun ganti ke emas yield-rendah, tapi barbell: ambil satu-dua coal yield-tinggi dengan cash kuat & cadangan panjang (ITMG net cash) sebagai income oportunistik yang Anda terima boleh turun, lalu jangkar portofolio di dividend defensif non-siklikal — bank besar (BBRI/BBNI yield 9-10% didukung ROE 18% dan NIM, bukan harga komoditas), telco/infra regulated (TLKM 6.8%, PGAS 9.9%), dan utility (POWR 9.7%) — yang dividennya disokong cashflow recurring, bukan harga spot.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, dividend_yield, payout_ratio, roe, net_debt_to_equity FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('PTBA','ITMG','ADRO','AADI','BSSR','GEMS','BYAN','ANTM','MDKA','EMAS') ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC;

SELECT f.ticker, f.sector, f.dividend_yield, f.roe, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m FROM fundamentals f JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE f.dividend_yield > 6 AND f.market_cap > 5e12 AND f.payout_ratio < 0.85 ORDER BY r.ret_1y DESC;

screen_stocks(sectors=['Energi','Keuangan','Infrastruktur'], min_dividend_yield=6, min_roe=15, max_payout_ratio=0.9, min_mcap_tn=5, sort='dividend_yield desc')

picks.rationale: Coal income pick terbaik: yield ~10% dengan posisi net-cash dan cadangan/biaya lebih sehat, jadi paling tahan saat harga coal turun dibanding peers leveraged — terima saja dividennya boleh menyusut dari puncak.
Yield 10.7% dan backing BUMN (kontrak domestik PLN via DMO) memberi dasar volume lebih stabil, tapi tetap perlakukan sebagai income siklikal, bukan jangkar.
Jangkar income stabil: yield 10.4% disokong ROE 18% dan NIM mikro-banking, dividen dari cashflow recurring, bukan harga komoditas — anti yield-trap.
Pelengkap bank: yield 9.4%, valuasi murah, dividen berbasis laba kredit yang jauh lebih halus siklusnya daripada coal.
Defensif regulated, yield 6.8% dengan ROE 18% dan cashflow langganan — komponen stabilitas non-siklikal portofolio income.
Eksposur countercyclical ke emas (hedge saat siklus coal turun & DXY melemah), tapi diposisikan sebagai growth/price hedge — yield rendah, bukan sumber income stabil.

answers.risk: Thesis batal kalau coal mengalami siklus tinggi berkelanjutan (gangguan supply Australia/Rusia, lonjakan permintaan listrik India/China, geopolitik energi) sehingga HBA bertahan tinggi dan payout coal tetap besar 2-3 tahun — saat itu overweight coal justru menang dan jangkar bank/telco underperform. Risiko spesifik lain: BI/OJK menekan payout bank demi capital buffer (memangkas yield 9-10% itu), royalti/DMO coal berubah, dan emas (ANTM) bisa mengecewakan sebagai income karena yield-nya tipis sehingga 'countercyclical' hanya bekerja pada apresiasi harga, bukan dividen. Validasi cepat: cek tren dividend_per_share & payout_ratio 3-5 tahun terakhir per nama via query di atas sebelum allocate.

answers.category: FX shock / sector rotation — IDR 17k + aggressive BI defense
answers.reasoning: An IDR break through 17,000 forces BI to defend with FX-reserve burn and a restrictive-to-higher BI rate, and that rate path — not the currency itself — is the real transmission channel. High rates plus stretched valuations punish rate-sensitive, high-debt sectors first (Properti, Teknologi/growth), while foreign outflows concentrate in the most foreign-owned and most-liquid large caps (banks BBCA/BBRI/BMRI dominate liquidity and the foreign book, so they are the funding source for any exodus). Exporters with USD revenue and IDR cost bases — coal (ADRO, PTBA, BYAN), CPO (TAPG, SSMS), nickel/gold (ANTM, AMMN) — get a direct earnings-translation tailwind, which is exactly why Energi (+120% trailing) and Barang Baku are already leading while the foreign-heavy banks (Keuangan +47%, weakest) lag. But the rotation only pays if the dollar revenue is real: a weak IDR with falling coal/CPO prices is a value trap, and consumer staples (UNVR, ICBP, INDF) are the ones squeezed by imported-input inflation and weak purchasing power — so the rotation thesis is sound but conditional on the commodity tape, not the FX print alone.
tool_queries: SELECT f.ticker, f.sector, f.dividend_yield, f.roe, r.ret_1y FROM fundamentals f JOIN returns r ON f.ticker=r.ticker WHERE f.sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND f.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','BYAN','ITMG','BSSR','GEMS','AADI','TAPG','SSMS','NSSS','ANTM','AMMN','NICL') ORDER BY f.dividend_yield DESC;

SELECT o.ticker, o.foreign_pct, l.mcap_tn, v.avg_daily_value_bn FROM ownership o JOIN latest l ON o.ticker=l.ticker JOIN (SELECT ticker, avg_daily_value_bn FROM eod_summary) v ON o.ticker=v.ticker WHERE o.foreign_pct > 50 AND l.mcap_tn > 50 ORDER BY o.foreign_pct DESC;

screen_stocks(sector IN ['Properti & Real Estat','Teknologi'], pb < 0.7 OR debt_to_equity > 1, order_by=ret_1y ASC) -- find rate-sensitive leverage names most exposed to BI staying high (BSDE, CTRA, SMRA, LPKR, GOTO, BUKA)

SELECT b.ticker, b.foreign_net_bn, b.date FROM broker_net b WHERE b.ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','TLKM','ASII') AND b.date >= date('now','-20 day') ORDER BY b.date DESC -- confirm whether foreign flows have already started rotating out of the foreign-heavy large caps

picks.rationale: USD-priced coal revenue vs IDR costs plus a 12.5% yield — the cleanest weak-IDR translation play that pays you to wait for trend confirmation.
State-linked coal exporter, ~10.7% yield; dollar earnings tailwind from a weak rupiah with a dividend cushion if commodity prices wobble.
CPO exporter, ~34% ROE and ~8.9% yield; palm-oil USD pricing benefits directly while consumer staples get squeezed by imported inflation.
Nickel/gold exporter and one of the most liquid Barang Baku names (496 rp bn/day) — gold especially is a natural hedge in an FX-defense scenario.
REDUCE/funding source — consumer staple with imported inputs and weak purchasing power; the rotation-out candidate squeezed by both input-cost inflation and high rates.
AVOID — high-debt, rate-sensitive property (P/B 0.37) is the first to break if BI is forced to hold rates restrictive to defend the rupiah.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the IDR weakness coincides with falling commodity prices — a weak rupiah plus a cracking coal/CPO/nickel tape turns exporters into value traps (note BYAN is already -46% trailing despite being an exporter, proof the FX tailwind doesn't save you when the commodity rolls over). Other invalidators: BI defends successfully and the rupiah snaps back (rate-sensitive consumer/property re-rate and you've sold the bottom), or a global risk-off drags everything down indiscriminately so exporters fall on outflows regardless of earnings. Tactically, scale in rather than rotate all at once — confirm the trend with two signals: coal/CPO spot holding up and foreign broker-net flow actually turning negative on the banks before committing to the full consumer-to-exporter switch.

answers.category: FX-sensitivity rotation: importer margin compression vs USD-revenue exporters under 8-10% IDR depreciation
answers.reasoning: Transmission: IDR weakness (driven by BI cutting toward ~5.25% while the Fed stays higher-for-longer, plus fiscal-deficit/foreign-outflow pressure on the rupiah) raises the IDR cost of USD-denominated raw materials (steel/components for ASII, wheat/dairy for INDF/UNVR) roughly 1-for-1, but pass-through to retail prices lags 1-3 quarters, so the depreciation lands on gross margin first. The three importers already run thin: from the data ASII profit margin is ~10%, INDF ~6.5%, ICBP ~8% — so a COGS line that is, say, 40-55% imported taking an 8-10% FX hit can compress operating margin by ~150-350bps before any hedging or price hikes, with INDF most exposed (Bogasari wheat is dollar-priced and import-bound) and ASII cushioned by UNTR/heavy-equipment and a 6.7% yield. The crucial nuance: UNVR's ~24% margin and ~107% ROE come from brand pricing power, not FX immunity — it can re-price faster than INDF but is the most expensive in the book (P/B ~16), so the FX hit is real but mean-reverting, not existential. Pure exporters invert the mechanism: ADRO/BYAN/PTBA bill in USD against IDR cost bases, so depreciation is pure margin tailwind (coal pm ~22-24% vs ASII ~10%), and the energy sector's #3 trailing-1y rank plus double-digit yields (ADRO 12.5%, PTBA 10.7%) give you paid-to-wait optionality — but ANTM is a weaker "exporter" hedge (pm only ~8.5%, nickel oversupplied), so the cleaner FX/commodity expression is coal dividends or CPO (TAPG pm ~32%, yield ~8.9%, a partial natural hedge as palm exports earn USD).
tool_queries: run_query: SELECT s.ticker, s.profit_margins, s.return_on_equity, s.price_to_book, s.dividend_yield, c.sub_sector FROM summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('ASII','INDF','UNVR','ICBP','ADRO','ANTM','PTBA','BYAN','TAPG') ORDER BY s.profit_margins ASC;  -- rank margin cushion: importers vs exporters

run_query: SELECT ticker, company_background FROM companies WHERE ticker IN ('INDF','UNVR','ASII') AND (company_background ILIKE '%import%' OR company_background ILIKE '%wheat%' OR company_background ILIKE '%raw material%');  -- confirm USD-COGS exposure qualitatively
screen_stocks: sector='Energi', min_dividend_yield=9, min_profit_margin=15, sort='dividend_yield desc'  -- surface USD-revenue coal payers that benefit from weak IDR (ADRO/PTBA/BYAN/GEMS/BSSR)
run_query: SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ASII','INDF','UNVR','ADRO','PTBA','ANTM','TAPG') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC;  -- check the FX/commodity trade is not already fully priced in

picks.rationale: Thinnest margin of your three (pm ~6.5%) and most import-bound via Bogasari dollar-priced wheat — biggest proportional margin hit, but cheap at P/B ~0.75 so the bad news is partly in the price; trim, don't dump.

pm ~10% with imported auto/heavy-equipment components, but UNTR coal exposure and a 6.7% yield make it a partial self-hedge; hold the FX pain is real but diversified — the most defensible of the three to keep.

Highest importer margin (~24%) and ~107% ROE means brand pricing power re-prices the FX hit fastest; the catch is valuation (P/B ~16) and a -29%-type consumer-staples derating risk, so margin compression is cyclical not structural — hold, don't add.
Cleanest pure-exporter switch: USD coal revenue vs IDR costs turns depreciation into a tailwind, pm ~24% and a ~12.5% yield pay you to wait; the textbook hedge against your importer book.
Second coal leg with ~10.7% yield and state-owned ballast; USD-linked thermal coal pricing makes it FX-positive, lower beta than ADRO/BYAN for a more defensive rotation sleeve.

Better risk-adjusted exporter than ANTM: CPO earns USD (natural FX hedge), pm ~32% and yield ~8.9%, and palm is in a tighter supply cycle than oversupplied nickel — prefer this over ANTM (pm only ~8.5%) if rotating into 'pure exporter'.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if BI defends the rupiah (rate hikes / FX intervention) and IDR depreciation stalls below ~5%, or if the importers pass FX through to prices within 1-2 quarters faster than expected — then the margin compression is transient and the importers re-rate up while you've paid the switching cost. Bigger tail risk: a global growth/China slowdown cracks the coal and nickel cycle, so ADRO/PTBA's USD revenue and yields fall even as IDR weakens (commodity beta swamps the FX tailwind) — coal is already a crowded, high-yield consensus trade (energy sector +120% trailing-1y, BYAN -46% shows how fast it unwinds). Also note yf P/B and some ratios in this dataset are unreliable for ADRO/BYAN/AMMN; size the rotation on margin/yield logic, not on the screen's reported P/B.

answers.reasoning: A Fed delay plus DXY toward 108 keeps US real yields elevated and the IDR under 16,500+ pressure, which forces Bank Indonesia to hold or hike to defend the rupiah rather than cut — that kills the rate-cut multiple-expansion thesis that drove the 2024-25 rally. The transmission to IDX is mechanical: foreigners are most concentrated in the liquid big-cap banks (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI are ~3 of the top-4 ADTV names and the largest foreign-held free floats), so a USD-funding squeeze hits them first and hardest — our flow data shows BBRI/BBCA/BMRI and GOTO as the biggest net foreign sellers in recent sessions, while coal names (ADRO/PTBA/AADI) actually saw net foreign buying. What survives a foreign sell-off is the low-beta, USD-hedged or domestic-cashflow cohort: dollar-revenue coal exporters (ADRO, PTBA — beta -0.23/-0.20) actually benefit from a weak IDR since costs are rupiah and coal is priced in USD, while sticky-demand staples (UNVR, ICBP, SIDO) and the monopoly telco TLKM carry sub-0.2 beta and double-digit-ROE domestic cash that foreigners are structurally underweight, so they have less to sell. The fiscal overhang (wide deficit, costly Danantara/free-meals spend) amplifies IDR risk and argues for high-yield dividend payers over rate-sensitive property and tech (GOTO, BUKA, SMRA, PANI) which are the first to be dumped.
tool_queries: run_query: SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell)/1e9 AS net_foreign_bn, SUM(value)/1e9 AS traded_bn FROM eod_summary WHERE date >= DATE '2026-03-01' GROUP BY ticker HAVING SUM(value) > 5e12 ORDER BY net_foreign_bn ASC LIMIT 20  -- who foreigners are actually dumping vs accumulating

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, s.beta, s.dividend_yield, s.return_on_equity, s.price_to_book, s.market_cap FROM summary s WHERE s.market_cap > 50e12 AND s.beta < 0.3 ORDER BY s.beta ASC  -- low-beta big-cap defensives most resilient to a sell-off
screen_stocks: {min_market_cap: 50e12, max_beta: 0.2, min_dividend_yield: 4, sort_by: dividend_yield, order: desc} -- large, low-beta, high-yield names that hold up when foreigners de-risk

run_query: SELECT ticker, SUM(CASE WHEN local_foreign='FOREIGN' THEN percentage ELSE 0 END) AS foreign_pct FROM ownership GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY foreign_pct DESC -- crowding check: avoid names with the most foreign ownership to unwind

picks.rationale: Beta 0.15, monopoly telco with ~7% yield and 100% domestic IDR cash flow; foreigners structurally underweight so little left to dump in a sell-off.
Lowest-beta bank (0.11), 21% ROE, deposit-franchise fortress; gets sold first on outflows but recovers fastest and is the quality core to add into weakness.
Negative beta (-0.23) USD coal exporter with ~12% yield; a weak IDR boosts rupiah-translated earnings and our data shows net foreign BUYING, not selling.
Beta -0.20, ~11% dividend yield, state-backed coal; classic foreign-sell-off hedge — dollar revenue, rupiah cost base, and net foreign accumulation in the data.
Beta 0.17, 100%+ ROE consumer-staples cash machine with sticky domestic demand; defensive multiple holds when growth/rate-sensitive names get hit.
Beta 0.11, ~37% ROE herbal-staples with high payout and minimal leverage; low foreign crowding makes it resilient to a DXY-driven outflow.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the IDR sell-off turns disorderly: a forced BI hike or a break past ~16,800/USD triggers indiscriminate de-risking where even low-beta defensives get sold for liquidity, and the dollar-coal hedge (ADRO/PTBA) fails if a global growth scare drags coal prices down faster than the IDR weakens (China demand shock), wiping out the FX tailwind. Also invalidating: a dovish Fed surprise or BI rate cut reverses the whole setup and rotates flows back into the high-beta banks and rate-sensitive property/tech the thesis is avoiding.

answers.reasoning: The trap is a currency mismatch on the balance sheet: issuers funded in USD (global bonds / dollar project loans) but earning rupiah get hit twice when IDR weakens — a non-cash FX translation loss balloons the rupiah value of the liability, and the USD coupon costs more rupiah to service, compressing interest coverage even if the operating business is fine. With BI holding rates relatively high to defend the rupiah and foreign flows fickle (banks dominate the index, commodity names like ADRO/PTBA/BYAN are natural USD hedges, not victims), the vulnerable cohort is capital-intensive DOMESTIC-revenue sectors: property developers (USD global notes vs IDR pre-sales — LPKR, BSDE, PANI, SMRA), toll roads/infra with USD-linked project debt against government-regulated IDR tariffs (JSMR), and IDR-revenue names carrying large USD obligations. The brief's cheapest-P/B and worst-1y screens are concentrated in exactly these rate-sensitive, high-debt property/infra names — the market is already pricing balance-sheet stress, and a further IDR leg-down (toward/through 16,500-17,000) is what turns leverage into a solvency conversation. Critically, you screen by financial_report_currency containing 'USD' or high total_debt/debt_to_equity in the summary table, then EXCLUDE USD earners (coal/CPO/nickel exporters) to isolate the true mismatch.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Infrastruktur') AND debt_to_equity > 100 ORDER BY total_debt DESC  -- rate-sensitive, IDR-revenue, leverage-heavy cohort

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, s.sector, s.total_debt, s.debt_to_equity, s.revenue, s.current_ratio, s.ebitda_margins, f.financial_report_currency FROM summary s LEFT JOIN fundamentals f ON s.ticker=f.ticker WHERE f.financial_report_currency LIKE '%USD%' ORDER BY s.total_debt DESC LIMIT 40  -- names that REPORT/borrow in USD; flag those whose business is domestic

run_query: SELECT ticker, sector, total_debt, total_cash, debt_to_equity, current_ratio, free_cash_flow FROM summary WHERE sector NOT IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND debt_to_equity > 150 ORDER BY debt_to_equity DESC  -- isolate high-leverage IDR earners, excluding commodity USD-revenue natural hedges

run_query: SELECT ticker, sector, total_debt, free_cash_flow, current_ratio FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('LPKR','BSDE','PANI','SMRA','CTRA','JSMR','TLKM','MDKA','ASII') ORDER BY total_debt DESC  -- verify coverage/liquidity for the candidate mismatch names from the brief

picks.rationale: Lippo Karawaci is the textbook IDR-revenue/USD-debt mismatch: a history of USD global bonds against domestic property pre-sales; P/B 0.25 and deep-discount valuation already prices balance-sheet/FX stress — most exposed if IDR jeblok.
Large developer with USD note issuance but rupiah pre-sales and recurring income; P/B 0.37 in the cheapest-decile screen reflects leverage and FX translation risk on a weaker rupiah.

Aggressive PIK/PSN-driven landbank expansion funded with heavy leverage against IDR property cash flows; thin 0.1% yield and ROE 7 on a 157tn mcap signal capital intensity that a USD-funding leg makes acutely IDR-sensitive.

Summarecon carries developer debt against domestic recurring/township revenue; P/B 0.47 puts it in the same rate-and-FX-sensitive property cohort that suffers when servicing costs rise in rupiah terms.

Jasa Marga funds toll-road capex with large, partly USD-linked project debt while tariffs are government-regulated in rupiah — the classic infra mismatch where a weak IDR lifts the rupiah debt/interest burden against capped IDR income (P/B 0.62).

Ciputra is a leveraged developer with rupiah pre-sales; P/B 0.55 places it with LPKR/BSDE/SMRA in the IDR-revenue, debt-heavy property group that is structurally exposed to any USD-funding line as the rupiah falls.

answers.risk: Main invalidator: the screen must be confirmed against the summary table's total_debt + financial_report_currency, because much of property/infra USD exposure may be NATURALLY HEDGED or already refinanced into IDR/bank loans (or hedged via cross-currency swaps) — in which case the FX 'time bomb' is just a non-cash accounting loss, not a cash-flow/solvency event. Two specific traps: (1) yfsummary debt_to_equity and revenue are flagged unreliable for IDR names (IDR-vs-USD scaling), so leverage must be cross-checked in fundamentals/keystats, not taken at face value; (2) if BI cuts and IDR stabilizes/strengthens, this entire mismatch cohort re-rates UP fastest (high beta to rates), so the thesis is a tail-hedge against IDR weakness, not an outright short — and commodity USD-earners (ADRO, PTBA, BYAN, AALI) are explicitly NOT in scope since their dollar revenue offsets dollar debt.

answers.category: macro-scenario equity selection (IDR weakness + commodity downcycle, 12M hold)
answers.reasoning: Skenario IDR ke 16.500 + harga komoditas turun memisahkan ketiganya lewat dua transmisi: sensitivitas komoditas dan struktur revenue/biaya valas. PGAS adalah distributor gas dengan pricing yang sebagian besar di-referensikan USD sementara sebagian opex rupiah, jadi IDR lemah cenderung netral-positif ke margin, dan oil/LNG yang turun justru menekan biaya feedstock sehingga spread distribusi di bawah skema HGBT bisa melebar—plus yield ~9.9% sebagai bantalan saat asing keluar dan BI menahan rate tinggi untuk jaga rupiah. TLKM telco rupiah murni tanpa eksposur komoditas; permintaan data inelastis bikin dia defensif, satu-satunya minus adalah capex/utang USD yang sedikit mahal saat IDR lemah dan dia rentan outflow asing karena masuk top-10 paling likuid. MDKA adalah yang paling kalah: revenue nikel/tembaga/emas turun langsung kena harga komoditas, dan walau IDR lemah membantu produsen biaya-rupiah, beban utang USD tinggi + posisi cashflow proyek nikel yang belum matang membuat downcycle komoditas jadi pukulan ganda tanpa yield penyangga. Konteks fiskal 2026 (defisit melebar, BI cenderung hawkish jaga rupiah) memperkuat preferensi ke cashflow/dividen defensif ketimbang growth/komoditas leveraged.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, dividend_yield, price_to_book, close FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('TLKM','MDKA','PGAS')
SELECT r.ticker, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m, r.cagr_3y FROM returns r WHERE r.ticker IN ('TLKM','MDKA','PGAS') ORDER BY r.ret_1y DESC
SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_60d FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('TLKM','MDKA','PGAS') AND date >= date('now','-60 day') GROUP BY ticker
screen_stocks(sector='Energi', dividend_yield>='8', market_cap>='5e12', sort='dividend_yield desc')

picks.rationale: Pilihan utama: distributor gas dengan pricing referensi USD + biaya rupiah (IDR lemah netral-positif), feedstock oil/LNG yang turun melebarkan spread HGBT, dan yield 9.9% sebagai bantalan defensif.
Pelengkap defensif: telco rupiah murni tanpa eksposur komoditas, permintaan data inelastis, yield 6.8% dan ROE 18%; risiko hanya outflow asing & capex USD yang sedikit mahal.

Hindari/underweight dalam skenario ini: nikel/tembaga/emas kena langsung harga komoditas turun, utang USD tinggi membuat IDR lemah jadi beban, tanpa yield penyangga—risk-reward paling buruk untuk hold 12 bulan.

answers.risk: Tesis batal kalau komoditas justru rebound (nikel/emas naik) atau IDR cepat stabil di bawah 16.000—itu membalik narasi dan MDKA yang high-beta melonjak paling kencang. Risiko spesifik PGAS: regulasi HGBT memaksa harga jual gas industri lebih rendah (margin tertekan) atau gangguan pasokan; risiko TLKM: perang tarif data dan persaingan FWA/fixed-broadband menekan ARPU. Sinyal pembatal lain: net_foreign yang dari query eod_summary menunjukkan akumulasi asing deras ke MDKA, atau emas naik tajam sebagai safe-haven saat IDR lemah (justru menolong sisi emas MDKA).

answers.reasoning: A stronger rupiah on an improving trade surplus is a terms-of-trade + rate story, and the transmission to IDX runs through three channels. First, foreign flows: IDR strength removes the FX-loss that has kept offshore money underweight Indonesia, and that money buys the most liquid, most foreign-owned large-caps — which on IDX means banks (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI together dominate mcap and the daily tape). Second, the BI rate: a firmer IDR and contained imported inflation give Bank Indonesia room to cut, which directly re-rates rate-sensitive domestics — banks (NIM/loan-growth and bond book), property (high debt, mortgage demand), and consumer-discretionary/tech (long-duration, capex-funded). Third, the import-cost channel the investor flagged is real: domestic-revenue companies that import inputs (consumer staples buying USD raw materials, names carrying USD debt) see rupiah margins expand — but it is a thinner, second-order edge than the bank+flow trade, and it is precisely the mirror image of the USD-revenue commodity exporters (coal/CPO/nickel like ADRO, PTBA, TAPG, ANTM) whose rupiah earnings deflate when IDR strengthens, so the cleaner trade is to rotate OUT of the dividend-heavy energy/commodity complex and INTO domestic rate-beta. So yes, leaning toward import-sensitive names is directionally correct, but the highest-conviction expression of an IDR-bull view on IDX is overweight banks + rate-sensitive domestics, funded by trimming USD-revenue commodity exporters.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(filters: sector IN ['Keuangan'], market_cap > 100e12, sort by return_on_equity DESC) -- isolate the liquid, foreign-owned large-cap banks (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/BBNI) that absorb returning foreign flow and gear to BI cuts

SELECT e.ticker, l.sector, SUM(e.foreign_buy - e.foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_30d FROM eod_summary e JOIN latest l ON l.ticker = e.ticker WHERE e.date >= (SELECT max(date) FROM eod_summary) - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY net_foreign_30d DESC LIMIT 25 -- confirm where foreign money is already rotating (validate the flow thesis with the tape, not just narrative)

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.return_on_equity, l.dividend_yield, l.price_to_book, l.beta, r.ret_6m, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING (ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Barang Konsumen Non-Primer','Teknologi') AND l.market_cap > 20e12 ORDER BY l.price_to_book ASC -- find rate-sensitive domestics (property/discretionary/tech) that are cheap on P/B and would re-rate on a BI cut

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING (ticker) WHERE l.sector = 'Energi' AND l.dividend_yield > 8 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC -- identify the USD-revenue coal/energy dividend names (ADRO/PTBA/BYAN) to UNDERWEIGHT as the IDR-strength headwind / funding source

picks.rationale: Top-tier IDR-bull vehicle: deeply foreign-owned, 2nd-most-liquid name on the tape, 18% ROE and 10.4% yield — the prime beneficiary of returning foreign flow plus loan-growth/NIM upside on BI cuts.
Large-cap bank trading cheaper than peers, 19% ROE; bond book and corporate-loan franchise re-rate directly as IDR strength opens room for rate cuts.
Higher-beta bank laggard (P/B ~lower, 9.4% yield, 12% ROE) — gives more torque to the rate-cut/flow re-rating than the index-anchor BBCA.

Rate-sensitive property developer at 0.55x P/B; mortgage demand and balance-sheet re-rating are the most direct domestic beneficiaries of a BI easing cycle, and as a domestic-cost builder it is an import-tailwind name.

Cyclical domestic-consumption proxy (auto/financing/heavy equip) at 14% ROE, 6.7% yield; autos are an import-input + USD-cost business whose rupiah margins and volumes improve as IDR firms and rates fall.

Direct import-cost play the investor asked about: consumer-staples maker with USD-priced raw materials and USD debt, down 29% over 1y — rupiah strength expands rupiah margins and is the cleanest expression of the import-sensitivity thesis.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the trade surplus improves for the wrong reason — i.e. weak import demand from a slowing domestic economy rather than strong exports — because then bank loan growth and consumer/property volumes stall and the rate-cut re-rating fails to show up in earnings. It also inverts if global commodity prices (coal/nickel/CPO) rip: that pushes the surplus AND the exporter complex up together, IDR strengthens but the rotation OUT of ADRO/PTBA/ANTM is wrong, and the import-cost edge for ICBP is swamped by input-price inflation. Finally, the foreign-flow channel is fragile to global risk-off / a hawkish Fed (USD strength), which would re-widen the IDR carry headwind and hit exactly the high-foreign-ownership banks this book is overweight. Watch BI's policy tone, the actual export vs import split behind the surplus, and the foreign-net tape on the heavyweights as the live confirm/invalidate signals.

answers.category: FX exposure - coal and CPO exporters
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, financial_report_currency FROM fundamentals f JOIN (SELECT ticker, max(date) d FROM fundamentals GROUP BY ticker) m USING(ticker) WHERE f.date = m.d AND ticker IN ('BYAN','ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','BSSR','GEMS','AADI','TAPG','AALI','LSIP','SSMS','NSSS')

SELECT ticker, sector, operating_margins, profit_margins, debt_to_equity, total_debt, dividend_yield, return_on_equity FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','TAPG','AALI','LSIP','SSMS') ORDER BY operating_margins DESC
SELECT ticker, ret_1y, ret_3m, ret_1m FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','ADRO','ITMG','PTBA','BSSR','GEMS','TAPG','AALI','LSIP','SSMS','NSSS') ORDER BY ret_1y

SELECT ticker, sum(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign FROM eod_summary WHERE date >= current_date - 60 AND ticker IN ('BYAN','ADRO','PTBA','TAPG','AALI') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign DESC

picks.rationale: Cleanest net-long-USD play: USD-linked CPO revenue vs largely IDR cost base, ROE 34 and about 8.9 pct yield, so a weak IDR widens margins most here, gated only by the export levy and DMO.
Domestic-heavy coal, about 10.7 pct yield; USD-priced sales give real FX uplift but DMO-priced PLN volumes and IDR costs cap it, a partial dividend-anchored beneficiary.
Large coal exporter, about 12.5 pct yield; USD revenue and USD reporting mean the FX gain shows up as a margin spread not a topline windfall, and coal price dominates.
Highest-margin pure USD coal earner ROE 31, most leveraged to weak IDR, but minus 46 pct trailing shows the coal price swamps FX, so the tailwind is real but second-order.
Pure CPO upstream ROE 41 with an IDR-dominant cost stack, a textbook net beneficiary of weak rupiah, but the most exposed to the progressive export-levy clawback.
Coal exporter, about 14.7 pct yield, ROE 38; USD sales make it a beneficiary, but the price cycle and DMO obligations gate the FX upside.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the commodity price cycle turns down, which dominates the FX term given BYAN is already minus 46 pct trailing on weak ICI coal. It also breaks if the government tightens the CPO progressive export levy, the DMO, or the B40 mandate to recapture the weak-IDR windfall, which specifically kills the CPO net-long edge. Second-order, pure-play coal carries USD-denominated debt and USD contractor and diesel costs plus USD-reported books, so for the most USD-cost-heavy names the net FX benefit can compress toward flat, far less than the headline implies. And if BI cuts and IDR strengthens, the whole premise inverts.
answers.reasoning: The weak-IDR-helps-exporters line is real but unevenly distributed, and the sharpest data discriminator is the reporting currency in fundamentals financial_report_currency, e.g. an array containing USD. Coal names BYAN ADRO ITMG PTBA and CPO names TAPG AALI price into USD-denominated benchmarks (ICI and Newcastle coal, CIF Rotterdam CPO), so a weaker rupiah mechanically inflates IDR-translated revenue and EBITDA. But the cost stack splits the group, because pure-play coal runs USD-heavy contractor diesel and equipment costs and often USD-reported books, so the net FX gain compresses to a margin spread rather than the topline, making them real but only partial beneficiaries. CPO is the cleaner net-long-USD story, with USD-linked revenue against a largely IDR land and labor cost base, but that edge gets clipped by the progressive CPO export levy (pungutan ekspor), the DMO, and the B40 biodiesel mandate, the government tools to recapture the windfall and the single biggest policy swing factor. With BI holding rates relatively high to defend IDR and foreign flows light on banks, USD-earning dividend exporters are a natural hedge, yet the commodity price cycle (ICI coal down hard, BYAN minus 46 pct trailing) swamps the FX term as the first-order driver, so it is not a myth but a gated margin-spread story rather than a topline windfall.

The transmission is mechanical: a volatile IDR forces Bank Indonesia to defend the rupiah (FX intervention + a hawkish hold/hike on the BI rate), which raises the local risk-free rate and crushes the duration-sensitive, foreign-heavy names first — banks (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI are ~half of IDX market cap and the default foreign vehicle, so passive/EM outflows hit them mechanically regardless of fundamentals) plus rate-sensitive property and tech/growth. Historically on IDX, the index drawdown from an IDR-driven foreign-flow shock is sharp but the recovery is bimodal: 2-4 months once IDR stabilizes and net foreign buy turns positive in eod_summary, but a year-plus if the outflow coincides with a commodity-price down-leg. The recovery is led, first, by USD-earning commodity exporters whose cash flows actually IMPROVE on a weak IDR — coal (ADRO/PTBA/ITMG) and gold/nickel (ANTM/AMMN) — because they get a natural FX hedge and fat dividend yields that anchor the floor; then by the high-ROE liquid banks (BBCA/BBRI) that snap back fastest once foreign flow reverses because they are the re-entry vehicle. The laggards are rate-sensitive property/tech (PANI, GOTO, BUKA) with USD/IDR-cost mismatch and no dividend support, so they should NOT be the timing entry. Net: wait for two confirmations — IDR stabilization and a positive 5-day foreign net in eod_summary — then lead with USD-earner dividend payers and quality banks, not the high-beta laggards.
tool_queries:
-- Recovery clock: rank sectors by speed back to pre-shock high. Quantify drawdown depth & current recovery using the returns view.
SELECT l.sector, count(*) n, round(avg(r.ret_3m),1) avg_3m, round(avg(r.ret_6m),1) avg_6m, round(avg(r.ret_1y),1) avg_1y
FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker)
WHERE l.market_cap > 5e12
GROUP BY l.sector ORDER BY avg_3m DESC;

-- Foreign-flow turn signal: net foreign (buy-sell) per ticker over the last ~20 sessions vs the prior shock window. Positive + accelerating net = the entry trigger.
SELECT ticker,
  sum(CASE WHEN date >= (SELECT max(date) FROM eod_summary) - INTERVAL 20 DAY THEN foreign_buy - foreign_sell END) net_20d,
  sum(CASE WHEN date >= (SELECT max(date) FROM eod_summary) - INTERVAL 60 DAY THEN foreign_buy - foreign_sell END) net_60d
FROM eod_summary GROUP BY ticker
ORDER BY net_20d DESC LIMIT 30;

-- Which USD-earner / dividend names lead: high yield + positive recent foreign flow + commodity sectors, screened on the latest view.
SELECT ticker, sector, market_cap, dividend_yield, return_on_equity, ret_1y
FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND dividend_yield >= 5 AND market_cap > 5e12
ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC LIMIT 20;

-- Bank re-entry timing: daily foreign net on the big-4 banks to time the snapback (banks are the foreign re-entry vehicle).
SELECT date, ticker, foreign_buy - foreign_sell AS foreign_net
FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI')
  AND date >= (SELECT max(date) FROM eod_summary) - INTERVAL 30 DAY
ORDER BY date DESC, foreign_net DESC;

picks.rationale: Coal exporter earns USD while costs are IDR — weak rupiah lifts margins; 12.5% yield anchors a floor and pays you to wait through the outflow, classic first-leg recovery lead.
State coal with a 10.7% yield and USD revenue — same FX-hedge logic as ADRO; dividend cushion makes it a defensive way to ride the recovery before banks reprice.
Gold/nickel exporter and one of the most-liquid names (Rp 496bn/day) — USD-linked commodity revenue plus the liquidity foreign money re-enters through; benefits if IDR weakness is global-USD driven.

Highest-quality bank (21% ROE), most liquid stock on IDX (Rp 1.16tn/day) and the default foreign re-entry vehicle — leads the second leg once net foreign flow flips positive; buy on the flow-turn confirmation.
18% ROE plus a 10.4% yield — gets sold indiscriminately in the outflow but snaps back hardest when foreign flow reverses; the dividend pays you to hold through the lag.
Down 46% over 1y (worst big-cap) but 31% ROE and a coal USD-earner — a contrarian mean-reversion play IF the commodity leg has bottomed; size small, it is the high-variance leg of the basket.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if IDR weakness coincides with a commodity DOWN-cycle — then the USD-earner 'hedge' is swamped by falling coal/nickel/gold prices (see BYAN -46%, the energy/metals selloff), and the dividend yields are value traps as payouts get cut. The recovery clock also stretches from months to 1y+ if BI is forced into a sustained hiking cycle (structural, not volatility-driven, IDR pressure) or if the outflow is global EM de-risking rather than Indonesia-specific — in that case banks keep bleeding on passive selling regardless of fundamentals. Invalidation signal: net foreign in eod_summary stays negative for 60d+ AND coal/nickel reference prices keep falling — do not catch the knife until both the IDR and the commodity leg stabilize."

answers.reasoning: Pair USD-revenue commodity exporters, whose rupiah earnings inflate when IDR weakens, against IDR-revenue importers and domestic franchises that de-rate on a weak IDR, so the two legs offset on the FX axis while both stay long Indonesia. A weaker IDR from the Fed-vs-BI rate gap, foreign outflows from SBN and equities, and a wider current-account and fiscal deficit lifts the rupiah value of coal and CPO export receipts at PTBA, ADRO and UNTR, while squeezing UNVR and ICBP whose COGS is dollarized inputs but pricing is sticky IDR. Coal exporters show negative-to-zero beta near -0.36 for ITMG and BYAN, -0.23 ADRO, -0.20 PTBA, -0.11 UNTR, a defensive FX-up sleeve, while staples sit near-zero beta and re-rate on a stronger IDR plus a falling BI rate. Banks like BBCA are domestic-IDR ballast that rally with staples in a strong-IDR rate-cut regime, tightening basket volatility rather than its FX direction.
answers.risk: The two legs offset translation FX but share one commodity-price axis, so a global coal or CPO price crash sinks the exporter leg even if IDR weakens, meaning the negative betas are regime-dependent not structural. The basket hedges currency translation, not a broad equity drawdown, since a risk-off shock hits all Indonesian equities at once as foreigners exit banks and commodities together. It inverts in a strong-IDR regime where BI cuts converge with the Fed and the current-account gap narrows on a commodity windfall, with exporters de-rating while staples and banks rally. Treat the negative yf betas for BYAN and ADRO directionally given the data-reliability caveat in the brief.

answers.reasoning: Skenario yield SBN 10y naik tajam + BI defend IDR (hold/hike high) adalah lingkungan yang secara struktural menguntungkan bank besar di atas properti. Transmisinya: bank Indonesia mayoritas pendanaannya CASA murah (BBCA ~70%+ CASA) sehingga NIM justru melebar saat asset yield naik lebih cepat dari cost of funds, dan kupon SBN tinggi langsung menambah net interest income dari portofolio obligasi pemerintah mereka — ROE BBRI 18%, BBCA 21%, BMRI 19% memberi bantalan tebal. Properti sebaliknya kena pukulan ganda: KPR (sumber demand utama) naik mengikuti BI rate sehingga affordability hancur, sementara neraca developer yang highly leveraged membuat beban bunga naik — tercermin di P/B tertekan (LPKR 0.25, BSDE 0.37, SMRA 0.47, CTRA 0.55) dan yield dividen tipis. Risiko spesifik Indonesia: defend IDR berarti BI bisa menahan rate tinggi lebih lama (higher-for-longer) dan jual SBN/intervensi, plus foreign outflow dari nama berkepemilikan asing tinggi (BNLI 98.7%, BTPN 98.6) bisa menekan harga saham bank di luar fundamentalnya — jadi pilih bank dengan funding domestik kuat dan likuiditas tinggi (BBCA, BBRI, BMRI termasuk 4 paling likuid). Kesimpulan: bank jelas lebih survive, dengan BBCA paling defensif (CASA + kualitas aset) dan BBRI menawarkan yield 10.4% sebagai bantalan; properti adalah avoid/underweight kecuali low-leverage dividend outlier (DMAS).

SELECT * FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BSDE','CTRA','SMRA','LPKR') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC; -- compare bank vs property trailing performance in rate-up regime
screen_stocks(sector='Keuangan', min_mcap_tn=100, order_by='dividend_yield desc') -- isolate liquid big-cap banks with dividend cushion
SELECT ticker, foreign_pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','BNLI','BTPN') ORDER BY foreign_pct DESC; -- gauge foreign-outflow vulnerability under IDR defense

picks.rationale: Paling survive: CASA dominan + kualitas aset terbaik bikin NIM tahan saat rate naik; ROE 21%, paling likuid (Rp1.16T/hari) jadi safe-haven domestik.
Asset yield naik + yield dividen 10.4% kasih bantalan total return; ROE 18%, likuiditas tinggi, funding domestik kuat menahan dampak outflow asing.
Wholesale-tilt tapi neraca besar dan ROE 19%; diuntungkan kupon SBN tinggi di portofolio obligasi, masuk 4 saham terlikuid.
Satu-satunya properti yang layak dipegang di skenario ini: yield dividen 43.9% dan model kawasan industri low-leverage, jauh lebih tahan dari developer ritel.
AVOID/underweight: developer leverage tinggi, demand KPR hancur saat BI rate naik; P/B 0.55 murah tapi value trap selama rate higher-for-longer.
AVOID/underweight: P/B 0.37 cermin tekanan rate-sensitive; beban bunga naik + affordability turun menekan presales dan arus kas.

answers.risk: Thesis batal jika kenaikan yield ternyata sementara dan BI cepat pivot dovish (IDR stabil) — maka properti rate-sensitive (CTRA, BSDE, SMRA) yang oversold di P/B <0.6 justru rebound paling kencang sebagai high-beta recovery, dan bank ketinggalan. Risiko kedua khusus bank: defend IDR yang agresif memicu foreign outflow tajam dari saham keuangan (kepemilikan asing tinggi) dan kenaikan NPL kalau rate tinggi terlalu lama memukul borrower — menekan harga BBRI/BMRI walau NIM membaik. Pantau arah BI7DRR, spread SBN 10y, dan trajektori USD/IDR sebagai sinyal invalidasi.

answers.reasoning: Saat rupiah melemah, transmisinya dua arah: emiten dengan utang USD besar tapi pendapatan IDR (properti high-leverage, sebagian telco/utilitas) kena rugi kurs non-tunai dan beban bunga riil naik mengikuti BI rate yang BI tahan tinggi untuk menjaga spread carry vs Fed dan menahan outflow asing dari SBN/saham; sebaliknya eksportir komoditas (batu bara, nikel/emas, CPO) justru diuntungkan karena harga jual di-USD sementara biaya gaji, royalti, dan solar sebagian besar IDR — ini natural hedge struktural yang jauh lebih kuat dan murah daripada kontrak forward apa pun. Kualitas hedging dari laporan keuangan tidak dinilai dari ada-tidaknya forward, tapi dari: (1) currency match — porsi pendapatan/aset USD vs utang USD di catatan "risiko mata uang asing" (sensitivity analysis 10% IDR move biasanya wajib diungkap), (2) sifat hedge — natural/operasional vs derivatif jangka pendek yang harus terus di-roll, (3) hedge accounting (cash-flow hedge yang lulus efektivitas masuk OCI, bukan bolak-balik di laba), dan (4) DSCR/interest coverage yang tetap aman pasca-devaluasi. Yang paling solid adalah emiten yang fungsional currency-nya memang USD atau pendapatannya USD sehingga utang USD-nya self-hedged secara ekonomis, bukan yang menambal mismatch dengan derivatif. CATATAN DATA: tabel fundamentals di server ini hanya memuat market_cap/EV/shares/free_float/dividend dan tidak punya field FX-debt, derivatif, atau catatan laporan keuangan; jadi pick di bawah dipilih dari struktur bisnis natural-hedge yang terverifikasi via company_background, bukan dari pembacaan langsung disclosure hedging.
tool_queries: get_company('ADRO') / get_company('PTBA') / get_company('AMMN') -- baca company_background untuk konfirmasi profil eksportir USD-revenue (natural hedge) vs pemain domestik IDR

run_query: SELECT f.ticker, c.sector, c.sub_sector, f.market_cap, f.enterprise_value, f.free_float_pct FROM fundamentals f JOIN companies c ON f.ticker=c.ticker WHERE c.sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND f.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','ITMG','BYAN','ANTM','AMMN','INCO','TINS','TAPG','AALI') ORDER BY f.market_cap DESC -- shortlist eksportir komoditas berkapitalisasi likuid

picks.rationale: Eksportir batu bara dengan harga jual USD dan biaya tambang/royalti IDR; neraca nyaris tanpa utang USD bersih, jadi natural hedge paling bersih plus yield ~10.7% yang menandakan arus kas USD kuat dan tak butuh derivatif.

Pemasok batu bara terintegrasi, secara historis net cash dalam USD dan fungsional currency USD; mismatch FX-nya mendekati nol sehingga rupiah melemah justru menaikkan laba dalam IDR — hedge struktural, bukan kontraktual.

Energi terintegrasi vertikal dengan pendapatan USD; meski leverage lebih besar dari peers, utang USD-nya match dengan revenue USD (self-hedged), dan yield ~12.5% menandakan ketahanan arus kas saat IDR gonjang-ganjing.
Eksportir tembaga/emas dengan penjualan USD penuh; aset dan pendanaan proyek smelter umumnya berbasis USD sehingga currency match-nya kuat — risiko di sini eksekusi capex, bukan FX translasi.
Produsen CPO dengan harga acuan USD (CIF Rotterdam/MDEX) dan biaya kebun IDR; natural hedge khas sawit, ROE ~34% dan yield ~8.9% menunjukkan margin tahan saat rupiah melemah.

Eksportir nikel/emas dengan komponen pendapatan USD dan likuiditas sangat tinggi; emas berfungsi sebagai hedge ganda terhadap pelemahan rupiah, memberi bantalan FX paling fleksibel di antara nama logam.

answers.risk: Tesis ini bertumpu pada natural hedge (pendapatan USD), BUKAN pada bukti langsung kebijakan hedging karena dataset tidak punya field FX-debt/derivatif/catatan laporan keuangan — jadi nilai hedging sesungguhnya harus diverifikasi manual di laporan tahunan (porsi utang USD, sensitivity analysis 10% IDR, status hedge accounting). Tesis batal jika: (a) harga komoditas (coal/nickel/CPO) jatuh sehingga revenue USD ikut anjlok dan natural hedge berbalik jadi beban operasi tinggi, (b) emiten ternyata punya utang USD jauh melebihi arus kas USD-nya tanpa lindung (mismatch tersembunyi), atau (c) rupiah justru menguat (BI rate turun + inflow asing balik) sehingga eksportir kehilangan tailwind kurs sementara importir/properti yang underweight di sini malah diuntungkan.

answers.reasoning: In an IDR-to-18,000 + downgrade tail, the transmission is brutal for IDX financials: BI is forced to defend the rupiah with rate hikes (or burns reserves), foreign holders of IDR bonds and bank equity sell on the rating cut, and BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/BBNI — which are the bulk of index weight, liquidity and foreign ownership — get hit on both NIM compression risk and forced foreign outflow, so "buy the cheap bank" is a value trap here (see PNBN -39, BANK -35 already). The cleanest pure defensives are companies whose cash flows are IDR-domestic, non-discretionary, low-leverage and dividend-funded from operating cash, not credit cycles: consumer staples (SIDO, ICBP/INDF) and a regulated/dividend telco-infra (TLKM), because demand for jamu, instant noodles and data is price-inelastic and these names carry low net debt so a rate spike doesn't gut them. The sharper insight is that the same shock is a tailwind for USD-revenue exporters: a weaker IDR inflates the rupiah value of coal/CPO/gold receipts, so high-yield coal dividend machines (PTBA, ADRO) and gold (ANTM, which doubles as a domestic safe-haven proxy plus USD-linked bullion) act as a natural FX hedge that pays you to wait. Net: pure equity defensives blunt the drawdown but do not hedge the currency; a barbell of staples/telco for stability plus exporter-dividend/gold for the FX upside beats both "cheap banks" and a naked cash position, while physical-gold-via-miner (ANTM) is the most bijak equity substitute for holding emas because it gives bullion exposure with a listed dividend and liquidity (496 rp bn/day) you can exit fast.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the shock is short-lived and BI defends successfully (sharp IDR snap-back + V-shaped foreign re-entry): then the de-rated banks/cyclicals you avoided rip while staples and gold give back the FX premium, and you underperform. Specific landmines: a global coal/CPO price collapse would kill the exporter-dividend hedge (PTBA/ADRO yields are unsustainable if thermal coal craters), commodity windfall/export-DMO or dividend-tax changes can claw back the FX gain, and ANTM gold can fall in nominal terms if the Fed stays higher-for-longer and real yields rise even as IDR weakens. yf yields here are directional only — verify payout coverage from operating cash before sizing, especially PTBA/ADRO at 10%+.

answers.reasoning: Belanja fiskal jor-joran (MBG ~Rp71tn skala penuh) plus defisit melebar memaksa pembiayaan lewat SBN dan tekanan ke rupiah, sehingga BI cenderung hold/hawkish demi menjaga yield spread dan stabilitas kurs — ini menahan ruang pemangkasan bunga yang dibutuhkan sektor leverage tinggi. IDR lemah justru menaikkan revenue rupiah eksportir komoditas (batu bara, sawit, nikel/emas) yang menjual dalam USD dengan biaya rupiah, sekaligus menebalkan dividen kas mereka. MBG menyalurkan daya beli langsung ke rantai pangan pokok — beras, susu, telur, ayam, minyak goreng — yang menguntungkan consumer staples dan terutama produsen CPO/sawit yang jadi input minyak goreng. Sebaliknya defisit + bunga tinggi + kurs lemah memukul properti dan tech/growth (utang besar, sensitif bunga, valuasi berbasis durasi) serta menekan bank lewat biaya dana dan risiko crowding-out, walau dividen bank besar tetap jadi bantalan. Foreign flow cenderung keluar dari nama rate-sensitive ke high-yield defensive/komoditas saat fiscal risk premium naik.

Risiko spesifik: MBG bisa dieksekusi lewat impor atau margin produsen pangan tergerus subsidi/kontrol harga; defisit memburuk bisa picu downgrade rating dan sell-off broad-market yang menyeret semua nama termasuk high-yielder. Yield batu bara juga jebakan kalau payout dipotong saat earnings turun.

answers.reasoning: Memotong subsidi BBM dan naikkan Pertalite adalah cost-push shock yang regresif: ia langsung menggerus real disposable income kelas bawah-menengah, persis basis volume ICBP/MYOR/AMRT, sambil mengangkat inflasi headline lewat jalur transport dan pangan. Inflasi yang lebih tinggi menahan ruang BI menurunkan rate (bahkan bias hold/hike untuk jaga IDR dan spread riil terhadap Fed), sehingga IDR rentan dan sektor rate-sensitive (properti high-debt, tech) tertekan. Ruang fiskal yang dibebaskan dialihkan ke capex IKN, bukan ke bansos konsumtif, jadi multiplier ke daya beli ritel lambat dan emiten consumer discretionary yang sudah terkoreksi (ICBP -29%, AMRT -29%) belum tentu rebound dekat. Transportasi/logistik kena dua sisi: biaya solar naik menekan margin operator, tapi operator jalan tol seperti JSMR relatif terlindung karena tarif terindeks inflasi dan trafik logistik inelastis. Posisi optimal: rotasi dari staples volume-elastis ke kombinasi defensive high-yield berkualitas (telco/infra terindeks, batubara dividen sebagai hedge inflasi/penerima belanja energi) plus emiten konsumsi yang pricing power dan yield-nya kuat (SIDO/MLBI), karena fiscal-discipline IDR-supportive justru menguntungkan dividend payers ketimbang growth.
