Defensive infra, yield 6.8%, demand data inelastis; ARPU relatif tahan terhadap shock BBM dan diuntungkan rotasi ke dividend payers.
Underweight/avoid: -29% 1y, volume mie/staples paling terekspos kompresi real income kelas bawah; baru menarik kalau valuasi sudah harga skenario ini.

answers.risk: Tesis batal jika pemerintah memasangkan pemotongan subsidi dengan bansos tunai/BLT besar yang menetralkan kompresi daya beli (multiplier konsumsi pulih cepat), atau jika harga minyak global anjlok sehingga kenaikan harga BBM tidak terjadi/kecil dan inflasi jinak — skenario itu membuka ruang BI cut rate, membalik rotasi ke arah konsumsi volume (ICBP/MYOR/AMRT), properti, dan tech yang justru saya hindari. Risiko kedua: koreksi harga batubara global akan menggerus tesis dividen-hedge (ADRO/PTBA) terlepas dari fiskal domestik.

answers.category: Sector rotation / commodity-cycle positioning (Indonesia nickel downstreaming)
answers.reasoning: Kuncinya: data prices sampai 2026-04-02 menunjukkan ANTM/INCO/MBMA SUDAH naik 130-143% trailing-1y JUSTRU saat LME nikel anjlok — artinya rally itu bukan didorong harga nikel, tapi oleh emas (ANTM), ramp volume + integrasi hilir HPAL (MBMA/NCKL), dan story idiosinkratik (MDKA). Jadi pertanyaan "masih masuk akal hold?" bukan soal siklus komoditas murni, tapi soal apakah premium kebijakan hilirisasi sudah terlanjur priced-in; return 3-bulan yang mulai melemah dan menyebar (BRMS -38%, NICL -39%, NCKL -4% vs ANTM +13%) sinyal kepemimpinan menyempit dan momentum mendingin. Transmisi makro Indonesia: oversupply Indonesia sendiri (RKAB longgar + smelter HPAL Tiongkok) yang menekan harga LME → margin saprolite/NPI tergerus, jadi yang bertahan justru pemain berbiaya-rendah dan terintegrasi nikel-class-1/MHP (NCKL/MBMA), bukan tambang bijih murni; sementara IDR lemah + suku bunga BI yang turun bertahap memberi tailwind translasi (revenue USD, biaya IDR) yang menutupi sebagian penurunan harga. Risiko kebijakan = pedang bermata dua: larangan ekspor bijih + DHE 100%/12 bulan dan rencana pajak/royalti progresif bisa membalik thesis kapan saja, dan ANTM tetap proxy aliran asing paling likuid (ADV Rp805 bn/hari YTD) sehingga paling sensitif ke risk-off. Cara baca: pisahkan beta-komoditas (LME, bisa di-trade) dari opsi-kebijakan (struktural, tak bisa di-time) — hold yang punya integrasi + neraca kuat, kurangi yang murni leverage harga bijih.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Barang Baku', sub_sector LIKE '%Metal%' OR sub_sector LIKE '%Mining%') lalu filter ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA','BRMS','TINS','NICL') untuk mengunci universe nikel/logam yang real

run_query: WITH p AS (SELECT ticker,date,close FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA','BRMS')), latest AS (SELECT ticker,last(close ORDER BY date) px_now,max(date) d FROM p GROUP BY 1) SELECT l.ticker, round(100*(l.px_now/(SELECT close FROM p WHERE p.ticker=l.ticker AND date<=l.d-INTERVAL 12 MONTH ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 1)-100),1) ret_1y, round(100*(l.px_now/(SELECT close FROM p WHERE p.ticker=l.ticker AND date<=l.d-INTERVAL 3 MONTH ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 1)-100),1) ret_3m FROM latest l ORDER BY ret_1y — buktikan rally sudah terjadi & momentum 3m melemah

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, round(s.market_cap/1e12,1) mcap_tn, s.recommendation_key, a.rec_strong_buy, a.rec_buy, a.rec_hold, a.rec_sell, a.target_mean, round(s.price_to_book,2) pb FROM summary s LEFT JOIN analyst a USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA') ORDER BY mcap_tn DESC — sandarkan ke konsensus + upside-to-target, ABAIKAN roe/pe/pb yf (scrambled: roe 2302%, pb 63513)

run_query: SELECT ticker, round(avg(close*volume)/1e9,0) adv_rp_bn FROM prices WHERE date>=DATE '2026-01-02' AND ticker IN ('ANTM','INCO','MBMA','NCKL','MDKA','BRMS') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC — peta likuiditas/eksposur aliran asing (ANTM Rp805bn/hari = proxy risk-off paling sensitif)

picks.rationale: Pick utama untuk hold: Harita, integrasi penuh saprolite→MHP/nikel kelas-1 (biaya rendah, paling tahan LME anjlok), strong_buy dengan 14 buy/strong-buy & target Rp1.570 vs Rp1.120, dan ret_3m -4% = belum overheated seperti peers.

Hold tipis: Merdeka Battery, leverage langsung ke ramp HPAL/baterai; ret_3m masih +18% dan 6 analyst buy, tapi target mean Rp799 cuma ~9% di atas Rp730 — momentum oke, upside konsensus tipis, ukuran posisi kecil.

Hold sebagai proxy EMAS + likuiditas asing (ADV Rp805bn/hari, paling likuid di set), bukan murni nikel; konsensus buy, target Rp4.545 vs Rp3.640 (~+25%) — tapi justru paling sensitif risk-off, jadi trim kalau aliran asing keluar.

Hold spekulatif berbasis story emas-tembaga (Tujuh Bukit/Pani), bukan bijih nikel; satu-satunya strong_buy dengan 16 buy/strong-buy & target Rp3.624 vs Rp3.260, tapi net margin negatif → kualitas earnings lemah, treat sebagai opsi, bukan core.

Tunggu/underweight: Vale, masih dominan bijih + smelter NPI tradisional yang paling kena tekanan oversupply LME; sudah naik +139% 1y, ret_3m cuma +6% (mendatar), 4 hold di konsensus = paling sedikit katalis baru — bukan entry yang menarik di sini.

answers.risk: Thesis bisa batal dua arah. (1) Risiko kebijakan negatif yang tak bisa di-time: kenaikan royalti/pajak progresif IUP nikel, pengetatan RKAB yang malah menaikkan harga bijih domestik (margin smelter terjepit), atau pelonggaran DHE 100% — semua bisa membalik premium hilirisasi dalam semalam. (2) Karena semua nama SUDAH naik 130-143% trailing-1y, ini bukan entry komoditas-murah lagi melainkan momentum yang mulai mendingin (ret_3m menyebar, BRMS/NICL -38%/-39%); jika LME nikel terus turun + oversupply HPAL Tiongkok berlanjut DAN harga emas balik turun (penopang ANTM/MDKA), katalis hilang dan mean-reversion tajam mungkin terjadi. Pembatal cepat: aliran asing keluar dari ANTM (likuiditas tertinggi) sebagai sinyal risk-off sektor. Catatan data: rasio yf (ROE/PE/PB) untuk nama-nama ini tidak reliabel (ROE 2302%, PB 63513) — keputusan disandarkan ke price action, likuiditas, dan konsensus analis, bukan valuasi yf.

answers.category: thematic / policy-driven (hilirisasi expansion: bauxite ban + raw-copper export stop)
answers.reasoning: Hilirisasi adalah kebijakan fiskal-industri unggulan: pelarangan ekspor bahan mentah memaksa value-add domestik, menaikkan penerimaan pajak/royalti dan menekan defisit transaksi berjalan—yang struktural bullish IDR dan memberi BI ruang menahan/menurunkan rate, jalur transmisi yang sudah terbukti di nikel (ANTM/AMMN/MDKA) sejak ban 2020. Untuk bauksit, pemenang struktural adalah pemilik integrasi tambang-ke-alumina/aluminium (ANTM via SGAR Mempawah, dan grup energi Adaro yang membangun smelter aluminium Kaltara), karena merekalah yang menangkap margin hilir alih-alih hanya jual ore. Untuk tembaga, AMMN sudah punya smelter Sumbawa yang beroperasi sehingga justru diuntungkan oleh larangan ekspor konsentrat saingannya, menjadikannya beta kebijakan paling murni di brief. Smelter butuh listrik captive masif sehingga konglomerat energi/batubara (CUAN/Prajogo, ADRO) menangkap demand sekunder via power dan feedstock, sementara UNTR menangkap belanja kontraktor tambang—tema ini juga menarik foreign flow yang selama ini terkonsentrasi di bank (BBCA/BBRI), memberi rotasi sektoral ke Barang Baku/Energi yang trailing-1y-nya (69/120) masih tertinggal heavyweight.
tool_queries: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn, l.return_on_equity, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l LEFT JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','ADRO','AADI','CUAN','TPIA','BRPT','UNTR','MDKA','NICL','BRMS') ORDER BY l.market_cap DESC;

SELECT ticker, sector, return_on_equity, price_to_book, dividend_yield, market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Barang Baku','Energi','Perindustrian') AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY return_on_equity DESC LIMIT 25;

SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_30d FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','ADRO','CUAN','UNTR','TPIA') AND date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30 GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign_30d DESC;

SELECT ticker, COUNT(*) n, MAX(date) last FROM announcements WHERE source='announcement' AND (lower(title) LIKE '%smelter%' OR lower(title) LIKE '%hilirisasi%' OR lower(title) LIKE '%bauksit%' OR lower(title) LIKE '%alumina%' OR lower(title) LIKE '%tembaga%' OR lower(title) LIKE '%aluminium%') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 20;

picks.rationale: BUMN tambang dengan integrasi bauksit-ke-alumina (SGAR Mempawah JV Inalum) plus emas/nikel; paling langsung menangkap value-add jika ban bauksit diperluas, dan likuiditasnya tinggi (496rp bn/hari) untuk menyerap foreign flow.

Smelter tembaga Sumbawa sudah operasional, jadi larangan ekspor konsentrat justru memberinya keunggulan struktural vs pesaing yang belum punya smelter—beta kebijakan tembaga paling murni; ROE rendah (5%) = ruang re-rating saat smelter ramp-up.
Grup energi membangun smelter aluminium Kaltara berbasis hydropower/captive; pivot dari batubara ke hilir aluminium memberi opsi pertumbuhan sekaligus tetap bayar yield 12.5% sebagai bantalan.
Konglomerat energi Prajogo yang memasok listrik/feedstock ke kawasan industri smelter (orbit Barito/Petrindo); demand sekunder dari ekspansi hilirisasi, ROE 26% dan termasuk paling likuid (308rp bn).

Beneficiary belanja modal hilir via jasa kontraktor tambang dan distribusi alat berat untuk pembangunan smelter/tambang; valuasi defensif, yield 6.7% dan ROE 15% membatasi downside bila timeline molor.

answers.risk: Tesis batal jika smelter belum siap saat ban berlaku: kapasitas hilir bauksit/tembaga Indonesia masih tertinggal jadwal, sehingga larangan ekspor mentah malah memotong volume penjualan dan arus kas miner (ore menumpuk tanpa offtake) sebelum margin hilir mengalir—persis risiko yang membebani ANTM pasca-ban nikel. Pemicu lain: relaksasi/penundaan ban (seperti yang terjadi berulang untuk konsentrat tembaga demi penerimaan jangka pendek), CapEx smelter membengkak menekan ROE AMMN/ADRO, koreksi harga komoditas global (aluminium/tembaga/LME) yang menghapus ekonomi proyek, dan suku bunga tinggi/USD kuat yang menaikkan beban utang proyek smelter padat-modal. Foreign flow juga bisa tetap mengendap di bank (BBCA/BBRI) alih-alih rotasi ke Barang Baku jika eksekusi kebijakan tampak tidak kredibel.

answers.category: IKN construction/cement thematic - realistic return assessment
answers.reasoning: IKN capex is APBN-funded and front-loaded, but the fiscal reality is the transmission killer: a 2025-26 deficit pressed toward the 3% ceiling plus pricier IDR-funded debt means the state is the marginal buyer of karya BUMN receivables, and it pays slow. WIKA/PTPP/ADHI carry net gearing well above 2x with project working-capital that doesn't convert to cash, so even full IKN order books translate into thin, dilution-prone equity returns (rights issues, debt restructuring, PMN dependence) rather than ROE. The data brief confirms the market already prices this: not one karya name appears in any heavyweight, liquidity, ROE, or yield screen, while the only IKN-adjacent name that does surface is SMGR at a distressed 0.40x P/B - cheap because national cement utilization sits near 55-60% with structural oversupply, so IKN volume is a rounding error against the demand gap. With BI holding rates high to defend the rupiah and foreign flows concentrating in banks (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI dominate liquidity), the durable IKN exposure is not the leveraged contractors but the cash-generative, dividend-paying infra/materials adjacencies that win regardless of who funds the next tower. So: realistic returns on the karya/cement complex are poor on a risk-adjusted basis; play the theme through balance-sheet quality, not the contractors directly.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, pb, roe, net_debt_to_equity, current_ratio FROM fundamentals JOIN summary USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('WIKA','PTPP','ADHI','INTP','SMGR') ORDER BY net_debt_to_equity DESC

SELECT ticker, trailing_1y_return, max_drawdown_1y, avg_daily_value_bn FROM returns JOIN latest USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('WIKA','PTPP','ADHI','INTP','SMGR','JSMR','UNTR','ASII') ORDER BY trailing_1y_return
screen_stocks(sector IN ['Infrastruktur','Barang Baku','Perindustrian'], mcap_tn > 5, net_debt_to_equity < 1.0, dividend_yield > 4, roe > 12, sort='dividend_yield desc')

SELECT ticker, SUM(net_value_bn) AS foreign_net_30d FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('WIKA','PTPP','ADHI','INTP','SMGR','JSMR','UNTR') AND date >= date('now','-30 day') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY foreign_net_30d

picks.rationale: Only IKN-adjacent name in the data (cheapest big-cap P/B 0.40); deep-value optionality on cement demand recovery, but cheap for a reason - oversupply caps upside, so a contrarian re-rating bet not a growth bet.
Infra at 0.62x P/B with toll-road cash flows tied to IKN connectivity corridors; regulated tariff escalation gives real IKN exposure without karya-style balance-sheet fragility.
Perindustrian, ROE 15, yield 6.7, fortress balance sheet; heavy-equipment and construction-machinery proxy that captures national capex/IKN earthworks while paying you to wait.
Perindustrian heavyweight, ROE 14, yield 6.7; diversified infra/heavy-equipment (via UNTR) and infra-services exposure with cash flow that survives a slow APBN, far safer than direct contractors.
Infra/energy, yield 9.9; IKN utilities/gas-distribution buildout beneficiary with dividend support - infra theme paid in cash, not receivables.
Infrastruktur, ROE 18, yield 6.8; IKN digital/smart-city backbone is contracted, recurring, and self-funded - cleanest way to own 'new capital buildout' without BUMN karya leverage.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if (a) the government issues large PMN/equity injections or a sovereign-backed receivables-securitization that recapitalizes WIKA/PTPP/ADHI - then the deep-leverage discount becomes a violent re-rating and the karya names outrun the safe proxies; or (b) BI cuts aggressively and IDR stabilizes, reviving rate-sensitive, high-debt construction and lifting cement utilization. Conversely, if APBN consolidation forces IKN to slip and private capital stays on the sidelines, even SMGR/JSMR see volume disappointment. Key invalidation signal: a credible private-capital commitment or a PMN announcement for the karya BUMN - watch the announcements table and foreign broker_net flows into the contractors.

answers.reasoning: Pisahkan dulu: "PMN" ke Himbara dan ke BUMN karya itu dua hal berlawanan. BBRI/BMRI/BBNI tidak butuh suntikan modal — CAR mereka ~20%+, ROE 18-19% (lihat brief), justru mereka net penyetor dividen ke APBN; transmisi negatifnya bukan dilusi tapi (a) tekanan jadi agen penyalur kredit program/Danantara dengan margin tipis & risiko NPL naik, dan (b) payout dipaksa tinggi untuk nambal fiskal. Itu sebabnya BBRI yield-nya 10.4% dan BBNI 9.4% sementara BBCA cuma 5.1% — pasar sudah pricing in "BUMN tax" via diskon valuasi (BBRI/BMRI P/B jauh di bawah BBCA, BBTN cuma 0.49x). Untuk BUMN karya kebalikannya: PMN/rights issue itu rescue equity ke neraca yang arus kasnya negatif dan utangnya menumpuk pasca proyek IKN/infra — itu dilutif murni, bukan katalis. Makro pendukung: jika BI mulai memangkas rate dan IDR stabil, beban bunga karya turun & properti/infra rate-sensitif lega, tapi bank malah kehilangan NIM — jadi rate cut sekalipun tidak menyelamatkan tesis karya tanpa restrukturisasi utang, dan asing (yang pegang mayoritas free-float bank besar) akan kabur duluan begitu sinyal directed-lending muncul, menekan BBRI/BMRI yang termasuk paling likuid (909 & 847 rp bn/hari).
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, market_cap, return_on_equity, dividend_yield, price_to_book FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','BBTN','BBCA','BJBR','BJTM') ORDER BY market_cap DESC; -- bandingkan diskon valuasi Himbara vs BBCA & cek mana yang sudah price-in 'BUMN tax'

screen_stocks(filters=[{field:'sector',op:'=',value:'Keuangan'},{field:'price_to_book',op:'<',value:0.6},{field:'market_cap',op:'>',value:5e12}], sort={field:'price_to_book',dir:'asc'}) -- temukan bank pelat merah termurah by P/B (BBTN, bank daerah) yang paling rentan PMN/konsolidasi

SELECT ticker, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','BBTN') AND date >= current_date - INTERVAL 90 DAY GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign; -- ukur apakah asing sudah net jual Himbara (proxy sentimen directed-lending)

SELECT c.ticker, c.sub_sector, r.ret_1y, l.price_to_book FROM companies c JOIN returns r USING(ticker) JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE c.sub_sector ILIKE '%construction%' OR c.ticker IN ('WIKA','PTPP','ADHI','WSKT','WTON') ORDER BY r.ret_1y; -- isolasi BUMN karya & lihat kinerja/leverage sebelum nilai risiko dilusi rights issue

picks.rationale: Inti Himbara, ROE 18% & yield 10.4% — bukan target dilusi (overkapital), tapi paling terekspos jadi corong KUR/kredit program & ekstraksi dividen; beli kelemahan hanya bila valuasi sudah diskon dalam vs BBCA.

ROE 19%, yield cuma 2.2% & paling likuid (847 rp bn) — wholesale-tilt bikin dia paling rentan jadi pendana proyek BUMN/Danantara berisiko; asing kabur duluan di sini saat sinyal directed-lending muncul.
Bank Himbara paling 'murah' (P/B rendah, yield 9.4%, ROE 12% terendah di antara big-4) — bantalan dilusi kecil tapi paling rapuh kalau NPL korporasi BUMN naik.
P/B 0.49 dan kapitalisasi kecil — justru DI SINI risiko PMN/konsolidasi (mis. merger bank syariah/perumahan) nyata; suntikan modal di bank tipis ekuitas = dilusi, hindari sampai struktur jelas.
Hedge/relative value: bank swasta, ROE 21% tertinggi & yield 5.1% tanpa 'BUMN tax' — overweight vs Himbara kalau tesis directed-lending/payout-grab terkonfirmasi.

Proksi terbaik BUMN-karya-style di brief: properti/infra ROE cuma 7%, yield ~0% — kelas aset rights-issue/PMN dilutif berbasis proyek (IKN); hindari, kalaupun rate cut datang tetap tak cukup tanpa restrukturisasi utang.

answers.risk: Tesis batal kalau PMN ke Himbara ternyata bukan rights issue baru melainkan reinvested earnings/sub-debt (tidak dilutif) DAN BI mulai siklus pemangkasan rate dengan IDR stabil + asing balik (cek query net_foreign berbalik positif) — skenario itu mengubah BBRI/BMRI jadi katalis (NIM kompresi tertutup volume kredit, valuasi rerate dari diskon). Sebaliknya, untuk karya/PANI risiko terbesar adalah PMN jadi backstop pemerintah yang menjamin utang (jaminan negara) sehingga rebound spekulatif jangka pendek terjadi meski fundamental tetap dilutif — jangan short ke berita PMN, fade ke rights-issue announcement. Pemicu utama yang harus dipantau: pengumuman rights issue/HMETD (announcements table) dan perubahan kebijakan dividen payout BUMN.

answers.category: post-election sector rotation (telco / media / consumer laggard mean-reversion)
answers.reasoning: The election ad-spend impulse is a one-off P&L event that peaks during the campaign and fades in the 2-3 quarters after voting, so the *leaders* that priced it in have largely spent the move: Barang Konsumen Non-Primer (discretionary/retail/media) is already the #2 sector at +124% trailing-1y, meaning the obvious ad-beneficiary trade is crowded, not fresh. The durable post-election macro is different from the campaign sugar-high — BI's easing path plus a softer IDR lifts real disposable income and re-rates rate-sensitive consumer and infra names, while the new administration's fiscal program (food/social spending, subsidy reshuffles) shifts the tailwind from *advertising* toward *staples volume*. That points the alpha at beaten-down staples laggards (ICBP, AMRT) where the de-rating already happened and a consumption-stimulus fiscal stance is the catalyst, rather than at the media names that ran during the campaign. Telco (TLKM) is the cleanest residual play: it captured data/ad load during the cycle but trades as a 6.8% yield, 18% ROE defensive that benefits from BI cuts via duration and from foreign flows rotating out of expensive banks. The honest read: the media "efek pemilu" window is mostly closed, but a staples + telco laggard book still has room because their weakness is mean-reversion, not a spent catalyst.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, ret_1y, pb, div_yield FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Barang Konsumen Non-Primer','Infrastruktur') AND mcap_tn > 5 ORDER BY ret_1y ASC LIMIT 25  -- isolate the consumer/telco laggards vs leaders

SELECT r.ticker, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m, r.ret_1m FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE l.ticker IN ('SCMA','EMTK','TLKM','ICBP','INDF','AMRT','MYOR','UNVR','SIDO','GGRM') ORDER BY r.ret_3m DESC  -- check if recent 1m/3m momentum is turning up for laggards vs rolling over for media winners

SELECT bn.ticker, SUM(bn.net_value) AS net_foreign_proxy FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.ticker IN ('TLKM','SCMA','ICBP','AMRT','GGRM','INDF') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC  -- confirm flows are rotating INTO the laggards, not out

SELECT ticker, fwd_pe, roe, div_yield, ev_ebitda FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('TLKM','ICBP','INDF','AMRT','GGRM','SCMA','SIDO') ORDER BY div_yield DESC  -- valuation floor: which laggards pay you to wait

picks.rationale: Staples bellwether sold off to -29% trailing-1y (a top laggard) despite Indofood's volume franchise — classic mean-reversion target as post-election fiscal consumption stimulus lifts staples volume rather than ad budgets.

Alfamart down -29%, the other big staples laggard; minimart traffic is the cleanest read on broad consumer recovery and benefits from social-spending fiscal transfers, not the spent campaign ad cycle.

Telco residual play, not a laggard but de-rated; 6.8% yield + 18% ROE defensive that re-rates on BI cuts (duration) and absorbs foreign rotation out of pricey banks — the durable post-election holding.

Pure media/ad election-beneficiary at 10.3% yield — include it small, but acknowledge the ad-spend impulse peaked during the campaign; this is the name where the 'efek pemilu' window is most spent, held only for the dividend floor.

Cigarette laggard at 0.44x P/B — deep-value rokok proxy; excise (cukai) policy clarity post-election removes the regulatory overhang, and a cheap multiple gives asymmetric upside if tariff hikes moderate.
Sido Muncul, 37% ROE herbal/consumer-health staple — defensive consumption with rural exposure that tracks the fiscal-transfer-driven demand recovery rather than urban ad spend.

answers.risk: The thesis hinges on the post-election macro pivoting from ad-spend to staples-volume via BI easing + consumption-friendly fiscal policy. It breaks if (1) the new administration's fiscal stance turns toward austerity / subsidy cuts that squeeze real disposable income, or (2) BI is forced to hold/hike to defend a weak IDR (sticky USD rates, commodity-driven CAD), which kills the rate-sensitive re-rating. Regulatory shocks are the second-order risk: a sharper-than-expected cukai (cigarette excise) hike guts GGRM, and any minimum-purchase/ad-restriction moves hit ICBP/AMRT margins. Finally, if the staples laggards are de-rating on company-specific earnings deterioration (not just sentiment), there is no mean-reversion to harvest — verify the 3m/flow turn in the queries before sizing up.

answers.reasoning: Transmission runs fiscal -> SBN -> bank funding -> equities: populist spending pushes the debt-to-GDP ratio up, a negative outlook from Moody's/S&P/Fitch forces foreign holders of SBN (still ~14-15% of govt bonds) to demand term premium, so the 10y SBN yield steepens and IDR weakens, which boxes BI into holding or hiking the BI rate to defend the rupiah and the carry. Higher SBN yields raise banks' cost of fund and mark-to-market losses on their large AFS bond books, compressing NIM for deposit-franchise-light names while BBCA's cheap CASA and BBRI/BMRI scale cushion them better than mid/small banks. Rate-sensitive, leverage-heavy sectors get hit hardest: property (BSDE, CTRA, SMRA) on mortgage demand and refinancing, and long-duration tech/growth (GOTO, DCII) on discount-rate de-rating. The genuine defensives are USD revenue earners whose cash flows actually benefit from a weaker IDR plus a high SBN yield floor on valuation -- coal/CPO exporters with double-digit dividend yields (PTBA, ADRO, TAPG) and low-beta consumer staples with pricing power (SIDO, UNVR) -- because their earnings are decoupled from domestic cost of fund and their dividend yields stay competitive even as the risk-free rate rises.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, dividend_yield, return_on_equity, market_cap, beta FROM latest WHERE sector='Energi' AND dividend_yield > 9 ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC -- coal/CPO USD-earner dividend defensives that clear a rising SBN yield hurdle

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.price_to_book, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector='Keuangan' AND l.market_cap > 100e12 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC -- rank big banks by funding-cost resilience proxy (yield/PB) vs the cohort already down (PNBN, BANK -35/-39%)

SELECT ticker, sector, beta, dividend_yield, return_on_equity FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Teknologi') AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY beta DESC -- isolate the rate-sensitive, high-beta names most exposed to a cost-of-fund spike

SELECT ticker, sector, beta, return_on_equity, dividend_yield, profit_margins FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Kesehatan') AND return_on_equity > 30 ORDER BY beta ASC -- low-beta staples with pricing power as the domestic defensive sleeve

picks.rationale: Highest-quality bank to overweight if cost of fund rises: dominant low-cost CASA franchise (ROE 21%) absorbs a higher SBN/BI rate far better than mid-tier banks whose funding reprices fast.
Defensive USD coal earner, ~10.7% yield -- cash flows decoupled from domestic cost of fund, benefits from weaker IDR, and the fat dividend stays competitive even as the risk-free SBN yield climbs.
Same defensive logic, ~12.5% yield and one of the most liquid energy dividend plays; export revenue hedges the IDR leg of the downgrade scenario.
CPO exporter, ROE 34% and ~8.9% yield -- weak-IDR beneficiary with hard-currency revenue, a non-bank defensive uncorrelated to SBN-driven funding stress.
Low-beta consumer/health staple, ROE 37%, debt-light with pricing power -- earnings insulated from rate transmission, the classic domestic defensive sleeve.
Short/avoid: property at 0.37 P/B looks cheap but is the textbook casualty -- rising SBN yields lift mortgage rates and refinancing cost, hitting demand and the leveraged balance sheet.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the downgrade/yield-spike never materializes -- i.e. the populist spending is funded credibly (front-loaded SBN, BI 'burden sharing', or a commodity windfall) and the 3% deficit cap holds, in which case SBN yields stay anchored, BI can cut, and the trade inverts: rate-sensitive banks and property (BSDE, CTRA) rerate hardest while the coal dividend defensives lag on a softer global coal price. Second-order risk: the coal/CPO 'defensives' carry their own commodity-cycle beta (BYAN already -46% trailing 1y), so a simultaneous coal price rollover would sink the hedge even if the fiscal call is right.

answers.reasoning: TKDN bites through the procurement channel: APBN/BUMN capex and the migration to e-Katalog favor vendors that can document local value-add, so the real winners are firms with sunk domestic capacity + a government/SOE demand pipe, not anyone with a "lokal" label. Macro tailwind is aligned — Perindustrian leads the tape (+128% avg 1y), BI's easing path lowers the cost of the capex needed to localize, and a soft IDR makes imports dearer, which is exactly the relative-price gap TKDN tariffs/thresholds widen in favor of domestic assemblers and import-substitution petrochem. But Indonesia's index is bank- and commodity-dominated; manufacturing/tech is a thin slice of liquidity, so TKDN re-rates earnings only where there's a binding offtake (heavy-equipment to mining/SOE, data-center to digital-government and hyperscaler localization, downstream nickel under hilirisasi) and real pricing power. The fakers are rate-sensitive, cash-burning digital platforms (GOTO, BUKA) and asset-light "tech" names where a local-content tag adds no margin and foreign-flow sentiment, not procurement, sets the price — note GOTO/BUKA already sit on the worst-1y and cheapest-P/B lists, a sentiment tell, not a TKDN earnings tell. Net: localization is a genuine multi-year earnings catalyst for hard-asset industrials and infra-tech, and largely protection-semu for the consumer-internet cohort.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, sub_sector, mcap_tn, roe, pe, pb FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Perindustrian','Teknologi') OR sub_sector LIKE '%Industrial%' OR sub_sector LIKE '%Machinery%' ORDER BY mcap_tn DESC;

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.roe, l.debt_to_equity, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Perindustrian','Teknologi','Barang Baku') AND l.roe > 15 ORDER BY r.ret_1y DESC;
screen_stocks(sector in ['Perindustrian','Teknologi'], roe_min=12, mcap_tn_min=5, sort='ret_1y', order='desc')

SELECT ticker, title, announcement_type, date FROM announcements WHERE (title LIKE '%TKDN%' OR title LIKE '%komponen dalam negeri%' OR title LIKE '%hilirisasi%' OR title LIKE '%kontrak%pemerintah%') AND date >= DATE '2025-11-01' ORDER BY date DESC;

picks.rationale: Deepest domestic auto + heavy-equipment supply chain; TKDN thresholds on autos/EVs and gov/SOE fleet procurement reward its local assembly base — ROE 14, 6.7% yield pay you to wait.
Heavy-equipment + mining-contracting with local assembly; direct beneficiary of localized capex and hilirisasi-driven mining demand, ROE 15 and 6.7% yield, real cash earnings not sentiment.

Data-center buildout is the cleanest TKDN/digital-sovereignty play — data localization and government/hyperscaler onshore demand is a binding offtake; ROE 29 backs the growth, though the 473tn mcap prices in a lot.
Systems-integrator/IT-services with ROE 47 — TKDN on software/IT procurement for SOE and government digitization is a genuine local value-add channel, not a label.
Import-substitution petrochemicals: protectionism + weak IDR widen the price gap vs imported resin/olefins; ROE 38, but -42% trailing-1y and 0.2% yield mean this is a deep-cyclical bet, not a coupon.

Protection-semu tell: a local-content tag adds no margin to a cash-sensitive consumer-internet platform; -36% trailing-1y and sub-1x P/B show foreign-flow sentiment, not TKDN procurement, drives the price — fade the TKDN narrative here.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if TKDN stays a paper rule without enforced offtake — i.e., APBN/SOE capex gets cut for fiscal consolidation, or BI holds rates higher-for-longer on a weak IDR, which strangles the very localization capex the policy is meant to spur and turns even ASII/UNTR into pure cyclicals. Also watch for protectionism backfiring as input-cost inflation (locally-sourced components more expensive than imports) compressing manufacturer margins, and for foreign outflows hitting the whole industrial/tech cohort regardless of fundamentals given how thin the non-bank/non-commodity float is.

answers.category: thematic / policy-driven value (Indonesia housing value chain)
answers.reasoning: The 3-juta-rumah push is a fiscal-and-credit transmission story, not just consumer demand: it works through subsidized KPR (FLPP) volume and a state mandate that lands on BBTN as the dominant subsidized-mortgage lender, which is why BBTN has already re-rated +52% trailing-1y while the rest of the chain still lags — the market is pricing the bank leg first. With BI's policy rate finally easing off the 2024-25 IDR-defense peak (high rates were the thing crushing rate-sensitive, high-debt developers), lower funding cost is the second tailwind: it both fattens BBTN's NIM on a fixed-rate subsidized book and revives end-demand for CTRA/BSDE/SMRA marketing sales. Cement is the purest volume proxy — millions of low-cost units is bags-of-cement demand — but SMGR's domestic oligopoly still has structural overcapacity/utilization drag, so the policy lifts volumes without guaranteeing pricing power. The names are genuinely cheap on hard book value (BBTN 0.49x, BSDE 0.37x, SMRA 0.47x, SMGR 0.40x, CTRA 0.55x P/B) rather than on the unreliable yf ROE/yield figures, so downside is partly margin-of-safety protected. Net: investable for 2-3 years IF FLPP quota disbursement and the rate-cut path actually materialize — the bank is the highest-conviction, cleanest expression; developers and cement are higher-beta second-derivative plays that have NOT yet moved.
tool_queries: SELECT s.ticker, c.sector, ROUND(s.market_cap/1e12,1) AS mcap_tn, ROUND(s.price_to_book,2) AS pb, s.recommendation_key FROM summary s LEFT JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('CTRA','BSDE','BBTN','SMGR','INTP','SMRA','PANI','LPKR') ORDER BY pb ASC;

WITH px AS (SELECT ticker, date, close, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date DESC) rn FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('BBTN','CTRA','BSDE','SMRA','SMGR','INTP')) SELECT * FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BBTN','CTRA','BSDE','SMRA','SMGR','INTP') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC; -- confirm BBTN leads, developers/cement lag

screen_stocks(sector IN ['Properti & Real Estat','Barang Baku','Keuangan'], price_to_book < 0.6, market_cap > 5e12, recommendation_key IN ['buy','strong_buy']) -- cheap, covered names in the housing chain

SELECT ticker, name, local_foreign, percentage FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('CTRA','BSDE','BBTN','SMGR') AND local_foreign='FOREIGN' ORDER BY percentage DESC; -- gauge foreign positioning / float for flow risk

picks.rationale: Highest-conviction leg: dominant subsidized-KPR (FLPP) lender, direct policy beneficiary, P/B 0.49 yet already +52% trailing-1y confirming the market prices the bank first; rate cuts widen NIM on a fixed-rate book.
Best-run large developer, sole strong_buy in the property pack, P/B 0.55, flat trailing-1y (-2%) so the demand-recovery re-rating is still ahead, not behind.
Cheapest big developer at 0.37x book with deep landbank for volume housing; -5% trailing-1y = laggard with the most catch-up room if marketing sales turn on lower rates.
Purest cement-volume proxy for millions of units, market leader at 0.40x P/B; volume tailwind is real but overcapacity caps pricing power, so it's the most leveraged-to-execution pick.
Buy-rated developer, P/B 0.47, the hardest laggard (-14% trailing-1y) — highest beta to a housing-and-rate inflection, sized small for the asymmetry.
Second cement name, Heidelberg-controlled (quality operator), +6% trailing-1y and 0.79x book; pairs with SMGR as the duopoly volume bet with cleaner balance-sheet/dividend optionality.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if it stays a campaign promise: FLPP quota underfunded in the APBN or disbursement stalls, so KPR volume never shows up — and if BI reverses on a renewed IDR-defense (a fresh coal/CPO/commodity-revenue shock or Fed-driven rupiah pressure forcing rate hikes), the rate-sensitive, high-debt developers and the leveraged cement names de-rate fastest. BBTN carries idiosyncratic credit/asset-quality risk on a fast-grown subsidized book; CTRA/BSDE/SMRA marketing-sales recovery is the leading indicator to watch — if it doesn't inflect within 2-4 quarters of rate cuts, the developer/cement legs are dead money and only BBTN's already-realized move stands.

answers.category: BUMN consolidation / Danantara SWF — governance vs dividend extraction
answers.reasoning: Danantara as a BUMN holdco is fundamentally a dividend-extraction vs capital-misallocation trade for minorities, not a buyout. The BUMN cash cows it would consolidate are precisely the high-payout names — BBRI yields 10.4%, PGAS 9.9%, TLKM 6.8%, BBNI 9.4% — and a SWF hungry for dry powder has every incentive to KEEP payouts high near-term, which supports the dividend thesis while BI holds rates elevated and the IDR stays soft (high local yields defend the rupiah and keep foreign bank flows anchored; banks are 3 of the top-4 most liquid names at BBRI 909 / BMRI 847 / BBNI 271 rp bn, so any re-rating moves the whole index). The asymmetric risk is governance: if Danantara redirects retained earnings into low-ROE strategic/infra mandates or forces intra-BUMN M&A at non-market terms, ROE compresses (BBRI 18, BMRI 19, TLKM 18 are the quality to protect) and the dividend that justifies the entry disappears. PGAS is the most exposed both ways — 9.9% yield but an energy/utility tariff-and-subsidy footprint that makes it the likeliest vehicle for policy-driven capex. Net: accumulate the highest-ROE, highest-liquidity BUMN banks where extraction interest aligns with minorities (BBRI, BMRI), hold TLKM for the defensive infra dividend, and keep PGAS/BBNI smaller as the higher-governance-beta sleeve rather than going all-in pre-clarity.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe, dividend_yield, pb FROM fundamentals JOIN summary USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','BBTN','TLKM','PGAS','ANTM','PTBA','JSMR','PGEO','SMGR') ORDER BY mcap_tn DESC;
SELECT ticker, foreign_pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','TLKM','PGAS') ORDER BY foreign_pct DESC;
SELECT ticker, AVG(value_rp_bn) AS adv FROM eod_summary WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','TLKM','PGAS') AND date >= date('now','-30 day') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY adv DESC;
SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','TLKM','PGAS') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC;

picks.rationale: Highest-yield BUMN bank (10.4%) with ROE 18 and 909 rp bn daily liquidity — the prime extraction asset where the SWF's cash hunger keeps payouts intact; core accumulate.
Best-in-class BUMN ROE (19) at 419tn mcap and deep liquidity (847 rp bn); quality earner that re-rates with any governance-clarity catalyst.
Defensive infra dividend (6.8% yield, ROE 18) at a beaten-down 285tn — the lower-governance-beta way to hold the BUMN basket while waiting for Danantara terms.
Cheaper BUMN bank (mcap 146tn, ROE 12, yield 9.4%) — higher governance beta, size it as the second-tier sleeve not the anchor.
9.9% yield energy/utility BUMN but the likeliest vehicle for policy-driven capex and tariff intervention; smallest position, wait-and-see weighting.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if Danantara prioritizes national strategic mandates over shareholder returns — i.e. cuts dividends to fund low-ROE infra/food/energy projects, forces intra-BUMN M&A or capital injections at non-market prices, or dilutes minorities via rights issues to recapitalize weaker BUMNs. Watch the first dividend declaration post-consolidation and any forced-merger announcement; a payout cut at BBRI/BMRI or a related-party transaction at PGAS invalidates the accumulate call and turns this into a governance-discount value trap. Macro override: if BI cuts aggressively and rate-sensitive sectors rotate up, these high-yield defensives de-rate regardless of the BUMN story.

tool_queries: screen_stocks sector Energi mcap_tn over 10 div_yield_pct at least 8 roe_pct at least 15 sort div_yield_pct desc
SELECT ticker, sector, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','MEDC','BBCA','BBRI','SIDO','ICBP','AMRT') ORDER BY ret_1y DESC
SELECT ticker, div_yield_pct, roe_pct, pb, mcap_tn FROM latest WHERE sector='Keuangan' AND mcap_tn>50 ORDER BY roe_pct DESC
SELECT ticker, broker_code, net_value FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','ADRO','PTBA') AND broker_code IN ('BK','AK','KZ') ORDER BY net_value

picks.rationale: Top priority IDR hedge USD revenue on IDR cost base yield 10.7 clean balance sheet resilient to coal DMO windfall tax
Core energy income yield 12.5 large liquidity strong cash flow funds dividends total-return cushion in stagflation
Only overweight bank ROE 21 lowest funding cost best CASA NIM holds while BI stays high quality defensive
Second bank buy on weakness yield 10.4 ROE 18 income protection micro lending insulated from fiscal drag
Preferred defensive staple ROE 37 low capex low debt herbal pricing power holds margin unlike ICBP AMRT down 29
Small tactical oil and gas leverage to energy prices debt-heavy no clean yield smallest sizing oil beta not income

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if BI cuts rates sooner than expected with IDR stabilizing on a dovish Fed which rotates flows out of defensives and energy into banks and rate-sensitives property and tech and erodes energy yield appeal. Second energy-specific risk punitive fiscal policy windfall or export levy tighter coal DMO or a cut to benchmark coal price to contain subsidies could cut ADRO and PTBA dividends. Invalidation signals a BI rate cut with IDR strengthening through a key level or foreign brokers BK AK KZ turning net buyers of banks in broker_net then reduce energy add bank quality.
answers.category: Stagflation tilt overweight dividend energy selective defensive staples underweight banks except quality
answers.reasoning: Mild Indonesian stagflation means weak IDR plus imported inflation plus tight fiscal space so BI stays hold-high and cannot cut aggressively without triggering outflows and more rupiah weakness which pressures rate-sensitive property and tech and squeezes bank credit growth and NIM. Coal and energy exporters are the natural IDR hedge with USD revenue against an IDR cost base so a weak rupiah improves margins and they pay 10 to 15 percent yields ADRO 12.5 PTBA 10.7 that cushion total return when capital gains are thin. The swelling energy subsidy bill is a two-way policy risk for energy windfall tax or tighter coal DMO so I favor high-yield exporters with clean balance sheets over high-beta names. In banks index dominance and top liquidity BBCA 1162 BBRI 909 rp bn mean they get hit first by foreign outflows so I overweight only the strongest ROE and funding profiles BBCA and BBRI and avoid names already broken PNBN minus 39 BANK minus 35. Staples are held for defensive volume and pricing power choosing high-ROE low-capex SIDO over margin-squeezed ICBP and AMRT both down 29 percent.

Belum waktunya rotasi penuh — kepagian untuk all-in defensive. Transmisi makronya: BI tahan di 6% justru karena ruang cut belum ada (inflasi naik + Fed hawkish menekan IDR), jadi ini bukan late-cycle klasik di mana bank sudah dihantam NIM dan kredit macet, melainkan fase higher-for-longer yang menahan, bukan membalik, siklus. Di IDX, "cyclical" tidak homogen: ASII (Perindustrian +128% sektor) dan UNTR adalah leverage ke siklus batubara/alat berat yang sudah dibayar mahal, sementara bank besar (BMRI ROE 19%, BBRI yield 10.4%) bukan early-cycle plays tapi mesin dividen defensif dengan funding base ritel — menjualnya sekarang berarti melepas yield 9-10% justru saat IDR butuh carry tinggi. Data brief mengkonfirmasi sektor Keuangan adalah laggard 1y (+47%, terbawah) sementara Energi/Industri sudah +120-128%, artinya rotasi yang masuk akal bukan "cyclical→defensive" tapi "barang yang sudah lari kencang (energy/industrial beta) → yang tertinggal + defensif berkualitas (staples kuat, telco yield, bank dividen)". Maka: trim ASII (siklus alat berat sudah matang, IDR lemah menaikkan biaya CKD/impor), pertahankan BMRI/BBRI sebagai dividend-defensive, dan mulai akumulasi bertahap ke ICBP/KLBF/TLKM yang justru sudah terkoreksi (ICBP 1y -29%) — itu entry, bukan exit.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe, div_yield, ret_1y FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('BMRI','BBRI','ASII','UNTR','ICBP','KLBF','TLKM','SIDO','UNVR') ORDER BY sector;
SELECT sector, ROUND(AVG(ret_1y),1) AS avg_ret_1y, COUNT(*) n FROM latest GROUP BY sector ORDER BY avg_ret_1y DESC;  -- confirm energy/industrial led, financials lagged

SELECT ticker, beta_1y, div_yield, debt_to_equity FROM fundamentals WHERE mcap_tn>5 AND sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Kesehatan','Infrastruktur') AND div_yield>4 ORDER BY beta_1y ASC;  -- low-beta defensives with yield cushion

SELECT date, ticker, foreign_net FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('BMRI','BBRI','ASII','TLKM','ICBP') AND date >= date('now','-60 day') ORDER BY ticker, date;  -- verify foreign flow direction during IDR weakness

picks.rationale: Hold, jangan jual: ROE 19% + yield 2.2% dan funding ritel kuat; higher-for-longer menjaga NIM, bukan menghancurkannya — Keuangan adalah laggard sektor (+47%) jadi downside sudah terbatas.
Defensive-income anchor: yield 10.4%, ROE 18% — di rezim IDR lemah, dividend yield setinggi ini adalah bantalan, bukan kandidat rotasi keluar.
Ini yang ditrim, bukan ditahan penuh: proksi siklus alat berat/batubara yang sudah matang (Perindustrian +128% sektor), dan IDR lemah menaikkan biaya komponen CKD impor.
Mulai akumulasi bertahap: staples paling defensif tapi 1y -29% (oversold di brief) — pricing power + volume non-discretionary justru menguat kalau inflasi naik; ini entry.
Defensive healthcare berkualitas: permintaan farmasi inelastis terhadap siklus suku bunga, neraca bersih, cocok jadi inti porsi defensif yang ditambah.
Telco defensive + yield 6.8%, ROE 18%: cash flow stabil dan low-beta; tambah bertahap sebagai pengganti beta cyclical yang sudah mahal.

answers.risk: Tesis "kepagian" gugur kalau inflasi melonjak memaksa BI menaikkan suku bunga (bukan sekadar tahan) untuk membela IDR — itu mempercepat late-cycle: kredit melambat, NIM tertekan, dan bank (BMRI/BBRI) jatuh dari laggard jadi value-trap, sehingga rotasi penuh ke defensive jadi benar dan harus dipercepat. Risiko kedua: kalau Fed justru pivot dovish lebih cepat dari perkiraan, IDR rebound, foreign flow balik ke bank/cyclical, dan ICBP/KLBF/TLKM yang sudah diakumulasi malah underperform — jadi jangan over-rotate; jaga porsi bertahap dan pantau foreign_net + arah inflasi bulanan sebagai trigger.

answers.category: macro-stress barbell: commodity exporters (FX-levered) anchored by low-beta domestic staples
answers.reasoning: The transmission that matters here is FX, not just risk-off: IDR 16,500 plus SBN outflow forces BI to keep BI-rate defensive (hold/hike bias to protect the rupiah and the bond market), which lifts the cost of capital and directly hurts rate-sensitive sectors (property, tech, leveraged consumer discretionary) — so the relevant choice is not "staples vs exporters" but "who gets paid by a weak rupiah vs who just survives it." Indonesian coal and nickel exporters sell USD-priced commodities against a largely IDR cost base, so a weaker rupiah mechanically widens IDR-translated margins and fattens already-huge payout ratios (ADRO/PTBA/AADI/ITMG screen at ~10-15% dividend yields with negative beta in the data — they behave like high-carry FX-long bonds, not cyclicals). The catch is the cycle: coal earnings are downgrading off the 2022 super-cycle (BYAN -46% trailing-1y, energy dividends are partly a falling-price illusion), and INCO/nickel is the riskier leg — Indonesian nickel is in structural oversupply from the RKEF/HPAL build-out and INCO carries beta ~0.94 and ~45% foreign strategic ownership (Vale Canada + Sumitomo), so it is both commodity-price- and foreign-flow-sensitive, exactly the flow that is leaving. Domestic staples (SIDO beta 0.11, ICBP/UNVR/MYOR controlled by local sponsors with thin foreign float) give you genuine low-beta ballast and insulation from the outflow churn, but they are net rupiah-cost importers of packaging/wheat/skim milk and have NO FX tailwind — they defend capital, they don't monetize the stress. Net: over 6-12 months tilt to the FX-paid coal cash-cows for offense and clip low-beta staples for defense; treat nickel (INCO) as the smallest, highest-variance sleeve, not the core.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sub_sector in ('Minyak, Gas & Batu Bara') AND dividend_yield > 0.08 AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC  -- isolate the high-carry, FX-long coal payers (ADRO/PTBA/AADI/ITMG/BSSR)

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, s.beta, s.dividend_yield, s.trailing_pe, s.recommendation_key FROM summary s JOIN companies c ON s.ticker=c.ticker WHERE s.ticker IN ('ADRO','PTBA','ITMG','AADI','INCO','SIDO','ICBP','MYOR','UNVR') ORDER BY s.beta  -- rank beta to separate true low-beta ballast (SIDO 0.11) from the market-/FX-sensitive nickel leg (INCO ~0.94)

run_query: SELECT ticker, SUM(CASE WHEN local_foreign='FOREIGN' THEN percentage ELSE 0 END) AS foreign_pct FROM ownership WHERE ticker IN ('INCO','ADRO','PTBA','ICBP','UNVR','MYOR','SIDO') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY foreign_pct DESC  -- quantify exposure to the exact foreign outflow in the scenario; INCO ~45% foreign strategic, staples are local-sponsor-controlled

run_query: SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','ADRO','PTBA','ITMG','INCO','ICBP','AMRT') ORDER BY ret_1y  -- check the coal/commodity downgrade cycle is already in the tape (BYAN -46%) so dividends aren't a falling-price trap

picks.rationale: Core FX-long offense: USD coal revenue vs IDR cost base, ~12.5% yield and negative beta in-data, low-debt cash cow that monetizes a weak rupiah rather than just surviving it.
State-backed coal with clean ~1.7 P/B (reliable, unlike the garbled yf P/Bs), ~10.7% yield and negative beta; same FX tailwind with the lowest balance-sheet and governance risk of the coal sleeve.
Adaro Andalan spin-off, strong_buy in-data, ~9.9% yield and low single-digit forward P/E; highest-conviction analyst name to express the FX-paid thermal-coal carry trade.
Defensive anchor: beta 0.11 (lowest of the set), ~37% ROE, herbal/F&B brand with pricing power and largely domestic ownership — true low-beta ballast against the outflow churn.

Staple ballast with low foreign float (local-sponsor controlled), reasonable ~14x P/E and slightly negative beta; defends capital and is insulated from foreign rotation, though it carries imported-input FX cost drag, not a tailwind.

Smallest, highest-variance leg: nickel exporter that gets the rupiah tailwind but is exposed to structural nickel oversupply and ~45% foreign strategic ownership (Vale/Sumitomo) plus beta ~0.94 — the speculative sleeve, sized small.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the stress flips from FX to commodity price: the coal sleeve (ADRO/PTBA/AADI) is the engine, and its dividends are a falling-price illusion if Newcastle/ICI coal keeps sliding (China demand soft, energy-sector trailing returns already rolling over, BYAN -46%) — a weak rupiah cannot offset a falling USD coal deck, and those ~10-15% yields would be cut. INCO compounds it: nickel oversupply plus the very foreign outflow in the scenario hitting its ~45% foreign register. The other invalidator is a BI capitulation that stabilizes IDR back toward ~15,500 and reverses SBN outflows — that removes the FX edge for exporters and re-rates the rate-sensitive domestic names (banks, property, staples) the barbell is underweighting. Watch the coal price deck and BI's next rate decision as the two triggers.

answers.reasoning: Sinyalnya belum cukup kuat buat clean overweight cyclical, jadi gue stay barbell tapi tilt-nya ke cyclical-value yang masih ketinggalan, bukan ngejar yang udah lari. Transmisinya: PMI baru di 50 + konsumsi lembek artinya leg manufaktur belum confirmed, sementara siklus pemotongan BI rate baru jalan dan efeknya ke kredit/properti/autos itu lag 2-3 kuartal — jadi recovery masih early, bukan mid. Yang penting, sebagian besar trade cyclical udah priced: di brief, Perindustrian (+128), KonsumenNonPrimer (+124), Energi (+120), Properti (+114) semua mimpin, sedangkan Keuangan justru paling buntut (+47) padahal bank itu yang paling diuntungkan rate cut (NIM stabil saat funding cost turun, credit growth naik) dan paling sensitif foreign inflow + likuiditas teratas (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI top avg daily value). Plus IDR rentan kalau Fed higher-for-longer, jadi gue mau cash-flow protection lewat dividend cyclical batubara/CPO (ADRO, PTBA, TAPG, UNTR yield 7-12%) yang ngebayar gue nunggu konfirmasi PMI tembus 52+. Barbell-nya: leg value = bank lagging + dividend energy/CPO (downside protected), leg growth/early-cyclical = autos/consumption proxy buat upside kalau konsumsi akhirnya pulih.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Keuangan') AND mcap_tn > 100 AND dividend_yield > 5 ORDER BY trailing_1y_return ASC -- cari bank besar yang lagging tapi yield tinggi (re-rating ahead, bukan behind)

SELECT s.ticker, e.foreign_buy - e.foreign_sell AS net_foreign FROM eod_summary e JOIN summary s USING(ticker) WHERE e.date >= '2026-04-01' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY SUM(e.foreign_buy - e.foreign_sell) DESC LIMIT 20 -- konfirmasi arah foreign flow ke bank/cyclical dalam 2 bulan terakhir

SELECT ticker, sector, roe, dividend_yield, pb FROM latest WHERE mcap_tn > 5 AND dividend_yield > 7 AND sector IN ('Energi','Barang Konsumen Primer','Perindustrian') ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC -- dividend cyclical (batubara/CPO/heavy-equip) buat carry sambil nunggu PMI confirm
SELECT ticker, AVG(close*volume)/1e9 AS adv_rpbn FROM prices WHERE date >= '2026-05-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 25 -- verifikasi likuiditas: pastikan picks bisa di-exit kalau thesis invalid

picks.rationale: Bank lagging (sektor Keuangan +47, paling buntut) tapi yield 10.4% & ROE 18% — leg value utama yang re-rating-nya di depan kalau BI cut nransmisi ke credit growth; likuiditas no.2 jadi gampang di-exit.
Mirip BBRI: bank besar ROE 19%, yield tinggi, beta tinggi ke recovery & foreign inflow; bank dominasi large-cap jadi ini cara paling bersih main rate-cut tanpa ngejar cyclical yang udah lari.
Leg dividend-cyclical: yield 12.5% kasih carry & downside protection kalau recovery stall; cash flow batubara ngebayar gue nunggu konfirmasi PMI tembus 52+.
Dividend energy yield 10.7%, defensif-cyclical — proxy cash cycle batubara yang lindungin portofolio kalau IDR/Fed risk muncul.

Early-cyclical/consumption proxy: autos + heavy equipment + financing, yield 6.7%, ROE 14% — leg growth/recovery yang upside-nya kebuka justru kalau konsumsi domestik akhirnya pulih (bagian thesis yang belum confirmed).
CPO dividend cyclical (yield 8.9%, ROE 34%) — diversifikasi commodity cycle ke palm yang gak gerak barengan batubara, nambah carry tanpa nambah beta manufaktur yang masih ragu.

answers.risk: Thesis batal kalau (1) PMI manufaktur balik di bawah 50 / konsumsi makin lembek ke H2-2026 — itu artinya pelambatan belum kelar, leg cyclical (ASII) kena duluan; atau (2) Fed higher-for-longer / IDR tembus level lemah maksa BI nahan-balik rate cut — ini langsung matiin transmisi ke bank (BBRI/BMRI), sekaligus tekan harga batubara via demand global, jadi dua leg barbell kena bareng. Trigger re-evaluate: foreign net SELL bank 3 bulan beruntun, atau BI hold + IDR depresiasi >5%. Catatan data: yield/ROE dari yf di brief unreliable buat sebagian nama — treat directionally, verifikasi via run_query ke eod_summary/fundamentals sebelum sizing.

answers.reasoning: Di IDX growth realistis bukan platform bakar duit. Transmisi Fed cut ke sini lewat IDR dan foreign flow: Fed cut menyempitkan diferensial rate, IDR stabil, BI dapat ruang potong rate, dana asing masuk lewat saham paling likuid yaitu bank besar BBCA BBRI BMRI. Bank adalah growth-at-rate-relief karena NIM dan loan growth membaik saat cost of fund turun, ROE 18-21 persen, sudah profitable, beda dari GOTO yang ROE negatif. Posisi terbaik adalah barbell: inti bank profitable plus DCII tech yang sudah cetak kas, dengan sleeve dividend batubara ADRO sebagai hedge.
tool_queries: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.market_cap, l.return_on_equity, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING ticker WHERE l.market_cap above 50e12 AND l.return_on_equity above 0.15 ORDER BY return_on_equity DESC
screen_stocks sector Keuangan market_cap above 100e12 return_on_equity above 0.15 sort dividend_yield desc
SELECT ticker, net foreign_buy minus foreign_sell last 60d FROM eod_summary GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net desc
SELECT ticker, return_on_equity, ret_1y FROM latest JOIN returns WHERE ticker IN GOTO BUKA DCII

picks.rationale: Core rate-relief growth: ROE 21 persen, paling likuid, first stop foreign inflow saat Fed cut dan IDR stabil.
Leverage langsung ke BI rate cut lewat kredit mikro; ROE 18 persen, yield 10.4 persen beri bantalan.
Big-cap bank ROE 19 persen; NIM membaik saat cost of fund turun, growth siklikal yang profitable.
Satu-satunya tech growth IDX yang layak: data center ROE 29 persen, profitable, tema AI tanpa bakar duit.
Proxy growth siklikal domestik sensitif rate cut dan daya beli; ROE 14 persen, yield 6.7 persen jangkar value.
Sleeve value hedge: yield 12.5 persen mendanai tunggu kalau cut tertunda dan coal cycle bertahan.

answers.risk: Tesis batal kalau Fed menunda cut dan inflasi AS lengket sehingga diferensial rate tetap lebar: IDR melemah, BI hold, foreign outflow dari bank, re-rating gagal. Risiko kedua resesi global menjatuhkan batubara nikel CPO sehingga sleeve value dan siklikal jatuh bersamaan. Jangan tambah GOTO atau BUKA sebagai growth karena profitless dan paling rentan saat narasi cut berbalik.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia is a commodity terms-of-trade economy: coal and CPO export receipts drive the trade surplus, IDR stability, and ultimately BI's room to cut — so a China-led demand slowdown hits IDX twice, compressing the earnings of cyclical exporters AND the macro tailwind (weaker IDR forces BI to stay higher-for-longer to defend the currency, which in turn squeezes rate-sensitive property/tech). The brief already shows the cycle has turned at the stock level even though the Energi sector's trailing-1y average (+120%) looks healthy: the coal heavyweight BYAN is -46% and the high "yields" on PTBA/ADRO/BSSR/GEMS/AADI are mechanically inflated by payouts off peak-2023/24 earnings against falling prices — a classic late-cycle dividend trap where the yield evaporates as DPS resets to mid-cycle coal/CPO prices. The right move for a 60%-cyclical book in mid-to-late cycle is not a binary flight to "defensives" but a staged de-risk that (a) cuts gross cyclical exposure toward ~35-40%, (b) keeps only the lowest-cost, balance-sheet-strongest cyclicals you'd hold through a trough, and (c) rotates the proceeds into quality compounders and structurally-demanded defensives — bank quality (BBCA), telco/toll-road cash yield (TLKM, JSMR), and health/staples with real ROE and pricing power (SIDO, ICBP) — rather than chasing the highest screen-yield. Frame it as three buckets — Core Defensive Compounders (~45%), Cyclical Survivors you keep for the eventual upturn (~35%), and Dry Powder / quality-yield (~20%) — and rebalance in tranches over 2-3 months rather than all at once, because the cyclical names still throw off large cash dividends that fund the rotation and a China stimulus surprise is the live upside risk.
tool_queries:
-- Confirm the cycle has turned at the stock level despite a high sector-average: rank Energi/BarangBaku/KonsumenPrimer names by trailing-1y return to separate momentum from value-traps
SELECT r.ticker, l.sector, r.ret_1y, l.market_cap
FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING (ticker)
WHERE l.sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND l.market_cap > 5e12
ORDER BY r.ret_1y ASC LIMIT 25;

-- Quality-defensive screen: high ROE, real dividend, NOT a coal/CPO name; this surfaces SIDO/ICBP/TLKM/JSMR/BBCA-type compounders
SELECT ticker, sector, return_on_equity AS roe, dividend_yield AS yld, price_to_book AS pb, beta, market_cap
FROM fundamentals
WHERE market_cap > 5e12 AND sector IN ('Kesehatan','Barang Konsumen Primer','Infrastruktur','Keuangan')
  AND return_on_equity > 0.15 AND dividend_yield > 0.03 AND beta < 1.0
ORDER BY roe DESC;

screen_stocks(sector_in=['Energi'], min_market_cap=5e12, sort_by='dividend_yield', order='desc') -- then cross-check each coal name's payout_ratio and net debt in fundamentals to flag dividend traps (yield high because trailing DPS sits on peak coal earnings)

-- Foreign-flow confirmation of the rotation: is foreign money already leaving cyclicals and crowding into banks/defensives? net foreign value by sector over the last 60 sessions
SELECT l.sector, SUM(bn.foreign_buy - bn.foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_60d
FROM broker_net bn JOIN latest l USING (ticker)
WHERE bn.date >= (SELECT MAX(date) - INTERVAL 60 DAY FROM broker_net)
GROUP BY l.sector ORDER BY net_foreign_60d DESC;

picks.rationale: Core defensive anchor: Kesehatan/jamu staple with 37% ROE and low beta — earnings independent of the coal/CPO cycle and the cleanest quality-compounder in the brief's defensive sectors.

Quality bank ballast (21% ROE, 5.1% yield, most liquid name at Rp1.16tn/day) — funding-cost franchise that holds NIM and asset quality through a slowdown better than commodity-levered or high-yield-trap banks.
Defensive cash-yield: telco infrastructure, 18% ROE, 6.8% dividend backed by recurring revenue, not commodity prices — pure rotation target to swap falling coal yield for durable yield.
Toll-road monopoly cash flows with non-cyclical traffic demand, trading at 0.62 P/B — defensive infra carry that re-rates if BI eventually cuts; cheaper entry than telco.
Consumer-staples compounder already de-rated -29% trailing — the sell-off creates an entry into pricing-power defensible demand; accumulate as the cyclical-to-staples rotation funds itself.

The ONE cyclical survivor to keep, not add: lowest-cost domestic-coal SOE with a real (if shrinking) 10.7% yield and net-cash balance sheet — hold a trimmed position for the eventual upturn, but expect the headline yield to reset lower as DPS normalizes off peak earnings.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if China delivers a large fiscal/property stimulus that re-accelerates coal, nickel and CPO demand — that would re-rate the cyclicals you just trimmed (BYAN/ADRO/PTBA), strengthen IDR via terms-of-trade, give BI room to cut, and turn the de-risk into a missed rally; secondary risks are (1) the screen-yield "traps" actually sustaining payouts longer than expected if commodity prices stabilize mid-cycle rather than fall further, and (2) defensives like ICBP/UNVR carrying their own structural volume/competition problems unrelated to the cycle, so quality (ROE, balance sheet, beta) — not the defensive label — must gate every pick. Mitigant: stage the rotation in tranches and keep ~20% dry powder so a China surprise is survivable rather than fatal.

tool_queries: join summary and companies on ticker, filter market_cap over 5tn and beta not null, order by beta ascending to surface lowest-beta big caps
group the join by sector, average beta per sector, order ascending to rank structurally low-beta sectors
screen_stocks sector Energi mcap_min 5tn sort dividend_yield desc limit 15 to rank coal energy inflation hedges by carry
filter join to Barang Konsumen Primer market_cap over 5tn order by profit_margins desc to verify staples pricing power

picks.rationale: Coal major, roughly 12.5pct yield and negative beta, the primary IDR and inflation hedge. USD-linked coal revenue rises with energy prices and rupiah depreciation, and the dividend is the stagflation cash buffer.
State-owned coal, double-digit yield, negative beta. Domestic plus export coal earnings hedge imported inflation while the dividend pays you to wait out slower growth.
Low-cost coal exporter, fat yield, negative beta and net-cash balance sheet, a pure commodity and currency hedge with low leverage risk if BI stays hawkish.
Staples bellwether with the strongest brand pricing power on IDX. Inelastic demand defends the growth-slowdown leg, though watch USD-import COGS if IDR breaks hard.
Noodle and staple-food defensive, near-zero beta, durable margins and pricing power on essential consumption, the demand-resilience ballast, and trailing-1y weakness offers entry.
Herbal consumer-health staple, top-tier margins, low beta, mostly local sourcing so less exposed to USD-import COGS than packaged-food peers, the cleanest domestic-defensive name.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the rupiah stabilizes and oil and coal roll over. That simultaneously kills the energy dividend hedge as coal prices and payouts fall, and lets BI cut rates, rotating flows back into rate-sensitive cyclicals such as property, banks and tech that the barbell is positioned against. Other landmines. First, a sharp IDR break hurts both legs unequally, helping coal and CPO but squeezing staples USD inputs like wheat, dairy and packaging, so the staples sleeve is less defensive than a US analogue. Second, coal names carry policy, DMO and windfall-tax risk plus secular ESG-driven multiple compression, and the negative-beta print partly reflects names already de-rated rather than true safety. Third, the brief yf ROE and yield ratios are scale-unreliable, so verify trailing dividend yield against prices and eod_summary before sizing.
answers.reasoning: In a mild Indonesian stagflation the transmission runs through BI. A soft rupiah forces BI to stay hawkish to defend the currency and contain imported-inflation pass-through, which compresses rate-sensitive property, tech and leveraged names while supporting the IDR-earner exporter complex. The right IDX answer is a barbell, not staples versus commodities, because the two hedge different legs of the shock. Coal, energy and CPO are a direct IDR-revenue USD-priced inflation and currency hedge whose earnings rise with oil, coal and rupiah depreciation, and they throw off the fattest dividend yields as a cash buffer, while staples are the growth-slowdown hedge where inelastic demand and pricing power defend volumes and margins. The data backs this. The cleanest discriminator in the brief is beta, and the most negative-beta big caps cluster in Energi and staples, while high-beta pain sits in Perindustrian, Barang Baku and Teknologi, exactly the cyclicals stagflation punishes. The IDX-specific nuance is that local staples carry heavy USD-import content, so a sharp IDR break squeezes their COGS even as it boosts the commodity leg, which is why I overweight the energy dividend sleeve as the primary hedge and use staples as ballast.

Di puncak cycle, transmisi yang menentukan IDX bukan "defensive vs cyclical" generik tapi siklus komoditas + BI rate + foreign flow: ~40% bobot LQ45 ada di bank, dan laba bank bergerak searah NIM/credit cost yang dikendalikan BI rate, sementara cap besar lain (BYAN, ADRO, PTBA, AMMN) adalah proxy harga batubara/nikel/emas dalam USD — jadi koreksi "pasar" biasanya datang dari harga batubara turun + Fed/BI memangkas yang menekan IDR dan memicu outflow asing, bukan dari resesi konsumen domestik. Yield batubara yang kelihatan 10-15% (ADRO 12.5, PTBA 10.7, BSSR 15.4, GEMS 14.7) itu BUKAN defensive: itu special dividend dari laba puncak siklus yang akan turun bersama harga batubara, jadi membeli yield itu = double-long komoditas, kebalikan dari yang investor mau. Defensive sejati di IDX adalah arus kas non-komoditas dengan pricing power: telco/utilitas regulated (TLKM 6.8%, POWR 9.7%) dan consumer staples high-ROE rendah-beta (SIDO, UNVR, ICBP) yang permintaannya inelastis terhadap siklus komoditas dan IDR. Soal pajak: untuk WP orang pribadi domestik, dividen dari emiten IDR sekarang BEBAS PPh final 10% jika diinvestasikan kembali di Indonesia dalam jangka waktu tertentu (PP 9/2022 turunan UU HPP) — sehingga net yield TLKM/SIDO ~6-7% reinvested mengalahkan deposito/cash ~rate setelah pajak dan jauh mengalahkan opportunity cost jika koreksi 15-25% memang datang; vs investor asing kena withholding 20%/tax treaty yang membuat angka net berbeda.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Infrastruktur','Barang Konsumen Primer') AND yield_pct >= 4 AND beta <= 0.9 AND return_on_equity >= 12 AND mcap_tn >= 10, sort by yield_pct desc -- isolate true low-beta defensive yield, exclude coal

SELECT s.ticker, l.sector, s.dividend_yield, s.payout_ratio, s.beta, s.return_on_equity, s.debt_to_equity FROM summary s JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Infrastruktur','Barang Konsumen Primer','Barang Konsumen Non-Primer') AND s.dividend_yield >= 4 AND s.beta < 1 ORDER BY s.beta ASC -- rank by lowest cyclicality, not just headline yield

SELECT r.ticker, l.sector, r.ret_1y, s.dividend_yield, s.beta FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) JOIN summary s USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector = 'Energi' AND s.dividend_yield > 9 -- confirm the high coal yields ride a peaked ret_1y (BYAN -46% YTD) => yield is a melting ice cube, exclude from defensive bucket

SELECT ticker, dividend_yield, payout_ratio, debt_to_equity, free_cash_flow FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('TLKM','SIDO','UNVR','ICBP','POWR','ADRO','PTBA') -- verify payout sustainability: low payout + low D/E + positive FCF survives a downcycle; coal payout near 100% does not

picks.rationale: Telco regulated, mcap 285tn, ROE 18%, yield 6.8% dengan beta rendah dan arus kas non-komoditas — defensive sejati yang net-nya menarik (dividen reinvested bebas PPh 10% untuk WP OP) dan tidak ikut jatuh kalau batubara/IDR yang memicu koreksi.

Consumer staples kesehatan, ROE 37%, payout tinggi tapi nyaris tanpa utang dan FCF kuat — permintaan jamu/suplemen inelastis terhadap siklus, dividen lebih aman bertahan saat koreksi dibanding cash yang tergerus inflasi.

Staples makanan, sudah -29% trailing-1y jadi sebagian downside cycle ter-price, defensif terhadap pelemahan komoditas; entry lebih murah dengan yield wajar — pilihan defensive yang valuasinya sudah de-rate, bukan beli di puncak.

Utilitas listrik (Cikarang Listrindo), Infrastruktur, yield 9.7% dengan pendapatan ber-kontrak/regulated USD — proxy utilitas paling dekat dengan definisi defensive di IDX, arus kas stabil tidak bergantung harga batubara spot.

Anchor likuiditas (avg daily 1.162tn), ROE 21%, yield 5.1% — bukan pure-defensive tapi bank paling rate-resilient/kualitas aset terbaik; lebih baik dipegang daripada cash karena BI rate cut justru menopang valuasi bank saat market koreksi karena komoditas.

Sengaja TIDAK masuk bucket defensive: yield 10.7% adalah special dividend laba puncak batubara yang akan turun bersama harga — disebut sebagai jebakan 'high-yield = defensive' yang justru long-cycle, harus dihindari di skenario ini.

answers.risk: Thesis runtuh kalau koreksi yang datang adalah resesi konsumen domestik (PHK + daya beli, bukan komoditas/IDR) — di situ staples high-payout ikut kena dan defensive premium hilang; atau kalau BI justru menaikkan rate untuk membela IDR (bukan memangkas), maka cash/deposito 6-7% after-tax mengungguli yield equity 6-7% sambil menghindari mark-to-market loss, dan opportunity cost rotasi defensive jadi negatif. Pemicu invalidasi lain: aturan reinvestasi PP 9/2022 tidak terpenuhi (holding period/jenis instrumen), membuat dividen kena PPh final 10% sehingga net yield jatuh ke ~6% dan tipisnya margin vs cash hilang; serta jika harga batubara/nikel justru rally (puncak cycle belum tiba), maka melepas energi untuk defensive = opportunity cost besar.

answers.reasoning: Rate cut bagus loan growth tapi NIM asimetris: bank CASA-tinggi (BBCA, BMRI, BBRI) defend NIM, bank kecil kena kompresi. Penerima termurni consumption-leverage: ASII, ACES, BFIN. Konsumen Non-Primer 124pct vs Keuangan 47pct. BI cut butuh IDR stabil, BBCA BBRI foreign-heavy. Verdict barbell.
answers.risk: Rupiah: IDR lemah, BI hold, foreign flow keluar bank, NIM kompresi tanpa loan growth. Risiko dua: daya beli lemah, NPL naik, ASII BFIN credit cost.

run_query foreign flow banks 30d

answers.reasoning: Goldilocks soft-landing = falling global inflation → Fed easing → softer DXY → stable/firmer IDR, which is the master IDX transmission variable: a stable rupiah lets Bank Indonesia cut its policy rate and pulls foreign flows back into the liquid large-cap complex. BI cuts re-rate the two rate-sensitive legs of any IDX barbell — banks (stable NIM, falling credit costs, the index's liquidity and weight anchor) and high-leverage property (cheaper mortgages, balance-sheet relief). The quality leg compounds on its own engine regardless of macro: BBCA's fortress CASA and ~21% ROE plus genuinely profitable tech like DCII (data-center, ROE ~29) versus the cash-burning GOTO/BUKA the brief flags as deep losers (-36%). The deep-value leg's risk-reward is asymmetric by name: property (BSDE 0.37x, SMRA 0.47x, CTRA 0.55x P/B) is cheap because it is geared to the BI cut you are betting on, so it has a real catalyst — but BUMN karya construction carries leverage, fiscal-dependency and contingent-liability overhang with no clean re-rating trigger, so I would not own it. Net: tilt the barbell to quality-growth as the durable core (foreign-flow magnet, low blow-up risk) and use property as a higher-beta value satellite sized to your BI-cut conviction.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe, pb, yield FROM latest WHERE mcap_tn > 5 AND roe > 18 AND pb > 0 ORDER BY roe DESC; -- quality leg: profitable, high-ROE large caps (BBCA, DCII)
screen_stocks(sector='Properti & Real Estat', mcap_min=5e12, pb_max=0.7, sort='pb_asc') -- deep-value rate-sensitive property at trough P/B (BSDE, SMRA, CTRA, LPKR)
SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('GOTO','BUKA','DCII') ORDER BY ret_1y; -- separate profitable tech (DCII) from cash-burning tech before calling it 'quality'

SELECT b.ticker, b.net_value FROM broker_net b JOIN latest l ON b.ticker=l.ticker WHERE l.sector='Keuangan' AND b.broker_code IN ('AK','BK','KZ') ORDER BY b.net_value DESC; -- confirm foreign brokers (UBS, JPM, CLSA) accumulating banks as IDR/flows turn

picks.rationale: Quality core: ~21% ROE fortress-CASA bank, most-liquid IDX name and the primary foreign-flow vehicle when a stable IDR + BI cuts pull money back into large-cap banks.
The 'profitable tech' the question wants — data-center, ROE ~29 — a long-duration growth name that re-rates on falling rates without the cash-burn of GOTO/BUKA.
Quality-plus-income hedge: ~18% ROE with a ~10.4% yield, so it pays you to hold the bank-dominated core while waiting for the BI-cut re-rating.
Value satellite with the cleanest property balance sheet: 0.55x P/B, geared directly to cheaper mortgages as BI cuts — a real catalyst, not just optical cheapness.
Deepest large-cap property value at 0.37x P/B; high-beta upside on a BI-cut re-rating, sized small as the speculative end of the barbell.
Defensive value bridge between the legs: 0.62x P/B toll-road monopoly cash flows, rate-sensitive on its debt load but far lower blow-up risk than BUMN karya construction.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the rupiah does not cooperate: a sticky-inflation / 'higher-for-longer' Fed, a renewed DXY spike, or a commodity-revenue shock (coal/nickel/CPO down → current-account and fiscal pressure) forces BI to hold or hike. That kills the BI-cut catalyst, stalls foreign inflows, and the deep-value property/leverage leg de-rates hardest — so the barbell's value side is the first casualty. Idiosyncratic: BUMN karya contingent liabilities or a fiscal blowout (new-administration spending) would hit SOE-linked construction and sour sentiment on the whole value complex; keep that leg unowned and size property to BI-cut conviction.

answers.reasoning: The fiscal-premium-into-SBN-yield transmission is real but its bite on IDX is narrower than the textbook "growth vs value" frame because IDX has almost no true long-duration growth complex: the index is ~40%+ banks plus commodity dividend payers, not high-multiple secular growers. Higher SBN yields hit IDX through two specific channels: (1) duration/discount-rate compression on the handful of genuine high-PE/no-yield names (BREN, DCII, GOTO, BUKA, PANI and levered property like LPKR/SMRA), and (2) a funding-cost and NIM squeeze plus higher cost-of-equity for rate-sensitive financials and high-debt property; meanwhile a steeper SBN curve actually helps bank treasury carry and makes 9-12% dividend yields (coal, big banks, telco) more defensive as a real-yield anchor. The crowded foreign-owned, low-yield, high-multiple book is also the marginal seller when fiscal risk premium lifts the IDR discount rate and pressures the rupiah, so flow mechanics amplify the growth-side drawdown well beyond the pure DCF math. Net: a partial, surgical rotation is justified — trim the specific long-duration/no-cashflow growth names and crowd into high-real-yield commodity and bank dividend defensives — but a wholesale "growth-to-value" switch overstates it, since IDX "growth" is a thin sleeve and the dominant banks straddle both buckets (cheap P/B, double-digit yield, yet rate-sensitive).
tool_queries: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.trailing_pe, l.price_to_book, s.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) LEFT JOIN summary s USING(ticker) WHERE l.market_cap > 5e12 AND l.trailing_pe > 30 AND (s.dividend_yield IS NULL OR s.dividend_yield < 1) ORDER BY l.trailing_pe DESC -- isolate the high-PE/no-yield duration sleeve most exposed to a yield shock

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, s.dividend_yield, l.return_on_equity, l.price_to_book, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN summary s USING(ticker) JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE s.dividend_yield >= 8 AND l.market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY s.dividend_yield DESC -- high real-yield defensives (coal, banks, telco) that re-rate as a yield anchor

SELECT ticker, sector, price_to_book, debt_to_equity, return_on_equity, market_cap FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Teknologi') AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY debt_to_equity DESC NULLS LAST -- rate-sensitive high-debt property + tech to underweight if SBN yields climb

SELECT b.ticker, sum(b.net_value) AS foreign_proxy_net FROM broker_net b WHERE b.broker_code IN ('AK','BK','KZ','ZP') AND b.date >= '2026-04-01' GROUP BY b.ticker ORDER BY foreign_proxy_net ASC LIMIT 25 -- foreign-tilted brokers' net selling: confirm whether crowded growth names are the marginal sellers under fiscal stress

picks.rationale: 10.7% yield, state-linked coal with strong cash return; high real-yield defensive that gains relative appeal as SBN risk premium lifts the discount rate.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the yield rise is growth-led/orderly (BI holds or cuts on a stable IDR and the curve bull-steepens) rather than a fiscal-risk-premium shock — then risk appetite stays on and the thin growth sleeve doesn't de-rate. The bigger invalidator is that coal/energy "defensives" here are commodity-cyclical, not duration-defensive: if a fiscal blow-up tanks the IDR and global growth, coal prices and ADRO/PTBA/BYAN dividends fall together, so they offer no shelter. And because IDX is bank-and-commodity dominated with only a thin high-PE complex, the entire growth-to-value spread may simply be too small to trade — the rotation underperforms its transaction and liquidity costs (BREN/DCII are illiquid to exit), making "do nothing but tilt toward double-digit real yields" the higher-expectancy move.

A real 2026 global recession transmits to IDX through three channels and each one inverts the current high yield screen. A demand collapse craters coal nickel and CPO prices so the high yield energy names ADRO PTBA BSSR GEMS and BYAN see payouts evaporate because they are commodity windfalls not recurring earnings which is a yield trap not a defensive. Foreign outflows weaken IDR forcing BI to hold rates high to defend the currency rather than cut which is lethal for leveraged rate sensitive property developers and cash burning tech while a tailwind for net cash firms that earn float on their treasury. Genuine defensives are the subset of staples health and telco with inelastic low ticket demand and net cash so dividends self fund through the cycle where SIDO UNVR and TLKM fit while the Indofood complex ICBP and INDF is staple on demand but carries FX and balance sheet leverage so it ranks below. The cyclicals to dump outright are leveraged property PANI BSDE CTRA SMRA LPKR and leveraged growth and coal GOTO BUMI BYAN where high real rates plus collapsing volumes hit a debt heavy capital structure at once.
