answers.reasoning: Transmisi krisis AS ke IDX bukan lewat ekspor barang (porsi ekspor RI ke AS kecil), tapi lewat jalur finansial: risk-off global memicu foreign outflow dari saham + SBN, IDR melemah ke 16.5k-17k, lalu BI terpaksa menahan/menaikkan rate demi stabilitas rupiah ketimbang langsung memangkas, yang menekan justru bank besar dan sektor rate-sensitive. Secara historis (2008 -50%+, 2015 taper/devaluasi yuan, 2020 COVID -37%, 2022 hiking cycle) IHSG menyerap sekitar 30-60% beta S&P pada drawdown 20-30%, jadi base case koreksi IHSG 12-22% dengan fat tail ke -30% kalau outflow dan rupiah panik bersamaan; tapi rebound IHSG juga historis lebih cepat karena valuasi domestik murah dan dominasi demand domestik. Yang paling tahan banting adalah cluster yang earnings-nya tidak bergantung commodity price global maupun foreign appetite: consumer staples permintaan domestik (rokok/makanan), telco-toll infrastruktur dengan cash flow regulated + dividen tinggi, dan paradoksnya batu bara dividen tinggi milik domestik karena yield 10-15% jadi bantalan total return meski harga batu bara turun. Yang harus dihindari justru bank foreign-heavy (rentan outflow), properti/tech high-beta rate-sensitive (GOTO, BUKA, BSDE), dan metals siklikal global (TPIA, AMMN, ANTM) yang langsung kena demand AS. Rotasi dilakukan SEKARANG (pre-crash) saat likuiditas masih ada, bukan saat outflow sudah mulai.
tool_queries: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.dividend_yield, l.beta, l.return_on_equity, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.market_cap > 5e12 AND l.dividend_yield > 6 AND l.beta < 1 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC LIMIT 30 -- high-yield low-beta defensives that cushion total return in a drawdown

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.beta, l.return_on_equity, l.profit_margins, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Infrastruktur') AND l.market_cap > 10e12 ORDER BY l.beta ASC -- isolate domestic-demand staples + regulated telco/toll with lowest beta

SELECT o.ticker, sum(CASE WHEN o.local_foreign='FOREIGN' THEN o.percentage ELSE 0 END) AS foreign_pct FROM ownership o GROUP BY o.ticker HAVING foreign_pct > 40 ORDER BY foreign_pct DESC -- flag high foreign-ownership names (banks/BNLI/BTPN) most exposed to outflow, to UNDERWEIGHT

answers.risk: Thesis batal kalau The Fed memangkas rate agresif lebih dulu (soft-landing/recession-with-easing): itu melemahkan USD, memutar foreign flow MASUK ke EM, dan justru bank foreign-heavy + properti/tech rate-sensitive yang rally, sementara defensif high-yield underperform. Risiko kedua: pelemahan IDR tak terkendali (>17k) memaksa BI hawkish defensif -- ini memukul SEMUA sektor termasuk yang dianggap defensif (toll/property leverage, dan multiple compression menyeluruh), sehingga "tahan banting" jadi relatif, bukan absolut. Ketiga: kalau harga batu bara jeblok >40% (bukan sekadar koreksi), yield PTBA/coal plays tidak sustainable dan dividen dipotong, membatalkan tesis bantalan yield.

answers.reasoning: Ini klasik "dollar smile": waktu recession fear, USD malah menguat karena flight to safety + USD adalah funding currency global, jadi Fed cut agresif tidak otomatis melemahkan dolar — DXY naik, IDR jebol ke 17.000 dan BI terpaksa defend rupiah (rate tinggi/intervensi cadangan devisa), bukan ikut cut, yang justru menekan bank dan emiten rate-sensitive (properti, tech). Untuk eksportir IDR melemah = tailwind translasi: revenue USD, cost base (gaji, royalti, biaya stripping/angkut) sebagian besar IDR, jadi margin per ton melebar. TAPI ada dua gaya berlawanan: harga komoditas global turun karena demand recession. Net effect-nya beda per komoditi — CPO relatif defensif (demand minyak nabati/food + biodiesel B40 domestik inelastis, supply ketat), sementara thermal coal lebih cyclical ke industrial/power demand China-India sehingga lebih rentan koreksi harga; emas/AMMN-ANTM justru menang dua arah (safe-haven + weak IDR). Jadi yang paling diuntungkan adalah eksportir dengan demand inelastis + neraca kas kuat + yield tinggi (buffer kalau harga turun), bukan blanket "semua eksportir cuan".

answers.risk: Tesis ini patah kalau leg "demand global turun" menang telak atas leg "weak IDR": recession dalam membuat harga thermal coal dan CPO jatuh lebih cepat daripada tambahan margin dari IDR, jadi coal cyclical (BYAN, ADRO) bisa rugi bersih meski rupiah lemah. Risiko kedua: BI menaikkan rate agresif demi defend IDR 17.000 -> cost of capital naik, foreign outflow dari nama foreign-ownership tinggi memperdalam koreksi indeks. Pembalik tesis: kalau ternyata Fed cut justru melemahkan USD (bukan dollar-smile) dan IDR menguat balik, keunggulan translasi eksportir hilang. Lindung paling bersih tetap emas (AMMN/ANTM) karena menang di kedua kaki; coal adalah taruhan harga, bukan taruhan FX murni.

answers.reasoning: Mekanisme transmisi: saat VIX>40 dan global risk-off, asing menarik modal dari EM melalui jalur paling likuid dan paling foreign-crowded. Di IDX, asing tidak menjual merata — mereka membuang big-cap likuid yang jadi proxy "Indonesia trade" (BBCA, BBRI, BMRI), karena itu yang bisa dijual cepat tanpa slippage; brokernya kelihatan dari dominasi BK J.P.Morgan, AK UBS, KZ CLSA di tape. BBCA adalah single most-liquid (avg daily Rp1.162 bn) dan mcap terbesar (733tn) sehingga jadi ATM utama outflow asing, sekaligus benchmark-weight yang otomatis dijual saat MSCI/EM funds redemption — tapi balance-sheet-nya (ROE 21%, yield rendah 5.1%) bikin domestik & lifer lokal cepat masuk lagi. BBRI justru lebih rentan secara fundamental-makro: dividend yield 10.4% bikin dia "EM carry/yield trade" yang langsung terpukul saat IDR melemah (risk-off → USDIDR naik → BI cenderung tahan/naikkan rate untuk bela rupiah → NIM mikro-banking & cost of fund tertekan, plus base kredit UMKM lebih sensitif siklus). TLKM paling defensif: kepemilikan negara, yield 6.8%, likuiditas lebih rendah (Rp331 bn) dan kurang jadi target hot-money, lebih di-support investor domestik & dana pensiun lokal sebagai bond-proxy saat yield SBN turun.

Juga: data broker_net/ownership di brief belum diverifikasi per-tanggal risk-off historis — kalau foreign ownership BBRI ternyata sudah jauh lebih rendah dari BBCA (sudah ter-derisk), urutan kerentanan outflow bisa terbalik (BBCA jadi yang paling rentan secara nominal sekaligus persentase).

answers.reasoning: A China hard landing transmits to IDX through three channels: (1) coal/nickel collapse craters earnings and the fat dividends at ADRO/ITMG/ANTM since those names are price-takers, not cost-curve winners (note ANTM nickel and ADRO/ITMG thermal coal are directly levered to Chinese property/steel demand); (2) a commodity terms-of-trade shock weakens IDR and widens the current account, which forces BI to keep policy rates higher-for-longer to defend the rupiah, pressuring rate-sensitive property (BSDE/CTRA/SMRA — high debt) and growth/tech (GOTO/BUKA/DCII); (3) foreign outflows hit the index but rotate within it toward IDR-revenue defensives. The hedge is to stay invested but rotate ~40% commodity weight into domestic-demand consumer staples whose COGS actually falls when commodities drop (UNVR/ICBP buy cheaper palm/inputs — margin tailwind, inverse to your current book), regulated/contracted-cash-flow infra and telco that pay through the cycle (TLKM, POWR), and the high-NIM mega-banks (BBCA/BBRI) that dominate IDX liquidity and keep ROE >18% even in a hold-rate regime. Within commodities, do not go full cash — barbell into gold/precious via ANTM's gold leg or pivot some weight to lowest-cost coal so dividends survive the price drop.

Other invalidators: BI cuts aggressively (re-rating property/tech, not banks), or a sharper-than-expected IDR collapse that lifts CPI and squeezes even staples' purchasing power and bank asset quality. answers.reasoning: Kalau UST 10Y balik ke 5 persen dan modal global lari ke obligasi AS transmisinya ke IDX lewat tiga jalur. Selisih bunga AS dan RI menyempit sehingga IDR tertekan dan BI naikkan suku bunga acuan demi jaga carry lalu outflow asing menghantam dulu nama paling likuid yaitu BBCA BBRI BMRI dan GOTO. Discount rate naik menekan valuasi growth PE tinggi yang arus kasnya jauh di depan seperti DCII BREN dan PANI. Rotasi defensif versi IDX adalah bank besar dengan margin bunga melebar batu bara berpendapatan dolar sebagai lindung nilai rupiah dan barang konsumen beta rendah. Bank mendominasi indeks dan pendapatan komoditas dolar jadi penyangga saat dolar kuat jadi ini momen geser ke value dividend lokal.

answers.reasoning: A 2008/Maret-2020-style halt on IHSG is a foreign-flow + currency event first, fundamentals second: asing dumps liquid large caps (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/TLKM) to repatriate USD, IDR melemah, and BI is forced to defend the rupiah with rate hikes (or at minimum delay cuts) instead of cutting into the crash — that tightening is the transmission that crushes rate-sensitive property/tech (PANI, GOTO, BUKA) and keeps the bottom from being a clean V. Mathematically you cannot identify the bottom ex-ante: returns in a halt regime are fat-tailed and serially correlated, so "nunggu bottom" is a bet on two correct timing calls (sell-out and buy-in) where missing the 5-10 best rebound days — which historically cluster within weeks of the low — destroys most of the recovery CAGR; DCA mechanically buys more units as price falls and guarantees participation in the rebound, which is why expected-value math favors it for ritel who have no edge on the turn. The IDX-specific kicker is that the highest-conviction averaging targets are not the deepest fallers but the cash-generative dividend compounders whose payout is funded by USD-linked commodity revenue (coal: ADRO/PTBA/ITMG) or by sticky domestic deposit franchises (BBRI/BBNI), because a weak IDR actually inflates rupiah coal/CPO earnings while the dividend yield rises as price falls, paying you to wait. Psychologically DCA also beats waiting because it converts an unbearable single decision under panic into a rules-based schedule, removing the loss-aversion paralysis that makes retail investors sell the bottom and buy back only after the recovery is already priced in.
tool_queries: SELECT t.ticker, v.ret_1y, f.div_yield, f.roe, f.pb FROM returns v JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) JOIN summary t USING(ticker) WHERE f.mcap_tn > 50 AND f.div_yield >= 8 AND v.ret_1y < 0 ORDER BY f.div_yield DESC;  -- fallers that pay you to DCA in

SELECT ticker, AVG(value_rp_bn) adv FROM (SELECT * FROM prices ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 30000) GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY adv DESC LIMIT 25;  -- most-liquid names = where foreign repatriation hits first / where you can DCA without slippage

SELECT b.ticker, SUM(b.net_rp_bn) net20d FROM broker_net b WHERE b.date >= date('now','-20 day') AND b.broker_code IN ('AK','BK','KZ','ZP') GROUP BY b.ticker ORDER BY net20d ASC LIMIT 20;  -- foreign-proxy broker net selling to size the flow shock
screen_stocks(sector IN ['Energi','Keuangan'], div_yield_min=9, mcap_tn_min=20, roe_min=15, sort='div_yield desc')  -- defensive cash-flow DCA universe

picks.rationale: First thing asing dumps in a halt (Rp1.16tn ADV, top foreign holding) so it overshoots on flow not fundamentals; 21% ROE deposit franchise = the highest-quality DCA core to accumulate on forced selling.
10.4% yield + 18% ROE; as price falls the yield rises and the dividend (sticky micro-lending NIM) pays you to average down rather than time the low.
10.7% coal yield where weak-IDR repatriation crash actually inflates rupiah earnings — buy the dividend, not the bottom; cash payout funds your wait through the drawdown.
12.5% yield coal cash machine; USD-priced revenue means an IDR-driven selloff improves the fundamental even as the tape panics — ideal DCA target.
6.8% yield defensive infra with domestic, non-cyclical cashflow; gets sold for liquidity in a halt despite stable earnings, so DCA captures flow-driven mispricing.

The 'wait for bottom' trap — already -36%, rate-sensitive, no dividend to pay you; in a BI-tightening crash it can keep falling, so explicitly NOT a DCA core, kept small/avoided vs the cash compounders.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the crash is a domestic-solvency event rather than a foreign-flow/liquidity event: if BI hikes hard to defend a collapsing IDR and triggers an NPL/credit cycle, the bank and property cashflows that fund the dividends get impaired, dividends get cut, and DCA into "yield" becomes averaging into a value trap (BBRI/BBNI yields are only attractive if the payout survives). Also invalidated for a leveraged retail investor — DCA assumes fresh monthly cash; if income itself is cyclical (commodity-linked) the contributions dry up exactly when prices are lowest, killing the mechanical edge.

answers.category: stagflation hedge — IDX commodity-energy + high-real-yield, not pure equity exit
answers.reasoning: In an Indonesian stagflation, BI is forced to hold its policy rate high to defend the IDR against persistent imported inflation and capital outflow, so the rate-transmission channel punishes the leveraged, rate-sensitive parts of the market (property and tech/growth) and caps bank loan growth — exactly why Properti and Teknologi screen rich and fragile while banks' NIM/credit cycle stalls. The winning channel is hard-asset cash flow: rupiah-reporting coal and metals exporters get a double tailwind because a weak IDR inflates their USD-linked revenue in rupiah terms while elevated commodity prices hold up margins, and they recycle it into the fattest yields on the exchange (BSSR 15.4, GEMS 14.7, ADRO 12.5, PTBA 10.7, PGAS 9.9). That dividend stream is the real-return hedge equities can offer when SBN real yields are compressed by above-target CPI; the play is to rotate within equity toward USD-revenue + high-payout names, not to abandon equity wholesale. Gold (ANTM, or the EMAS/MDKA complex) is the genuine non-equity insurance leg and deserves a sleeve, while the broad market's energy sector already leads trailing-1y (Energi 120 vs Keuangan 47), confirming the rotation is underway. Net: barbell IDR-hedged energy/metals yield against a gold sleeve; underweight property, tech, and rate-sensitive banks.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector IN ('Energi'), dividend_yield >= 8, mcap_tn >= 5, sort=dividend_yield desc) -- surface USD-revenue coal exporters with the fattest payouts (expect ADRO, PTBA, BSSR, GEMS, AADI, PGAS)

SELECT f.ticker, f.sector, f.div_yield, f.roe, r.ret_1y FROM fundamentals f JOIN returns r ON f.ticker=r.ticker WHERE f.sector='Energi' AND f.div_yield>=8 ORDER BY f.div_yield DESC; -- cross-check yield vs trailing-1y momentum to avoid value traps (flag BYAN at -46%)

SELECT ticker, sector, pb, ret_1y FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Teknologi') AND mcap_tn>=5 ORDER BY pb ASC; -- confirm the rate-sensitive shorts/underweights (LPKR 0.25, BSDE 0.37, GOTO -36, BUKA) are the stagflation losers

SELECT o.ticker, o.foreign_pct, r.ret_1y FROM ownership o JOIN returns r ON o.ticker=r.ticker WHERE o.ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','ADRO','PTBA','TLKM') ORDER BY o.foreign_pct DESC; -- gauge foreign-flow exposure on the gold/metals + defensive sleeve, since high-foreign names bleed first on outflows

picks.rationale: USD-linked coal revenue + weak-IDR uplift, 12.5% yield — core stagflation cash-flow hedge with deep liquidity.
State coal, 10.7% yield, low-cost domestic-plus-export mix; pays through the cycle when growth stalls.
Top energy dividend at 15.4% — highest real-yield instrument on the exchange if margins hold.
Gas utility, 9.9% yield, regulated USD-linked tariffs — defensive energy income, less price-cyclical than coal.
Gold + nickel, the listed proxy for the non-equity gold-insurance leg; most liquid metals name (Rp496bn/day).
Defensive infra with pricing power and 6.8% yield — the equity ballast that absorbs the property/tech derating.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the regime is disinflationary recession rather than true stagflation: coal/CPO prices and global demand roll over, collapsing the very commodity cash flows the yields depend on (BYAN already down 46% trailing-1y shows the value-trap path). It also breaks if BI is forced to cut despite high CPI or the IDR stabilizes — that re-rates property/tech/banks and undercuts the weak-IDR revenue tailwind. Dividend yields here are trailing and partly unreliable per the brief, so a payout cut on a coal price drop is the fastest way to turn these income picks into capital-loss traps; size the gold/SBN sleeve as the hedge-of-the-hedge.

answers.category: balance-sheet-quality / credit-crunch defensive screen (low DER, cash-rich) + USD-debt refinancing risk flag
answers.reasoning: Transmisi credit crunch ke IDX jalan lewat tiga pipa: (1) BI harus jaga spread terhadap Fed untuk tahan IDR, jadi saat likuiditas global kering BI cenderung hold/hike, biaya rollover utang naik dan emiten yang refinancing 2026-2027 kena re-pricing kupon; (2) IDR melemah menggandakan beban emiten dengan utang USD tapi pendapatan rupiah — ini menghantam properti (PANI, BSDE, CTRA, SMRA, LPKR yang funding capex jangka panjang) dan sebagian energi/petrochem yang capex-nya dolar, sementara eksportir komoditas dengan revenue USD justru ter-hedge natural; (3) foreign outflow lari ke nama paling likuid/asing-heavy dulu (BBCA, BBRI, BMRI, plus BNLI/BTPN/BNII yang foreign ownership ~98%), menekan harga terlepas dari fundamental. Survivor-nya adalah net-cash atau near-net-cash dengan free cash flow positif dan dividen ter-cover: kas batubara (PTBA, ITMG, BSSR, ADRO) yang biasanya net-cash dan revenue USD, plus consumer staples defensif (SIDO hampir tanpa utang, UNVR, ICBP) yang cash-generative dan demand inelastis. Yang bahaya: leverage tinggi + utang USD + revenue rupiah + refinancing wall — kombinasi paling mematikan ada di grup Barito/petrochem dan properti rate-sensitive, di mana DER tinggi bertemu mismatch mata uang.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(filters: debt_to_equity < 40 AND total_cash > total_debt AND free_cash_flow > 0 AND market_cap > 5e12, sort: total_cash_per_share DESC) -- net-cash, FCF-positive survivors atas view `latest`

run_query: SELECT s.ticker, c.sector, s.debt_to_equity, s.total_debt, s.total_cash, (s.total_cash - s.total_debt) AS net_cash, s.current_ratio, s.free_cash_flow, s.dividend_yield FROM summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE s.market_cap > 5e12 AND s.debt_to_equity < 50 AND s.total_cash >= s.total_debt ORDER BY net_cash DESC LIMIT 30;

run_query: SELECT f.ticker, c.sector, f.financial_report_currency, s.debt_to_equity, s.total_debt, s.current_ratio FROM fundamentals f JOIN summary s USING(ticker) JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE f.financial_report_currency LIKE '%USD%' AND s.debt_to_equity > 80 ORDER BY s.total_debt DESC; -- flag USD-reporting / USD-debt names with high leverage

run_query: SELECT ticker, debt_to_equity, total_debt, total_cash, current_ratio, quick_ratio, interest? FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('BRPT','TPIA','PANI','BSDE','CTRA','SMRA','LPKR','BREN') ORDER BY debt_to_equity DESC; -- verify the at-risk leveraged/USD-debt set before flagging

picks.rationale: Kas batubara klasik: historis near-net-cash, revenue USD natural-hedge terhadap IDR lemah, yield ~10.7% ter-cover FCF — survivor ideal saat likuiditas kering.
Net-cash murni, hampir tanpa utang dan tanpa refinancing wall; cash pile + revenue dolar bikin imun terhadap kenaikan biaya rollover dan IDR depresiasi.
Consumer staple/kesehatan dengan balance sheet praktis tanpa utang, ROE ~37%, demand inelastis — defensive cash-generative murni tanpa FX risk.
Staples defensif, brand pricing power, cash conversion tinggi; meski sudah jatuh, kebutuhan dasar tetap jalan saat resesi — bukan emiten yang gagal refinancing.
BAHAYA: petrochem Barito, DER tinggi dan capex/utang berdenominasi USD sementara siklus margin tertekan — kombinasi leverage + mismatch mata uang paling rentan refi.
BAHAYA: properti mcap besar dengan ROE rendah (~7%) dan funding capex jangka panjang yang rate-sensitive; spread BI tinggi + likuiditas kering menaikkan biaya rollover tajam.

answers.risk: Tesis batal kalau Fed pivot dovish lebih cepat dari ekspektasi: likuiditas global pulih, BI bisa potong, IDR menguat, dan justru nama leveraged/properti (BRPT, PANI, BSDE) rally paling kencang sementara cash-rich defensive underperform. Risiko data: ratio yf (debt_to_equity, total_debt) untuk emiten IDR campur skala IDR/USD dan tidak reliabel per data-contract — net-cash dan DER WAJIB diverifikasi ke laporan keuangan aktual + cek financial_report_currency, bukan ditelan mentah. Juga: kas batubara bergantung harga komoditas — kalau credit crunch menyeret demand global dan harga coal anjlok, FCF dan dividen PTBA/ITMG/ADRO ikut terkompresi meski balance sheet kuat.

answers.reasoning: Indonesia is a net crude importer, so an oil spike is not a terms-of-trade windfall — it widens the current-account deficit and revives fuel-subsidy fiscal pressure, which forces BI to stay hawkish to defend an already-pressured IDR while the Fed pulls global USD liquidity and foreign funds exit the most liquid IDX names (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI are the foreigners' ATM). The hardest triple-whammy lands on rate-sensitive, IDR-funded, China-demand-linked sectors: Properti & Real Estat (high leverage, every BI hold compounds funding cost) and base-metals/nickel (ANTM, AMMN, BRMS) which take the direct China trade-war demand hit just as financing tightens; growth-tech (GOTO, BUKA) gets repriced by the discount-rate shock and is already the worst trailing cohort. The local safe havens are USD-earning thermal-coal dividend machines (ADRO, PTBA, ITMG, BYAN-complex) whose rupiah earnings actually rise as IDR weakens and whose thermal-coal price tends to track the energy complex higher, plus gold exposure via ANTM/EMAS as the genuine geopolitical hedge, and defensive consumer staples with pricing power and low foreign float (UNVR, ICBP, SIDO). Net: short the rate-and-China-beta complex, hide in dollarized coal cash-flow and gold, with staples as the low-beta ballast.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector='Energi', subindustry IN ('coal','thermal coal'), min_dividend_yield=8, min_mcap_tn=10) — pull USD-revenue coal dividend payers (ADRO, PTBA, ITMG, BSSR, GEMS, AADI) ranked by yield and ROE as the safe-haven candidates

SELECT t.ticker, t.sector, l.mcap_tn, f.debt_to_equity, f.pb, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN fundamentals f USING(ticker) JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE t.sector='Properti & Real Estat' OR ticker IN ('GOTO','BUKA','DCII') ORDER BY f.debt_to_equity DESC — quantify rate/leverage blast radius on property + growth-tech

SELECT ticker, foreign_pct, avg_daily_value_bn FROM ownership o JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI') ORDER BY avg_daily_value_bn DESC — measure foreign-outflow vulnerability of mega-cap banks (most-liquid = first sold)

SELECT ticker, sector, ret_1y FROM returns r JOIN latest USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('ANTM','AMMN','BRMS','NICL') OR sector='Barang Baku' ORDER BY ret_1y — isolate China-trade-war-exposed base-metals vs gold names (split ANTM gold vs nickel beta)

picks.rationale: State-backed thermal-coal pure play, 10.7% yield, USD revenue that rises with a weaker IDR and a coal price that tracks the oil/energy complex higher — top local safe haven.
Largest coal dividend machine at 12.5% yield; dollarized cash flow and balance-sheet strength make it the prime hedge against IDR depreciation in the triple shock.
Two-faced exposure: nickel side gets hit by the China trade-war demand shock, but its gold segment is the genuine geopolitical safe haven — own it for the gold optionality, hedge the nickel beta.
Defensive staple with 107% ROE and pricing power; low-beta domestic-demand ballast that holds up when foreigners dump liquid cyclicals and banks.
Triple-whammy casualty: unprofitable growth-tech crushed by the discount-rate/Fed-hawkish shock, already -36% trailing — the clearest avoid/short on the rate leg.
Leveraged property name (P/B 0.55) that compounds funding-cost pain every time BI holds to defend the IDR — core of the rate-sensitive sector to underweight.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if the oil spike is brief and BI is allowed to cut alongside a dovish Fed pivot — that flips the entire setup: IDR rallies, foreign flows return to banks/property/tech, and the coal-dividend hedge underperforms as thermal coal de-rates. Second invalidator: if the oil move is supply-driven but coal decouples (China demand collapse from the trade war drags thermal coal down with metals), the safe-haven coal trade fails and only gold (ANTM gold segment, EMAS) and low-float staples remain defensive. Also note coal names like BYAN already printed -46% trailing, so the dividend-hedge entry is timing-sensitive, not a one-way bet.

answers.reasoning: IHSG sekarang adalah pasar yang didominasi bank (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/BBNI ~40%+ bobot dan likuiditas) plus siklus komoditas (batubara, nikel, CPO), jadi "crash" vs "koreksi sehat" bergantung pada dua transmisi: (1) jalur suku bunga/IDR — kalau Fed higher-for-longer + defisit fiskal mendorong outflow asing, BI terpaksa hold/naikkan rate demi stabilkan IDR, yang langsung memukul valuasi growth/properti rate-sensitive (GOTO, BUKA, DCII, BSDE, CTRA, PANI) dan menekan NIM bank; (2) jalur komoditas — harga batubara/nikel/CPO yang melemah memangkas EPS energi & barang baku (lihat BYAN -46%, TPIA -42% trailing-1y) tapi yield batubara yang tebal (ADRO 12.5%, PTBA 10.7%, BSSR 15.4%) jadi bantalan kalau harga cuma sideways. Logika positioning: jangan all-in cash, pakai barbell — inti defensif berbasis arus kas (consumer staples ROE tinggi + telco/utility dividen + bank tier-1) yang tetap menghasilkan walau IHSG flat, plus sisakan cash sebagai dry powder yang dikerahkan bertahap saat foreign net-sell mereda. Alokasi optimal bersifat rule-based, bukan tebakan: anchor ke band 30-50% cash dan geser ke ekstrem (50%+ cash) hanya jika early-warning menyala bersamaan, karena di IDX outflow asing + IDR melemah + spread obligasi melebar adalah trio yang historisnya mendahului drawdown tajam; satu sinyal saja biasanya cuma koreksi sehat.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Infrastruktur','Keuangan') AND market_cap > 5e12 AND dividend_yield > 5 AND return_on_equity > 15 AND beta < 1 ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC -- cari defensive cash-flow core: yield tinggi, ROE solid, low-beta

SELECT s.ticker, s.beta, s.dividend_yield, s.payout_ratio, s.debt_to_equity, s.current_ratio, r.ret_1y FROM summary s JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE s.ticker IN ('UNVR','ICBP','SIDO','TLKM','PGAS','POWR','BBCA','BBRI','PTBA','ADRO','BSSR','GEMS') ORDER BY s.beta ASC -- verifikasi low-beta + balance-sheet sehat untuk inti defensif

SELECT date, SUM(foreign_buy - foreign_sell)/1e9 AS net_foreign_bn FROM eod_summary WHERE date >= current_date - INTERVAL 60 DAY GROUP BY date ORDER BY date -- proxy foreign flow harian (early-warning #1): net-sell beruntun = de-risk

SELECT bn.broker_code, SUM(bn.net_value)/1e9 AS net_bn FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.date >= current_date - INTERVAL 20 DAY AND bn.broker_code IN ('AK','BK','KZ','ZP') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 -- broker asing (UBS/JPM/CLSA/Maybank) net flow: konfirmasi tekanan jual asing

picks.rationale: Bank tier-1 paling likuid (Rp1.16tn/hari), ROE 21%, yield 5.1% — anchor equity yang paling tahan banting dan jadi proksi paling bersih untuk arah foreign flow di IHSG.
Telco/infra defensif, yield 6.8% + ROE 18%, arus kas regulated yang stabil saat IHSG flat — bayar Anda untuk menunggu tanpa ketinggalan kalau cuma koreksi sehat.
Consumer staples (jamu/herbal) ROE 37%, low-debt, permintaan inelastis — kompounder defensif untuk porsi inti yang tidak rate-sensitive maupun komoditas-sensitif.
Batubara state-owned dengan yield 10.7% dan neraca bersih; bantalan dividen tebal kalau harga batubara sideways, tapi dikira bagian 'income' bukan growth karena risiko siklus komoditas.
Energi yield 12.5%, kas kuat — high-carry income sleeve; pasangkan dengan PTBA sebagai keranjang dividen batubara, bukan taruhan harga komoditas tunggal.
Bank dengan yield 10.4% + ROE 18%, likuiditas Rp909bn/hari; dry-powder deployment target — tambah bertahap justru saat foreign net-sell mereda karena valuasi sudah memuat pesimisme.

answers.risk: Tesis barbell ini gagal jika terjadi shock korelasi-satu: outflow asing tajam memukul SEMUA blue-chip likuid (BBCA/BBRI/TLKM) bersamaan karena merekalah yang paling mudah dijual asing, jadi 'defensif' tidak melindungi di hari crash sesungguhnya. Trigger pembatalan/de-risk agresif (geser ke 50%+ cash): foreign net-sell beruntun >2 minggu DI ATAS broker asing (AK/BK/KZ) + IDR tembus level kritis memaksa BI hawkish + harga batubara/nikel/CPO breakdown — ketiganya menyala bersama. Jika hanya SATU sinyal (mis. foreign keluar tapi IDR & komoditas stabil), itu kemungkinan besar koreksi sehat: tahan inti, jangan over-hedge ke cash dan malah ketinggalan rebound. Risiko sekunder: yield batubara dua-digit (PTBA/ADRO) adalah yield trap kalau harga batubara strukturally turun — jangan over-weight income sleeve melebihi inti staples/telco.

answers.category: rate-sensitive sectors under a BI defensive-hike / recession scenario
answers.reasoning: When a global risk-off shock hits, foreign investors flee IDR assets (banks/large-caps are the most foreign-owned and most liquid: BBCA, BBRI, BMRI), IDR sells off, and BI is forced to hike into a recession to defend the rupiah and cap imported inflation — exactly the 2013/2018 playbook. This crushes rate-sensitive, high-leverage sectors fastest: property developers (BSDE, CTRA, SMRA) sell long-dated units on KPR mortgage financing, so a higher BI rate directly raises mortgage rates, kills marketing sales, and inflates their own refinancing cost — and they already screen as distressed-cheap (BSDE P/B 0.37, CTRA 0.55, LPKR 0.25, SMRA 0.47). Autos (ASII) get a double hit: ~60-70% of Indonesian car sales are credit-financed, so higher rates throttle 4W/2W volumes via Astra's own multifinance arm, while a weak IDR lifts imported CKD/component costs and squeezes margins — though ASII's commodity legs (UNTR/coal, CPO) and 6.7% yield partially cushion it. The contrarian window is real but conditional: these names get oversold to trough P/B, and the buy signal is not the trough rate but the first credible IDR stabilization / BI pause, since property and autos are early-cyclicals that re-rate hard on the rate-cut anticipation, not the cut itself.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, pb, roe, dividend_yield, trailing_1y_return FROM latest WHERE sector LIKE 'Properti%' AND mcap_tn > 5 ORDER BY pb ASC;
screen_stocks(sector IN ['Properti & Real Estat','Perindustrian'], mcap_tn > 5, pb < 0.6, sort_by='pb', order='asc')

SELECT b.ticker, SUM(b.net_value) AS foreign_net FROM broker_net b WHERE b.ticker IN ('BSDE','CTRA','SMRA','ASII','LPKR') AND b.date >= date('now','-30 day') GROUP BY b.ticker ORDER BY foreign_net ASC;
SELECT ticker, debt_to_equity, interest_coverage, current_ratio FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('BSDE','CTRA','SMRA','ASII','LPKR','PANI') ORDER BY debt_to_equity DESC;

picks.rationale: Deepest-discount large property at P/B 0.37 with the biggest landbank — maximum drawdown in the hike, maximum re-rate beta when BI pivots; core contrarian buy.
Best-managed developer with recurring mall/hospital income to cushion the KPR slowdown; P/B 0.55 entry, more resilient through the rate trough than peers.
The rate+IDR double-hit name (credit-financed autos), but 6.7% yield, P/B-cheap, and UNTR/coal + CPO legs cushion the downside — a leveraged play on the eventual rate cut.
P/B 0.47, high financial leverage means it falls hardest in the hike and rebounds hardest on the pivot — higher-beta version of the BSDE/CTRA trade.
Defensive hedge inside the basket: Astra's heavy-equipment/coal arm with a 6.7% yield and 15% ROE that benefits from a weak IDR (USD coal revenue), offsetting the auto-cycle drag.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if BI is forced to keep rates high for longer than expected (a sustained IDR/balance-of-payments crisis or persistent imported inflation rather than a quick defend-and-pause). Property and autos have high leverage, so a prolonged high-rate regime turns the "cheap P/B" into a value trap — marketing sales collapse, refinancing risk bites, and the names keep de-rating. Heavy foreign outflows (these are foreign-favored, liquid stocks) can also overshoot fundamentals on the downside; the only valid entry trigger is confirmed IDR stabilization plus a credible BI pause, not the first dip.

answers.category: Structural secular-growth accumulation basket for a recession buying-the-dip, 5-10y horizon
answers.reasoning: A recession sell-off in IDX is overwhelmingly a foreign-flow + rate event, not a domestic-demand collapse: ~40% foreign-owned float means a global risk-off de-rates BBCA/BBRI/BMRI and the GOTO/DCII growth complex first and hardest, even though Indonesian nominal consumption keeps compounding on a young, formalizing population. The transmission for the buy thesis is the BI pivot — a recession forces Bank Indonesia to cut to defend growth, which (a) re-rates the big banks (cheaper funding, NIM stabilizes, credit cost peaks then falls) and reflates rate-sensitive digital/data-center and healthcare names, while a weaker IDR is actually a tailwind for USD-priced nickel/gold miners. Banks are the only liquid, ROE-durable (BBCA 21%, BBRI 18%, BMRI 19%) way to own the formalization + digital-banking secular trend at scale, and they pay you 5-10% to wait; nickel/gold (ANTM, MDKA, NCKL) is the structural EV/reserve-asset leg that the data shows held above its 200-day even in this drawdown, so it de-rates less in panic. Pure-defensive secular compounders (SIDO healthcare, 37% ROE; consumer health) are the ballast that lets you average down without portfolio drawdown forcing you out. The catch the data exposes: as of the latest bar nearly every name is still below its 50-day MA (BBCA -28.8% from 52w high and -5.8% vs MA50, BBRI -22.6%/-10.6%), i.e. still falling — so this is an accumulation list to scale into, not a lump-sum, and the entry rule has to be mechanical.
tool_queries: WITH p AS (SELECT ticker,date,close, max(close) OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date ROWS 251 PRECEDING) hi52, avg(close) OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date ROWS 49 PRECEDING) ma50, avg(close) OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date ROWS 199 PRECEDING) ma200, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY ticker ORDER BY date DESC) rn FROM prices) SELECT ticker, 100*(close/hi52-1) dd_from_high, 100*(close/ma50-1) vs_ma50, 100*(close/ma200-1) vs_ma200 FROM p WHERE rn=1 ORDER BY dd_from_high -- screen for deep drawdown but find names reclaiming MA50 (entry trigger, avoid falling knife)

SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe, div_yield, pb FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Keuangan','Kesehatan','Barang Baku') AND mcap_tn > 5 AND roe > 15 ORDER BY roe DESC -- durable-ROE survivors in the secular sectors
SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','SIDO','ANTM','MDKA','NCKL') ORDER BY ret_1y -- rank discount depth across the basket
screen_stocks(sector='Keuangan', min_mcap_tn=100, min_roe=15, min_yield=5) -- liquid, well-capitalized banks that pay you to wait through the recession

picks.rationale: Best-in-class deposit franchise (ROE 21%), the cleanest proxy for Indonesia formalization + digital banking; -28.8% off its 52w high and below MA50 = scale in on a BI-cut/MA50-reclaim, not all at once.
Micro/MSME lender with 10.4% yield paying you to wait; most foreign-flow-sensitive of the big banks so it overshoots down in panic and rerates hardest on the rate pivot.
Corporate/transaction banking, ROE 19%, the most resilient in the data (-14% from high, near MA200) — the lower-beta core bank holding for the accumulation sleeve.
State nickel+gold miner, the liquid EV-supply-chain + safe-haven barbell; held above its 200-day through the drawdown and a weaker recession-IDR lifts its USD-priced output.
Gold + nickel (HPAL) growth story tied to the EV battery chain; still above MA200 (+27%), so add on pullbacks toward the rising average rather than chasing.
Integrated nickel/HPAL play on the structural EV-battery thesis, -27.5% from high near MA200 — the most direct downstream-nickel exposure in the liquid set.
Defensive healthcare/herbal compounder, 37% ROE and only -8% off its high (basing at MA50/MA200) — the recession-proof ballast that funds averaging into the cyclicals.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the recession is a stagflation/IDR-crisis rather than a clean disinflationary slowdown: if inflation forces BI to hike (or hold high) to defend a collapsing rupiah, banks face peaking credit costs with no NIM relief and the rate-sensitive re-rating never comes — the falling knife keeps falling. Nickel is the other invalidator: a structurally oversupplied global market (Indonesian + Chinese smelter glut) can keep prices below cash cost regardless of macro, so ANTM/MDKA/NCKL are a commodity bet, not a guaranteed secular winner. Mechanically, do not lump-sum here — the data shows most names still below MA50; only start each tranche when a name reclaims its 50-day MA on rising volume, keep dry powder, and size so a further 30% drawdown does not force you out.

answers.reasoning: This is a glide-path problem, not a timing problem: with 8 years to pensiun and 80% in rate-sensitive growth (GOTO is on the brief's worst-1y list at -36%, the whole Teknologi sector lags at +68% vs Energi/Konsumen leaders), the dominant risk is sequence-of-returns, so the answer is a phased 12-18 month rotation, not a single rebalance day. The Indonesia macro setup actually favors moving now: BI's easing cycle (rate cuts to defend growth as Fed pressure on IDR eases) lifts bond prices via duration and re-rates the high-yield defensive equities whose payout is bond-like — TLKM, telco/infra, and the big banks BBRI/BBNI whose 9-10% yields are real (not cyclically inflated). Crucially I cap coal/energy exposure: BSSR/GEMS/ADRO/PTBA screen at 11-15% yield but that yield is a peak-cycle artifact of high thermal-coal prices and ~100% payout — as the coal cycle rolls over those dividends compress, so they are a sweetener, not the core. The transmission to watch is foreign flows: BI cuts + a stable Rupiah pull foreign money back into IDX large-cap banks and TLKM (the most-liquid, foreign-owned names), which is exactly the defensive ballast a near-retiree wants, while a fiscal-deficit scare or IDR break would reverse it. Net: glide GOTO down on strength into bank/telco dividend compounders plus a government-bond (SUN/FR) sleeve laddered to capture the rate-cut duration, dollar-cost-averaged over 4-6 tranches to neutralize the volatile-IHSG timing fear.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: filters=[{field:'dividend_yield',op:'between',value:[5,11]},{field:'market_cap',op:'>',value:50e12},{field:'return_on_equity',op:'>',value:12},{field:'beta',op:'<',value:1.1}] sort={field:'market_cap',desc:true} -- find large-cap, profitable, lower-beta dividend payers for the defensive core (catches BBRI/BBNI/TLKM/ASII/UNTR, screens out cyclical coal)

run_query: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.market_cap, l.dividend_yield, l.return_on_equity, l.beta, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.dividend_yield >= 5 AND l.market_cap > 5e13 ORDER BY l.beta ASC NULLS LAST -- rank defensive yield candidates by stability and momentum before sizing tranches

run_query: SELECT ticker, sum(foreign_buy - foreign_sell) AS net_foreign FROM eod_summary WHERE date >= current_date - INTERVAL '60 days' AND ticker IN ('BBCA','BBRI','BMRI','BBNI','TLKM','ASII','GOTO') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY net_foreign DESC -- confirm foreign flows are rotating INTO defensive large-caps (the BI-cut transmission) and OUT of GOTO before timing exit tranches

run_query: SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector = 'Energi' AND l.dividend_yield > 9 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC -- isolate coal/energy yield names (BSSR/GEMS/ADRO/PTBA) to size as a capped cyclical sweetener, not core

picks.rationale: Core defensive anchor: 477tn mcap, ROE 18, real 10.4% yield from the most-liquid bank franchise; prime beneficiary of BI cuts and returning foreign flows — the bank-dominance pillar of any IDX pension book.
Defensive infra/telco compounder, 285tn mcap, ROE 18, 6.8% yield with bond-like cash flows and low cyclicality — the lowest-beta large-cap income sleeve to replace GOTO.
Second bank leg for diversification: 9.4% yield, ROE 12, large-cap liquidity; cheap-ish and rate-cut-sensitive, spreads single-name bank risk vs concentrating in BBRI.
Diversified industrial/auto blue-chip, 238tn mcap, 6.7% yield, ROE 14 — broadens the defensive book beyond banks/telco into domestic-consumption cyclicality with a solid payout.
Capped coal-dividend sweetener (10.7% yield) for near-term income — explicitly sized small because the 10%+ yield is a peak-coal-cycle artifact that compresses as thermal prices normalize.

The position to glide OUT of, not into: on the worst-1y list at -36%, rate-sensitive zero-dividend tech; trim in 4-6 tranches on strength to fund the dividend+bond rotation rather than dumping into a volatile tape.

answers.risk: Main risk is the BI-easing thesis reversing: a Rupiah break (Fed staying higher-for-longer) or a fiscal-deficit/ratings scare would trigger foreign outflows from exactly these large-cap banks and TLKM, hitting the new defensive core AND bond prices simultaneously — the diversification fails when rates and FX move against you together. Secondary risk: the coal/energy yields (PTBA and the BSSR/GEMS/ADRO complex) are cyclical peaks tied to thermal-coal prices and ~full payout ratios; a coal-price rollover cuts those dividends hard, so over-weighting them for headline yield would re-introduce the cyclicality the glide-path is meant to remove. Invalidated if foreign flows stay net-sellers of IDX large-caps and BI pauses cutting — then favor shortening the bond ladder and holding more cash through the volatility.

answers.reasoning: Net 7% post-tax needs ~8.8% gross (10% final WHT on Indonesian dividends per UU HPP), which forces the high-yield buckets: coal/energy, banks, and a few staples. The dividend-trap filter is structural: coal payers (PTBA, ADRO, BSSR, GEMS, AADI) print yield off peak-2022-24 thermal-coal FCF that mean-reverts with the China/Newcastle price cycle, so their payout is cyclical, not a floor — high yield there is the market discounting falling forward earnings, not a gift. The durable monthly-income core is BI-rate-sensitive cash-flow compounders: state banks (BBRI, BBNI) run 18-19% ROE with formal high-payout policies, and regulated/defensive cash machines (TLKM telco, UNTR via Komatsu+coal-services annuity, ASII conglomerate) pay 6-7% off recurring earnings with low payout strain. With BI holding rates elevated to defend a weak IDR and foreign flows thin, rate-sensitive property/tech offer no income, so the safe income sleeve is banks + telco/infra + defensive staples, using coal only as a tactical, position-sized cyclical kicker. Sustainability test = multi-year recurring dividends in prices.dividends, payout < ~70% of earnings, positive trailing return (no falling-knife trap), and ROE that funds the payout.
tool_queries: screen_stocks {"filters":[{"field":"dividend_yield","op":">","value":8.8},{"field":"return_on_equity","op":">","value":0.12},{"field":"market_cap","op":">","value":5e12}],"sort":"dividend_yield","limit":30} -- gross>=8.8% (net 7% after 10% WHT), ROE funds the payout, liquid large caps only

SELECT ticker, COUNT(DISTINCT strftime('%Y', date)) AS years_paid, SUM(dividends) AS total_div, MAX(dividends) AS max_single FROM prices WHERE dividends > 0 GROUP BY ticker HAVING years_paid >= 4 ORDER BY years_paid DESC; -- recurring multi-year payers vs one-off special-dividend names (low years_paid, max_single ~= total_div)

SELECT l.ticker, l.sector, l.dividend_yield, l.return_on_equity, l.price_to_book, r.ret_1y, r.cagr_3y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.dividend_yield > 6 AND r.ret_1y > 0 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC; -- screen out dividend traps: yield high BUT price not falling (ret_1y>0), positive 3y CAGR

SELECT ticker, sector, dividend_yield, return_on_equity, profit_margins FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('BBRI','BBNI','TLKM','UNTR','ASII','PTBA','PGAS','BJTM','BJBR') ORDER BY dividend_yield DESC; -- verify yield/ROE/margin on the income shortlist before committing

picks.rationale: State bank, 10.4% yield + 18% ROE; high-payout policy on recurring micro-lending earnings — the cleanest large-cap net-7% income anchor, not a trap.
State bank, 9.4% yield + 12% ROE, P/B-cheap; durable payout from recurring banking NIM, core defensive income holding.
Telco utility, 6.8% yield + 18% ROE off recurring connectivity ARPU; sub-8.8% so pair with a coal kicker, but it's the most sustainable single name here.
Komatsu distribution + coal-services + gold annuity, 6.7% yield + 15% ROE, modest payout; diversified recurring cash flow, lower cyclicality than pure coal.
Coal yield 10.7%, ROE-strong, state-owned with consistent high payout — use as a position-sized cyclical income kicker, NOT the core; yield falls if Newcastle coal rolls over.
Regional development bank, 9.6% yield; sticky provincial-government deposit/payroll franchise makes payout recurring — a higher-yield bank diversifier vs the big-3.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if BI cuts aggressively to chase growth — that compresses bank NIM (BBRI/BBNI/BJTM) and the income core weakens — or, conversely, if a thermal-coal price slump cuts the cyclical payers' (PTBA, and the ADRO/BSSR/GEMS/AADI cohort) earnings so the headline 10%+ yields evaporate as one-cycle highs, the classic dividend trap. Single biggest invalidator: a fiscal/tax change to the 10% final dividend WHT or a payout-ratio cut at the SOE banks under government dividend-grab pressure, either of which sinks net yield below the 7% target. Verify each name's prices.dividends history is multi-year recurring (not a single special dividend) and that ret_1y is not deeply negative before buying.

answers.reasoning: PER 4-6x di saham komoditas hampir selalu trailing multiple atas peak earnings, jadi pertanyaannya bukan "murah?" tapi "murah pada laba siklus berapa?". Di Indonesia transmisinya spesifik: harga batu bara Newcastle/ICI sudah turun dari puncak 2022, sementara permintaan ekspor terbesar (China property + India) melemah, dan rupiah lemah (BI rate masih tinggi ~5.5-6% menjaga IDR) justru menahan margin emiten karena revenue USD tapi biaya royalti/PNBP dan DMO domestik naik — fiskal pemerintah makin agresif memungut royalti progresif yang langsung memotong free cash flow tambang. Itu sebabnya Energi rata-rata +120% setahun (sinyal cycle-elevated, bukan awal siklus) padahal BYAN -46% dan TPIA -42% sudah mulai re-rate turun: pasar mulai mendiskon normalisasi earnings sebelum laba akuntansinya jatuh. Cara membedakan cheap vs cyclically cheap: low P/E + low P/B + net cash + posisi biaya kuartil terbawah (cash cost rendah) = deep value sejati; low P/E tapi P/B tinggi dan ROE jauh di atas mid-cycle = jebakan peak-earnings yang akan mean-revert. Timing yang benar bukan saat PER terlihat termurah (itu justru puncak laba), melainkan saat harga komoditas sudah kapitulasi, PER terlihat tinggi/negatif karena laba anjlok, tetapi P/B mendekati replacement value dan dividen masih bertahan — beli kelelahan harga, bukan kelelahan multiple.
tool_queries: SELECT f.ticker, l.sector, f.pe, f.pb, f.roe, f.div_yield, f.net_debt_to_equity, r.ret_1y FROM fundamentals f JOIN latest l ON f.ticker=l.ticker JOIN returns r ON f.ticker=r.ticker WHERE l.sector IN ('Energi','Barang Baku') AND f.pe BETWEEN 3 AND 7 AND l.mcap_tn > 5 ORDER BY f.pb ASC;

screen_stocks(sector in ['Energi','Barang Baku'], pe_max=7, pb_max=1.2, net_cash=true, div_yield_min=8, mcap_tn_min=5) -- isolate low-P/E names that are ALSO low-P/B + net cash, the real-value filter vs peak-earnings traps

SELECT ticker, AVG(close) FILTER (WHERE date >= date('now','-30 day')) AS px_now, MAX(close) AS px_peak, AVG(close) FILTER (WHERE date >= date('now','-30 day'))/MAX(close) AS drawdown FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('BYAN','PTBA','ADRO','AADI','BSSR','GEMS','TAPG','ANTM','SRTG','NICL') GROUP BY ticker ORDER BY drawdown ASC; -- find who has already capitulated vs who is still near cycle highs

SELECT ticker, period, net_income, revenue FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('PTBA','ADRO','AADI','BYAN','TAPG') ORDER BY ticker, period; -- reconstruct multi-year earnings to normalize mid-cycle EPS and compute forward PER on through-cycle earnings, not trailing peak

picks.rationale: Deep-value sejati bukan trap: low-cost mine-mouth, net cash, BUMN dividen 10.7% berkelanjutan; pasokan domestik PLN (DMO) memberi floor volume saat harga ekspor jatuh.
Cash cost kuartil bawah, neraca net cash dan yield 12.5%; cocok untuk dibeli saat harga batu bara kapitulasi karena bertahan di siklus turun, bukan saat PER terlihat termurah.

Justru contoh peak-earnings re-rate: ROE 31% tapi sudah -46% setahun dan yield turun ke 3.7% — pasar mendiskon normalisasi laba; tunggu P/B mendekati replacement value, jangan kejar PER murahnya sekarang.
CPO punya struktur siklus beda dari batu bara (biofuel B40/mandat domestik = demand floor); ROE 34, yield 8.9% — diversifikasi dari risiko harga termal, lebih dekat ke value struktural.
Proxy nikel/komoditas via holdco dengan P/B 0.41 — diskon NAV besar memberi margin of safety jika ingin eksposur metals tanpa membayar peak-earnings multiple langsung.

Spin-off batu bara Adaro dengan yield 9.9%; gunakan sebagai konfirmasi kapitulasi — jika dividen tetap utuh saat harga turun, sinyal margin biaya tahan banting (cheap), jika dividen dipangkas itu konfirmasi value trap.

answers.risk: Tesis batal jika harga batu bara/nikel/CPO tidak sekadar normalisasi tapi masuk siklus turun struktural (demand China lemah berkepanjangan + akselerasi energi terbarukan), karena PER 4-6x akan melar ke 15-25x saat laba anjlok 50-70% dan dividen dipotong — itu definisi value trap. Pemicu lain: royalti/PNBP progresif dan DMO yang lebih ketat memotong FCF emiten, plus rupiah yang justru menguat (BI memangkas rate) akan menekan revenue rupiah eksportir. Sinyal invalidasi konkret: dividen dipangkas, P/B naik bukan turun (harga jatuh lebih lambat dari ekuitas), dan cash cost emiten naik mendekati harga jual spot.

answers.reasoning: Konteks makro IHSG sekarang condong ke late-cycle momentum, bukan reversal buta. Energi (+120% 1y) dan Barang Baku/metals lagi memimpin karena siklus komoditas: harga batu bara/nikel/CPO yang masih tinggi mengalir langsung ke FCF emiten coal (BYAN, GEMS, ADRO) dan smelter/gold (ANTM, AMMN), dan transmisi ini diperkuat IDR yang lemah karena 70-90% revenue mereka USD sementara cost rupiah. Tapi rotasi BI rate yang mulai turun adalah variabel kunci: tiap pemangkasan menekan cost of equity dan memutar flow ke nama rate-sensitive (properti, tech, growth) dan bank big-cap yang dominan di likuiditas (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI = 4 dari 6 paling likuid) -- ini reservoir tempat foreign flow masuk-keluar duluan. Kelemahan momentum murni: nama yang sudah +50-100% YTD biasanya komoditas yang harganya mean-revert tajam, jadi yang masuk akal adalah "momentum yang dibatasi risiko" -- ikut tren di leader bervolume tinggi tapi pakai trailing stop berbasis ATR/volatilitas, dan hindari ekor (BYAN -46%, TPIA -42% 1y membuktikan reversal di big-cap bisa brutal). Mean-reversion hanya layak di pocket value bank/properti P/B<0.5 yang punya katalis (rate cut), bukan sebagai default; di rezim flow-driven sekarang, momentum di sektor dengan FCF + dividend cushion (energi) lebih defensible daripada beli yang jatuh.
tool_queries: SELECT r.ticker, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3m, l.sector, l.mcap_tn FROM returns r JOIN latest l ON r.ticker=l.ticker WHERE r.ret_1y BETWEEN 50 AND 100 AND r.ret_3m > 0 ORDER BY r.ret_3m DESC; -- momentum names still trending up, not yet rolling over

SELECT p.ticker, AVG(p.value)/1e9 AS adv_rp_bn, STDDEV(p.close/LAG(p.close) OVER (PARTITION BY p.ticker ORDER BY p.date) - 1) AS daily_vol FROM prices p WHERE p.date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '60 days' GROUP BY p.ticker HAVING AVG(p.value)/1e9 > 200 ORDER BY adv_rp_bn DESC; -- liquidity + realized vol to size ATR-based trailing stops

SELECT ticker, sector, pb, roe, div_yield, ret_1y FROM fundamentals f JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE f.mcap_tn > 5 AND f.div_yield > 8 AND r.ret_1y > 40 ORDER BY div_yield DESC; -- momentum + dividend cushion (energy) = lower drawdown risk on reversal

SELECT bn.ticker, SUM(bn.net_value)/1e9 AS foreign_net_5d_bn FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.broker_code IN ('AK','BK','KZ') AND bn.date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '5 days' GROUP BY bn.ticker ORDER BY foreign_net_5d_bn DESC; -- confirm foreign (UBS/JPM/CLSA) accumulation behind the momentum

picks.rationale: Coal momentum leader dengan 12.5% yield -- dividend cushion meredam drawdown saat harga batu bara reversal, entry ideal saat pullback ke MA20 dengan trailing stop berbasis ATR.
Energi 10.7% yield, likuid; cocok untuk momentum-with-income di mana cut loss dipasang di bawah swing low terakhir karena downside dibantu dividen tinggi.
Nikel/gold proxy paling likuid (ADV Rp496bn) -- likuiditas tinggi bikin trailing stop bisa dieksekusi tanpa slippage; ikut tren komoditas logam tanpa beli di nama tipis.
Big-cap bank paling likuid kedua + yield 10.4%; jangkar mean-reversion/quality saat rotasi rate-cut, tempat parkir saat momentum komoditas mendingin.
Justru contoh BAHAYA beli di pucuk -- 1y -46% setelah jadi darling; dipakai sebagai pagar: jangan kejar nama parabolik bervolume tipis, ini yang trailing stop seharusnya selamatkan.
Energi ROE 38% + 14.7% yield, momentum + fundamental kuat; entry on breakout-retest, cut loss bila menembus support karena valuasi sudah tidak murah.

answers.risk: Thesis momentum gagal jika harga komoditas (batu bara/nikel) ambruk atau BI menahan/menaikkan rate sehingga IDR menguat tajam -- itu memukul FCF eksportir USD dan membalik flow keluar dari leader energi/metals secara serempak. Tanda invalidasi konkret: foreign net jual beruntun di nama-nama ini, ret_3m berubah negatif sementara ret_1y masih +50-100% (klasik pucuk yang rolling over seperti BYAN), atau harga menembus trailing stop ATR. Jika itu terjadi, rotasi ke mean-reversion value (bank/properti P/B<0.5: BSDE, CTRA, PNBN) jadi lebih masuk akal daripada terus mengejar momentum. Disiplin: jangan rata-rata turun di nama momentum yang sudah patah -- hormati cut loss.

answers.reasoning: In Indonesia, "quality" and "interest rates" are joined at the hip: BBCA's 21% ROE and its premium ~4-5x PBV are both a function of a low cost-of-funds CASA franchise that the market capitalizes hardest when BI rate is falling and IDR is stable, because a rate-cut cycle steepens NIM-stable banks' terminal value while a weak rupiah forces BI to hold rates high and compresses that same multiple — so the "premium vs wait" decision is really a bet on the BI/IDR path, not on the business. The DCF logic is mechanical: a true compounder reinvesting at ROE >> cost of equity (Indonesian COE ~13-15% given the ~7% 10y INDOGB + equity risk premium) grows intrinsic value fast enough that paying 3-4x PBV today can still beat buying a 0.4x-PBV bank whose ROE has collapsed to single digits — value is destroyed, not stored, when ROE < COE, which is exactly why PNBN/PNLF/BBTN are "cheap." So the discipline isn't "premium good / cheap bad"; it's: justify the premium by checking that (terminal ROE - reinvestment-implied growth) supports the implied multiple, demand ROE consistency through the 2020 and 2023 rate shocks, and only pay up where the moat (deposit franchise for BBCA/BMRI, distribution+brand for SIDO/UNVR, fortress balance sheet + dividend for TLKM) is structural rather than commodity-cycle ROE like the coal names (BYAN, GEMS) whose 31-38% ROE is a CPO/coal-price artifact that mean-reverts. Foreign flows amplify this: BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/TLKM are the foreign liquidity proxies (top daily-value names), so a risk-off IDR shock drags their multiples down on flows even when fundamentals hold — which is precisely the correction a patient compounder buyer should pre-commit to buy into. Net: pay up only for the proven structural-ROE franchises, size partial now / add on the rate-or-flow-driven correction, and hard-reject the optically cheap low-ROE banks as value traps.
tool_queries: SELECT f.ticker, f.sector, f.roe, f.pbv, f.per, l.div_yield FROM fundamentals f JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE f.mcap > 5e12 AND f.roe >= 18 ORDER BY f.roe DESC; -- isolate high-ROE quality, then eyeball ROE vs PBV to flag where premium is/ isn't justified

SELECT ticker, roe FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BMRI','BBRI','TLKM','SIDO','UNVR','ASII') /* pull multi-year ROE history from prices/eod_summary-derived fundamentals snapshots */ ORDER BY ticker; -- test ROE CONSISTENCY through 2020 + 2023 rate shocks (Buffett's real test, not single-year level)

SELECT ticker, pbv, roe FROM fundamentals WHERE sector='Keuangan' AND mcap>5e12 ORDER BY pbv ASC; -- separate genuinely-cheap banks from value traps: confirm low-PBV names (MAYA,PNBN,PNLF,BBTN ~0.4x) carry depressed ROE < cost-of-equity

SELECT ticker, trailing_1y_return FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('BBCA','BMRI','TLKM','BBRI','BYAN','TPIA') ORDER BY trailing_1y_return; -- find which quality names are already mid-correction (e.g. BYAN -46%) vs still extended, to time partial entries

picks.rationale: The benchmark IDR compounder: 21% ROE on a low-cost CASA deposit moat, most-liquid stock on IDX (Rp1.16tn/day) — pay the premium, but scale in on foreign-flow / IDR-driven drawdowns rather than chasing.
19% ROE state-bank scale franchise at a structurally lower multiple than BBCA — the better risk-adjusted 'pay up' if you want quality with less premium and a 2.2% yield cushion.
Defensive 18% ROE infra-moat with a 6.8% yield; already de-rated, so this is quality you can buy without waiting — the dividend pays you to hold through volatility.
Consumer-staples compounder at 37% ROE with a distribution/brand moat and asset-light reinvestment — the clearest case where a premium PBV is DCF-justified by sustainable high return on capital.

18% ROE micro-lending moat trading at a 10.4% yield — rare combination of quality and income; the yield itself signals the market is already pricing a partial 'correction,' so it's a pay-a-fair-price entry.

Counter-example / value-trap flag: 0.49x PBV looks cheap but the depressed ROE means value is being eroded (ROE < cost-of-equity) — proof that 'wait for cheap' fails when the discount reflects broken economics, not a sale on quality.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if IDR weakens materially and forces BI to hold/hike rates: that simultaneously compresses the premium multiples on BBCA/BMRI/TLKM (higher cost-of-equity in the DCF) and triggers foreign outflows from exactly these liquid large-caps, so 'paying up' loses money near-term even though the businesses are fine. The discipline-killer is paying a premium for cyclical-ROE masquerading as quality (coal/CPO names whose 30%+ ROE mean-reverts with the commodity cycle); and the DCF is only as good as the ROE-consistency assumption — if a 'compounder's' return on equity proves unsustainable, no entry price was cheap enough.

answers.reasoning: Kekhawatiran Anda benar secara arah, tapi jangan over-react. Di Indonesia, NIM bank tertekan justru di 1-2 cut pertama karena sisi aset (KPR floating, kredit korporasi) reprice lebih cepat daripada deposito berjangka — funding cost baru ikut turun belakangan, jadi ada lag yang menjepit margin sebelum volume kredit pulih; itulah kenapa Keuangan adalah sektor dengan return trailing-1y TERENDAH di brief (47%, ROE heavyweight bank hanya 12-21% vs energi/barang-baku 30%+) dan asing sudah rotasi keluar. Tapi BI cut adalah tailwind untuk aset rate-sensitive berdurasi panjang: properti (beban bunga turun, demand KPR naik) dan infra/telco dividend (re-rating saat yield obligasi turun) — dengan catatan IDR harus stabil supaya BI punya ruang memotong tanpa memicu outflow. Masalahnya, kelompok yang paling "growth" (properti, tech) sekaligus paling rate-sensitive sering sudah mahal di puncak siklus; solusinya pilih nama yang growth-nya nyata TAPI valuasi masih waras (P/B rendah / yield tinggi sebagai jangkar), bukan kejar momentum. Jangan keluar total dari bank — likuiditas, dominasi large-cap, dan yield BBRI 10.4% adalah income anchor; cukup pangkas konsentrasi dari 60% ke ~30-35%.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, pb, dividend_yield, roe, mcap FROM latest WHERE mcap > 5e12 AND pb < 0.7 AND sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Infrastruktur') ORDER BY pb ASC; -- cari proxy growth rate-sensitive yang belum mahal

SELECT r.ticker, l.sector, r.ret_1y, l.dividend_yield, l.roe FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector IN ('Infrastruktur','Energi','Barang Konsumen Primer') AND l.dividend_yield > 6 AND l.roe > 15 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC; -- kandidat income+kualitas pengganti yield bank
screen_stocks(sector='Keuangan', min_mcap=5e12, order_by='dividend_yield desc') -- konfirmasi BBRI/BBNI mana yang layak dipertahankan sbg core bank

SELECT ticker, foreign_pct, avg_daily_value FROM ownership o JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE sector='Keuangan' ORDER BY avg_daily_value DESC; -- cek arah flow asing keluar dari bank sbg konfirmasi tesis rotasi

picks.rationale: Infra/telco defensif, ROE 18% + yield 6.8%, langsung di-re-rate saat yield turun pasca BI cut — pengganti income bank yang lebih stabil daripada commodity yield.
Tol berdurasi panjang, paling diuntungkan rate cut (beban bunga utang besar turun), P/B 0.62 masih murah untuk growth trafik & tarif terjadwal.
Properti rate-sensitive dengan balance sheet lebih sehat dari peers; P/B 0.55 belum mahal, marketing sales naik saat KPR melandai — ini 'growth tapi tidak overvalued'.
Energi yield 10.7%, neraca net-cash, BUMN payout tinggi — pengganti income BBRI tanpa eksposur NIM, jangkar dividen sambil tunggu siklus batubara.
Kesehatan/consumer defensif, ROE 37%, low debt jadi tidak tergantung arah rate; diversifikasi ke konsumsi domestik yang tahan banting bila IDR bergejolak.
Pertahankan sebagai core bank: yield 10.4% tertinggi di big-cap bank + likuiditas terdalam; pangkas bobot, jangan buang — cut justru menahan funding cost di portofolio mikro yang sudah floating.

answers.risk: Tesis ini gagal kalau IDR melemah tajam / inflasi rebound sehingga BI MENUNDA atau membatalkan cut — maka durasi (JSMR/CTRA) yang paling rate-sensitive justru jatuh, dan bank malah outperform karena NIM bertahan. Risiko kedua: rotasi sudah crowded — Properti/Infra/Energi sudah +114/+83/+120% trailing-1y, jadi sebagian re-rating mungkin sudah harga-in (kontras Keuangan +47% yang tertinggal bisa jadi value rebound). Target alokasi sehat: Bank ~30-35% (BBRI core + sisakan satu dari BBCA/BMRI), Infra/Telco-dividend ~15-20% (TLKM/JSMR), Properti ~10% (CTRA), Energi-yield ~10-15% (PTBA), Konsumen/Kesehatan defensif ~10-15% (SIDO), sisanya kas/likuid — jangan eksekusi sekaligus, average-in 2-3 bulan ikut konfirmasi cut.

answers.reasoning: Secara expected return murni, lump sum biasanya menang karena IHSG cenderung naik dari waktu ke waktu, jadi DCA 6 bulan rata-rata menahan ~50% cash di sideline dan menyerahkan upside — itu pajak yang Anda bayar untuk menurunkan variance. Tapi titik masuk sekarang bukan netral: rupiah tertekan ke 16-ribuan memaksa BI menahan/menunda pemangkasan rate demi membela kurs, dan rate tinggi itu menekan justru sektor rate-sensitive (properti, tech/growth, bank yang NIM-nya tergerus oleh cost of fund) sekaligus memicu foreign outflow yang terlihat di eod_summary (net foreign jual di BBCA/BBRI/GOTO/bank kecil seperti BANK/PNBN yang turun 35-39% setahun). Justru di sini eksportir komoditas jadi natural hedge: coal/CPO/nickel/gold menerima revenue USD sementara biaya rupiah, jadi IDR lemah menggemukkan margin mereka — itulah kenapa Energi (+120%) dan Barang Baku tertentu memimpin, dan dividend yield 10-15% (ADRO, PTBA, BSSR, GEMS) memberi carry yang mengalahkan deposito sambil Anda menunggu kejelasan global. Maka rekomendasi rasional: hybrid — lump sum ~40-50% sekarang ke kombinasi defensif berdividen + hedge IDR (yang sudah de-rated dan membayar Anda untuk menunggu), sisanya DCA 4-6 bulan untuk merata-ratakan sisa volatilitas global dan menambah ke nama rate-sensitive berkualitas hanya saat ada sinyal BI mulai cutting / IDR stabil.
tool_queries: SELECT r.ticker, l.sector, l.market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn, l.dividend_yield, l.return_on_equity, l.price_to_book, l.beta, r.ret_1y, r.ret_6m FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE l.market_cap > 1e13 AND l.dividend_yield > 0.07 AND l.beta < 1.1 ORDER BY l.dividend_yield DESC LIMIT 30;

SELECT s.ticker, c.sector, SUM(s.foreign_buy - s.foreign_sell) AS net_foreign_60d FROM eod_summary s JOIN companies c USING(ticker) WHERE s.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 60 DAY GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY net_foreign_60d DESC LIMIT 25;
screen_stocks(filters: {sector IN ['Energi','Barang Baku'], dividend_yield > 0.08, return_on_equity > 0.20, market_cap > 1e13}, sort: dividend_yield desc) -- IDR-hedge exporters that pay you to wait

SELECT ticker, sector, market_cap/1e12 AS mcap_tn, price_to_book, return_on_equity, dividend_yield FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Properti & Real Estat','Teknologi') AND price_to_book < 0.6 ORDER BY price_to_book ASC LIMIT 20; -- rate-sensitive names to BUY only on BI-cut/IDR-stabilization signal, via the DCA tranche

picks.rationale: Lump-sum anchor: bank paling likuid (turnover Rp1.16tn/hr), ROE 21%, balance sheet terbaik untuk menahan rate tinggi; yield 5% lebih rendah tapi paling defensif sebagai inti portofolio.
Lump-sum income core: yield 10.4% dengan ROE 18% dan likuiditas tinggi; sudah ter-derate oleh foreign outflow sehingga entry-nya menarik untuk dividend carry.
IDR-hedge: batu bara revenue USD vs biaya rupiah, yield ~10.7% mengalahkan deposito; carry tinggi sambil menunggu kejelasan global.
IDR-hedge berkualitas: coal play likuid, yield ~12.5%; margin diuntungkan rupiah lemah, cocok untuk separuh lump sum yang cari yield + proteksi kurs.
Defensif non-komoditas: infra/telco, ROE 18%, yield 6.8%, beta rendah; menyeimbangkan portofolio kalau siklus komoditas berbalik.
Target tranche DCA: properti P/B 0.55, sangat sensitif rate — hanya ditambah bertahap saat ada sinyal BI mulai memangkas rate / IDR stabil, bukan sekarang.

answers.risk: Tesis hedge-eksportir + DCA ini batal kalau (1) harga komoditas global (coal/CPO/nickel) anjlok — IDR lemah tak menolong kalau ASP jatuh lebih cepat, dividend 10-15% bisa dipotong karena rasio payout dari laba yang menyusut; atau (2) skenario kebalikan: rupiah cepat pulih dan BI memangkas rate lebih agresif dari ekspektasi — di situ rate-sensitive (properti/tech/bank) reli kencang dan sikap defensif/DCA Anda justru kalah dari lump-sum penuh ke beta tinggi. Eksekusi: kalau coal spot turun >15% atau BI memberi sinyal dovish jelas, geser bobot dari hedge-eksportir ke nama rate-sensitive yang ada di query keempat.

answers.reasoning: Dividend growth beda dari yield tinggi. Emiten IDX yang DPS-nya naik konsisten secara struktural adalah bank top-tier dan defensif consumer telco, karena labanya tumbuh lepas dari siklus komoditas. Bank besar menaikkan payout ke 60 sampai 80 persen seiring kredit tumbuh sekitar 10 persen per tahun, NIM terlindung selama BI rate sekitar 5.5 persen sebab penurunan BI rate menekan cost of funds lebih cepat dari asset yield untuk bank CASA-heavy seperti BBCA, ROE terjaga sekitar 21 persen. SIDO dan TLKM ditopang disinflasi dan transfer fiskal. Sebaliknya yield raksasa energi ADRO PTBA BSSR GEMS dan CPO TAPG itu siklikal mengikuti harga batu bara dan CPO, DPS naik turun, terbukti ASII BBRI BSSR GEMS turun 2024 ke 2025, jadi bukan growth. Itu kunci kenapa dividend growth realistis di IDX tapi hanya di subset emiten yang arus kasnya tidak bergantung komoditas.
tool_queries: run_query deret DPS per tahun per ticker dari tabel prices, jumlahkan dividends positif di-group per ticker dan tahun, untuk uji konsistensi kenaikan year over year
run_query CTE DPS per tahun lalu cek apakah dps tahun ini lebih besar atau sama dengan dps tahun sebelumnya pakai window LAG, untuk memfilter emiten yang DPS-nya tidak pernah turun

screen_stocks sektor Keuangan Infrastruktur Kesehatan dan Barang Konsumen Primer, mcap di atas 50 triliun, roe di atas 15 persen, yield 2 sampai 8 persen, untuk menyaring kompounder dan membuang yield siklikal energi

run_query gabung fundamentals ke view latest pada ticker, ambil latest_dividend dan roe untuk mcap besar, catatan field latest_dividend_year di mirror dev basi tahun 2020 jadi konfirmasi DPS terkini dari tabel prices

picks.rationale: Inti dividend growth, ROE sekitar 21 persen, DPS naik 277.5 ke 305 di data, CASA-heavy tahan saat BI rate turun, payout naik bertahap, kompounder paling reliable di IDX.
DPS naik 353 ke 466 dalam window, ROE sekitar 19 persen, payout dinaikkan sekitar 60 persen, kenaikan DPS struktural bukan siklikal.
Defensif telco, DPS naik 178.5 ke 212.5 atau plus 19 persen, yield sekitar 6.8 persen dengan arus kas stabil, growth plus yield, tahan siklus komoditas dan IDR.
Dividend grower murni defensif, ROE sekitar 37 persen, DPS 36 ke 43 atau plus 19 persen, nyaris zero-debt, profil compounding 15 tahun paling bersih meski mcap kecil.
Yield sekitar 10.4 persen plus ROE 18 persen, DPS sempat turun 2024 ke 2025 karena siklus kredit mikro dan NPL, jadi anchor yield tinggi untuk reinvestasi bukan grower termulus.
Yield sekitar 6.7 persen tapi DPS turun 2236 ke 2051 karena tertaut harga batu bara dan alat berat, contoh kenapa yield siklikal bukan dividend growth, pegang porsi kecil saja.

answers.risk: Data dev hanya sekitar 2.25 tahun dari 2024 sampai 2026 sehingga track record 5 sampai 10 tahun tidak bisa diverifikasi dari DB ini dan butuh sumber DPS multi-tahun eksternal seperti RUPS atau keystats produksi, sementara field latest_dividend_year di mirror malah basi tahun 2020. Tesis batal jika satu, BI rate dipangkas agresif menekan NIM bank di luar perlindungan CASA; dua, pemerintah memaksa BUMN seperti BBRI dan BMRI menahan dividen demi setoran modal atau PMN atau Danantara mengubah kebijakan dividen; tiga, tekanan fiskal atau IDR memicu pajak dividen lebih tinggi. Proyeksi compounding 15 tahun realistis hanya untuk subset kompounder BBCA BMRI TLKM SIDO dengan asumsi DPS-growth konservatif 8 sampai 12 persen per tahun plus reinvestasi, jangan ekstrapolasi yield siklikal energi atau CPO sebagai growth.

answers.category: Fed-cut / EM-inflow sector rotation on IDX — foreign-flow positioning vs buy-and-hold quality
answers.reasoning: Transmission is currency-first, not story-first: a Fed cut compresses DXY, which stabilizes IDR, which is the precondition for BI to cut — and IDR stability is what actually pulls foreign money back, because unhedged offshore investors earn the carry plus the currency gain. That flow does not spray across the market; it enters through a handful of foreign desks (J.P.Morgan/BK, UBS/AK, CLSA/KZ, Maybank/ZP) into the most liquid, most index-weighted names, which on IDX means the big banks first. Banks are the highest-conviction beneficiary on two counts: they are the literal liquidity vehicle for foreign flow (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI dominate ADTV), and a rate cut is a direct fundamental tailwind via stabilizing NIM, falling credit cost, and reviving loan growth — and right now Keuangan is the worst-performing sector (avg ~47, lowest of 11) trading at the cheapest P/B cluster, i.e. the de-rated, under-owned setup that mean-reverts hardest when inflow returns. The "real flow vs narrative" filter is mechanical: a name only catches foreign money if it has the ADTV to absorb size AND shows foreign-desk net buying AND has high/rising foreign ownership — a thematic story with thin liquidity and no foreign-desk prints (much of property/tech) cannot, regardless of how good the narrative sounds. So front-running beats buy-and-hold only where you can verify the flow is actually arriving (broker_net on foreign desks turning positive) into something cheap enough to re-rate; absent that confirmation, hold quality compounders (BBCA) because illiquid narrative names hand the flow straight back on the first risk-off.
tool_queries: screen_stocks: sector='Keuangan', min_mcap=20_000_000_000_000, sort by pb ascending — surface the de-rated big banks (BBRI/BMRI/BBNI vs cheap-PB BDMN/BBTN/PNBN) that are the primary foreign-flow vehicle and rank them by re-rating room

run_query: SELECT bn.ticker, bn.broker_code, SUM(bn.net_value) AS net FROM broker_net bn WHERE bn.broker_code IN ('BK','AK','KZ','ZP') AND bn.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAY GROUP BY 1,2 HAVING net > 0 ORDER BY net DESC — isolate REAL foreign-desk (J.P.Morgan/UBS/CLSA/Maybank) net buying to separate names already catching flow from pure narrative

run_query: SELECT o.ticker, o.foreign_pct, l.mcap, l.adv_value, r.ret_1y FROM ownership o JOIN latest l USING(ticker) JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.adv_value > 100e9 AND o.foreign_pct > 30 ORDER BY o.foreign_pct DESC — keep only names liquid enough (ADTV>Rp100bn) to absorb size with foreign ownership already high/rising

run_query: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap, pb, roe, div_yield, ret_1y FROM latest JOIN returns USING(ticker) WHERE sector='Keuangan' AND mcap>20e12 ORDER BY ret_1y ASC — confirm banks are the most de-rated liquid sector (cheap PB + lagging 1y) = highest mean-reversion beta to inflow

picks.rationale: Most de-rated liquid mega-bank (10.4% yield, ~18% ROE, top-2 ADTV at Rp909bn) — the prime foreign-flow vehicle with the most re-rating room when EM inflow returns and BI cuts.
State mega-bank, Rp847bn ADTV and deep foreign-desk usage; direct beneficiary of NIM stabilization and loan-growth recovery on a rate-cut cycle — high flow-beta, not narrative.
The buy-and-hold quality anchor: highest ADTV (Rp1.16tn), 21% ROE — wins if you cannot confirm flow is arriving, since it compounds and is always the first foreign re-entry point.
Cheapest of the big-four mega-banks (9.4% yield, P/B near book) with ample liquidity (Rp271bn ADTV) — highest mean-reversion torque to bank-sector re-rating.
Liquid (Rp331bn ADTV), 6.8% yield large-cap proxy for foreign re-entry beyond banks; index-weighted and historically a core foreign holding, so it catches breadth-driven inflow.
Highest-ADTV metals name (Rp496bn) — the genuinely liquid commodity beneficiary if Fed-cut weakens USD and lifts gold/nickel, vs thinner narrative-only resource plays that cannot absorb size.

answers.risk: The thesis is currency-gated, not Fed-gated: if the Fed cuts but IDR stays weak (sticky US inflation keeping DXY firm, a domestic fiscal/twin-deficit shock under the new administration's spending, or a commodity-price drop hurting the trade balance), foreign flow does not return and the cheap banks stay cheap — a value trap, not a setup. The "real flow" filter also invalidates the front-running edge if the broker_net foreign desks (BK/AK/KZ/ZP) are still net sellers: front-running an inflow that hasn't started underperforms simply holding BBCA. Front-running only beats buy-and-hold once foreign-desk net buying turns positive AND IDR is stabilizing; absent both, default to quality.

answers.reasoning: Makro Indonesia sekarang ada di titik balik dovish: BI sudah mulai cutting cycle untuk topang pertumbuhan, tapi rupiah tetap rapuh ke arus keluar asing dan defisit fiskal yang melebar di bawah agenda belanja pemerintah baru, jadi penurunan suku bunga datang dengan ekor risiko IDR. Itu transmisinya: rate turun menekan yield deposito/obligasi sehingga high-dividend equity (terutama coal cash cows ADRO/PTBA dengan yield 10-12% dan banks BBNI/BBRI dengan yield 9-10%) jadi pengganti income yang menarik DAN dividen batubara dibayar dari arus kas USD yang justru lindung nilai kalau IDR melemah — itu sebabnya sisi defensif di sini bukan sekadar low-beta, tapi income riil + hedge mata uang. Di sisi upside, rate cut + reflasi global mengangkat metal/gold (ANTM +134% 1y, BRMS +121%) dan energy/data-center proxy (CUAN +62%, DCII), yang datanya saya konfirmasi punya beta tinggi 1.0-1.3 = persis mesin upside yang dicari. Banks mendominasi likuiditas (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI = top turnover) jadi sisi defensif tetap likuid untuk rebalancing, sementara nama momentum komoditas adalah taruhan siklikal yang bisa balik tajam (BYAN -46%, TPIA -42% buktinya). Untuk makro late-cycle dovish-tapi-rapuh ini, barbell condong defensif: 60% defensif / 40% growth.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(filters: market_cap>5e12, dividend_yield>0.08, beta<0.6, recommendation_key in ('buy','strong_buy')) -- saring sisi defensif: high-yield, low-beta, likuid, di-cover analis

run_query: with p as (select ticker,date,close from prices), m as (select max(date) md from p), last as (select ticker,close lc from p,m where date=md), yago as (select ticker,close yc,row_number() over(partition by ticker order by date desc) rn from p,m where date<=md-interval 365 day) select l.ticker, round((l.lc/y.yc-1)*100,0) ret_1y from last l join yago y on l.ticker=y.ticker and y.rn=1 order by ret_1y desc limit 30 -- ranking momentum 1y riil dari prices (bukan rasio yf yg unreliable)

run_query: select ticker, round(market_cap/1e12,1) mcap_tn, round(dividend_yield*100,1) yld, round(beta,2) beta, recommendation_key from read_parquet('summary/latest.parquet') where ticker in ('ADRO','PTBA','BBNI','BBRI','TLKM','ANTM','BRMS','CUAN','DCII') -- verifikasi beta/yield/rekomendasi tiap kandidat barbell

run_query: select broker_code, sum(net_value) nv from broker_net where ticker in ('ANTM','CUAN','BRMS') group by 1 order by nv desc limit 10 -- cek apakah broker asing (AK/UBS, BK/JPM, KZ/CLSA) lagi akumulasi di sisi momentum sbg konfirmasi flow

picks.rationale: Jangkar sisi defensif: yield ~12.5% dibayar dari arus kas USD batubara (hedge IDR) DAN momentum positif +59% 1y riil — income tinggi tanpa korbankan total return.
Defensif murni: yield 10.7%, beta rendah/negatif, BUMN coal cash cow — pengganti income saat BI cutting cycle menekan yield deposito.
Sisi defensif likuid: bank besar yield 9.4%, rekomendasi strong_buy, beta moderat — proxy income perbankan yang gampang ditambah/dikurangi saat rebalance.
Mesin upside utama: +134% 1y riil (momentum #1 di data), leverage ke siklus gold/nickel saat rate cut global melemahkan USD — taruhan reflasi paling tajam.
Sisi growth high-beta (1.32, +62% 1y): energy/infra Prajogo group, ROE 26% — upside siklikal dengan likuiditas tinggi (turnover top-15).
Growth struktural (bukan sekadar siklikal): data-center/AI-infra, ROE 29%, mcap 473tn — eksposur tema sekuler untuk diversifikasi sisi momentum dari pure-komoditas.

answers.risk: Risiko utama: IDR crash / BI dipaksa hike balik. Kalau rupiah jeblok karena outflow asing memburu fiskal Indonesia yang melebar, BI bisa membalik ke hawkish — itu meledakkan KEDUA sisi sekaligus: nama growth high-beta (ANTM/CUAN/DCII) jatuh duluan dan paling dalam (lihat BYAN -46%, TPIA -42% sbg bukti betapa cepat komoditas berbalik), sementara coal dividend cuma sebagian terlindung karena dividen forward-nya bergantung harga batubara yang juga turun di resesi global.
