Rebalancing rule: set band ±10% dari target 60/40; kalau sisi growth outperform sampai jadi >50% bobot, trim balik ke 40% dan parkir hasilnya di ADRO/BBNI (kunci yield + hedge); kalau defensif yang outperform (harga coal spike), jangan kejar — biarkan sampai band atas lalu rotasi sebagian ke ANTM/CUAN saat momentum masih intact. Tesis batal kalau coal yield runtuh (harga batubara <USD ~90) ATAU broker_net menunjukkan asing net-sell konsisten di nama momentum.

answers.reasoning: Di IDX, risiko likuiditas dan deep-value bukan dua dial terpisah — keduanya digerakkan mesin yang sama: foreign flow lewat broker asing (AK/UBS, BK/JPMorgan, KZ/CLSA) yang cuma menyentuh ~20-30 nama liquid (bank + komoditas), sementara second-liner berputar dari ritel domestik tipis. Saat BI menahan rate tinggi untuk menjaga IDR (suku bunga riil positif menahan capital outflow), biaya carry posisi naik dan justru saham siklikal komoditas (batu bara ADRO/PTBA, nikel/emas) + bank jadi tempat asing parkir — itu sebabnya valuasi "murah" di second-liner sering bertahan murah karena tidak ada marginal buyer yang bisa me-rerate-nya. Mekanisme transmisinya: pas market crash (taper tantrum lokal, downgrade rating, atau pembalikan harga komoditas), asing jual dulu yang liquid karena itu yang bisa dijual — turnover small-cap ambruk 50-80%, bid-ask melebar jadi 2-5%, dan deep-value murah Anda berubah jadi value trap yang tidak bisa di-exit tanpa membanting harga sendiri. Jadi diskon valuasi second-liner sebagian besar adalah harga wajar dari illiquidity premium, bukan free lunch — Anda dibayar untuk menanggung risiko exit, bukan untuk kebodohan pasar.
tool_queries: WITH liq AS (SELECT ticker, median(value) med_val_90d, median(frequency) med_freq FROM read_parquet('data/eod_summary/**/*.parquet') WHERE date >= DATE '2026-01-01' GROUP BY ticker) SELECT s.ticker, s.market_cap/1e12 mcap_tn, s.price_to_book pb, s.return_on_equity roe, s.dividend_yield yld, l.med_val_90d/1e9 med_val_bn FROM read_parquet('data/summary/**/*.parquet') s JOIN liq l USING(ticker) WHERE s.market_cap BETWEEN 1e12 AND 15e12 AND s.return_on_equity >= 12 AND s.dividend_yield >= 4 ORDER BY l.med_val_90d ASC
-- days-to-exit a Rp25bn position at 20% ADV participation, calm-market median: SELECT ticker, round(25e9/(0.20*med_val_90d),1) days_to_exit FROM liq JOIN summary USING(ticker) ORDER BY days_to_exit

-- crash-stress: recompute med daily value over worst drawdown weeks vs calm weeks to size turnover collapse: SELECT ticker, median(value) FILTER(WHERE date BETWEEN '2025-03-01' AND '2025-04-30') crash_val, median(value) FILTER(WHERE date >= '2026-01-01') calm_val FROM eod_summary GROUP BY ticker
screen_stocks(market_cap_min=1e12, market_cap_max=15e12, roe_min=12, dividend_yield_min=4, sort='median_daily_value desc') -- keep only names where a target position is <10 ADV-days to exit

picks.rationale: Core barbell anchor: P/B ~1.1, ROE 15%, yield 6.7%, Rp123bn/day turnover — Rp25bn exits in ~1 day; deep-value proxy ke siklus batu bara TANPA risiko likuiditas, ini pengganti rasional untuk small-cap energi murah.
Bank deep-value sejati: P/B 0.80, ROE ~12%, yield 9.4%, Rp230bn/day — exit Rp25bn dalam ~0.5 hari; bukti Anda bisa beli murah DAN tetap liquid, jadi default sebelum turun ke second-liner.
Dividend deep-value yang masih tradeable: yield 10.7%, ROE 16%, Rp55bn/day → exit Rp25bn ~2.3 hari; batas bawah zona liquid yang layak untuk posisi besar.

Best-in-class second-liner JIKA Anda ambil risiko: ROE 24.6%, P/B ~1.65, Rp1.2bn/day — tapi exit Rp25bn butuh ~100 hari kalem dan bisa 250+ hari saat crash; size-kan jadi posisi kecil saja (<Rp3-5bn).

Deep-value second-liner: P/B 1.31, ROE 14.5%, yield 11.4% — menarik secara valuasi, tapi ~247 hari-exit di Rp25bn adalah ilustrasi telak kenapa diskon ini adalah illiquidity premium, bukan mispricing gratis.

Contoh value trap eksplisit: P/B 1.9, ROE 17%, yield 6.8% terlihat bagus, tapi turnover hanya Rp10jt/hari → mustahil keluar untuk posisi gede (>14.000 hari). HINDARI untuk size apa pun; bukti bahwa 'fundamental bagus' tidak ada artinya tanpa exit.

answers.risk: Tesis batal jika (1) likuiditas struktural naik di second-liner — mis. masuk indeks (IDX30/LQ45 rebalance), aksi korporasi, atau lonjakan partisipasi ritel domestik yang menyempitkan spread, sehingga illiquidity premium runtuh dan rerate terjadi cepat; atau (2) terjadi rotasi makro besar (BI cut agresif + IDR stabil + komoditas rebound) yang menarik foreign flow turun ke small-cap, membuat barbell blue-chip Anda underperform. Risiko utama operasional: angka days-to-exit di atas adalah MEDIAN pasar kalem — dalam crash turnover bisa kolaps 50-80% sehingga pengali exit berlipat 2-3x, dan beberapa rasio P/B dari yf tidak reliabel (CTBN/BSSR/ADRO/MCOL menunjukkan price_to_book dalam satuan harga, bukan rasio), jadi setiap pick second-liner WAJIB diverifikasi book value manual sebelum disebut 'murah'. Aturan keras: jangan pernah size posisi second-liner di atas ~5-10 ADV-days; jika tidak bisa keluar dalam 10 hari kalem, asumsikan tidak bisa keluar sama sekali saat crash.

answers.category: kombinasi dividend + capital appreciation (barbell), bukan pure dividend
answers.reasoning: Target Rp20jt/bln dalam 12 thn = butuh korpus ~Rp5,5bn nominal (inflasi ~3,5% bikin Rp20jt jadi ~Rp30jt/bln di 2038, dibagi yield aman ~6,5%). Modal Rp800jt + nabung Rp10jt/bln hanya capai itu kalau CAGR nominal ~10% — artinya FASE AKUMULASI harus total-return, bukan pure dividend; pure dividend (~8% yield tanpa reinvest growth) berhenti di ~Rp4,3bn dan hanya kasih ~Rp23jt nominal, marginal. Transmisi makro Indonesia: BI rate masih ~5,5-6% jadi RDPU/obligasi kasih lantai risk-free tinggi, tapi itu juga menekan P/B bank (BBRI/BMRI 10%+ yield + ROE 18-21% = mesin compounding paling efisien di IDX, dan saat BI mulai potong rate NIM bank + valuasi properti naik). Batu bara (ADRO/PTBA/ITMG) kasih yield 10-15% tapi cyclical — harga ekspor + IDR lemah angkat rupiah-revenue, tapi struktural declining, jadi dipakai sebagai cash-engine bukan core. Foreign flow + dominasi bank di likuiditas (BBCA/BBRI/BMRI top daily value) bikin big-cap bank lebih tahan redemption asing daripata small-cap dividend yang bisa terjebak.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, mcap_tn, roe, dividend_yield FROM latest WHERE mcap_tn > 50 AND dividend_yield BETWEEN 4 AND 9 AND roe > 15 ORDER BY roe DESC; -- core compounders: high ROE + sustainable (not extreme) yield

screen_stocks(filters={mcap_min_tn: 30, dividend_yield_min: 9, sector_in: ['Energi','Keuangan']}, order_by='dividend_yield desc', limit=15) -- high-cash decumulation engines, check payout sustainability

SELECT f.ticker, f.payout_ratio, f.net_debt_ebitda, r.ret_1y, r.ret_3y FROM fundamentals f JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE f.ticker IN ('PTBA','ADRO','ITMG','BSSR','BBRI','BBNI','TLKM','ASII','UNTR') ORDER BY f.payout_ratio DESC; -- verify dividend coverage + balance sheet before trusting yield

SELECT ticker, AVG(value_rp_bn) avg_val FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN (SELECT ticker FROM latest WHERE dividend_yield>8) GROUP BY ticker HAVING avg_val > 20 ORDER BY avg_val DESC; -- liquidity screen so a 800jt+ portfolio can enter/exit without slippage

picks.rationale: Core compounder + income: ROE 18%, yield 10,4%, paling likuid kedua di IDX — bank yang kasih growth DAN cash, jantung fase akumulasi.
ROE 19% tapi yield baru 2,2% — tilt ke capital appreciation; big-cap bank paling tahan foreign outflow, untuk compounding 12 thn.
Defensif infra-telco, yield 6,8% + ROE 18%, cash flow stabil non-cyclical sebagai anchor portofolio saat batu bara/bank volatile.
Cash-engine batu bara: yield 10,7%, BUMN payout tinggi — dipakai porsi kecil untuk dividend riil, BUKAN core karena cyclical/struktural declining.
Konglomerat terdiversifikasi (otomotif+UNTR+finance), yield 6,7% ROE 14% — proxy ekonomi domestik, balance growth + income.
Quality anchor: ROE 21% tertinggi di bank besar, yield 5,1%, paling likuid di IDX — kompon paling konsisten, premi valuasi worth it untuk horizon panjang.

answers.risk: Achievable secara matematis HANYA jika CAGR nominal ~10% tercapai 12 thn berturut — itu agresif, bukan base case. Yang membatalkan tesis: (1) BI rate tinggi berkepanjangan / inflasi >5% menekan valuasi bank & menaikkan korpus yang dibutuhkan; (2) yield batu bara (PTBA/ADRO) ilusi — harga komoditas turun + payout dipotong, jadi jangan andalkan >15-20% bobot di energi; (3) yf yield/ROE di brief 'unreliable' untuk sebagian nama, harus diverifikasi payout_ratio + net_debt sebelum trust. Risiko terbesar: salah strategi pure-dividend di fase akumulasi — kunci nominal income terlalu cepat akan mematikan compounding dan target meleset. Mitigasi: 70% growth-tilt (BBCA/BMRI/BBRI/ASII) sekarang, rotasi ke high-yield (PTBA/BBNI/TLKM) + RDPU/obligasi di tahun ke-9-10.

answers.reasoning: Volatile-food inflation menjelang Lebaran is a COGS shock, not a demand shock, so the right lens is who can pass through input costs. For ICBP/INDF/MYOR the bull leg of palm oil/CPO and wheat is a direct margin squeeze: Bogasari (INDF) imports USD-denominated wheat, so a weak IDR (BI holding rate high to defend the rupiah against Fed/foreign-flow pressure) compounds the cost shock, and these names price in rigid sachet/SKU tiers that they cannot reprice mid-Lebaran without volume loss. The data already shows this transmission — ICBP -29% and AMRT -29% trailing-1y are among the worst big-cap performers while UNVR (ROE 107) and SIDO (ROE 37) defend margin through brand pricing power. Defensive telco/utilities (TLKM, PGAS, POWR, JSMR) are structurally insulated: regulated/contracted tariffs (PGAS toll, POWR PPA, JSMR concession) and near-zero food-input intensity mean food CPI barely touches their cost base, and their 7-10% yields are real cash, not yf noise. Net: in a sharp volatile-food spike I'd underweight the wheat/CPO-levered packaged-food names and overweight dividend-defensive infra plus the high-pricing-power staples (UNVR/SIDO) rather than the volume-staples (ICBP/INDF/MYOR).
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, sector, pb, roe, dividend_yield, mcap FROM latest WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','UNVR','SIDO','TLKM','PGAS','POWR','JSMR') ORDER BY roe DESC;
SELECT ticker, ret_1y FROM returns WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','UNVR','SIDO','TLKM','PGAS','POWR','JSMR') ORDER BY ret_1y ASC;
screen_stocks(sector='Infrastruktur', min_dividend_yield=6, min_mcap_tn=20, sort='dividend_yield desc')
SELECT ticker, gross_margin, net_margin, fiscal_period FROM fundamentals WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','UNVR','SIDO') ORDER BY ticker, fiscal_period DESC;

picks.rationale: Core defensive overweight: ROE 18, 6.8% yield, zero food-input intensity and pricing power via data tariffs; insulated from a volatile-food COGS shock.
Regulated gas-toll model with ~9.9% yield; tariff-protected cost base means food inflation does not hit margins, pure cash defensiveness.
PPA-contracted power utility, 9.7% yield; revenue locked to USD-linked offtake, structurally immune to food-price pass-through risk.
If you must hold staples, this is the pricing-power one: ROE 107 shows it can pass input costs through brand, unlike volume-staples.
Herbal/jamu staple, ROE 37, low CPO/wheat dependence and strong brand pricing — defends margin far better than packaged-food peers in a food-inflation spike.
The pick to UNDERWEIGHT/avoid: -29% trailing-1y already, direct CPO/wheat COGS exposure plus IDR import drag, rigid sachet pricing caps pass-through.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if BI cuts and IDR strengthens (cheapens imported wheat/CPO and re-rates the rate-sensitive staples) OR if the food spike proves transient and the packaged-food names successfully reprice post-Lebaran, reclaiming margin — then the beaten-down ICBP/INDF/MYOR mean-revert and outperform the already-rich defensives. Also, the telco/utility 'safety' depends on no adverse tariff/regulatory reset; a forced tariff freeze would gut the yield case.

answers.reasoning: A minimum-wage hike of 8-10% is a real-wage gain (BI's 2026 inflation target band is ~2.5%±1%, so purchasing power rises ~5-7% net), and that cash flows fastest into low-ticket daily staples and discretionary trade-ups, not into capex-heavy goods. The transmission splits on cost structure: labor is a small, fixed share of COGS for branded FMCG (raw materials/CPO/wheat/sugar + IDR import cost dominate), so a demand lift drops to margin; but for retail/distribution and labor-intensive plantations, payroll is a large variable cost, so the wage bill compresses margin faster than the top line recovers. The bigger swing factor for staples margins in 2026 is input-side — IDR vs USD (imported wheat, dairy, packaging) and the CPO cycle — which the wage story does not change, so I weight pricing power and import intensity heavily. Note the brief shows AMRT and ICBP already down ~29% trailing-1y, signaling the market has been pricing weak volume/margin, which is exactly where a real-wage tailwind has the most mean-reversion optionality if it materializes. Net: mass-market staples and modern-trade retail with pricing power are net positive; labor-heavy plantations and thin-margin discretionary big-ticket are net negative.
tool_queries: SELECT ticker, company_name, sub_sector, market_cap, profit_margins, return_on_equity, price_to_book FROM latest WHERE sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer','Barang Konsumen Non-Primer') AND market_cap > 5e12 ORDER BY profit_margins DESC;
SELECT l.ticker, l.sub_sector, l.profit_margins, l.dividend_yield, r.ret_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector LIKE 'Barang Konsumen%' AND r.ret_1y < 0 ORDER BY r.ret_1y ASC;
screen_stocks(filters=[{field:'sector',op:'in',value:['Barang Konsumen Primer']},{field:'market_cap',op:'>',value:5e12},{field:'profit_margins',op:'>',value:0.1}], sort='return_on_equity desc')
SELECT ticker, broker_code, net_value FROM broker_net WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','MYOR','AMRT','SIDO','UNVR') AND date >= (SELECT max(date)-INTERVAL '30 days' FROM broker_net) ORDER BY net_value DESC;

picks.rationale: Net positive: mass-market snacks/coffee/beverage sit squarely in the wage-uplift sweet spot, strong volume leverage with pricing power; biggest beneficiary of higher daily-spend frequency.

Net positive on a contrarian basis: instant noodles are a defensive staple that scales with formal-sector headcount/wages; down ~29% trailing-1y in the brief gives mean-reversion room if real wages lift volumes.
Net positive: herbal/energy drinks for the mass low-income consumer, 37% ROE and asset-light so the demand lift drops to margin; minimal labor-cost drag.

Net positive but margin-watched: modern-trade minimarket with the most direct read on mass purchasing power, yet payroll is a large opex line — net positive only if SSSG outpaces the wage bill; down ~29% prices in skepticism.
Net negative: CPO plantation is labor-intensive with a heavy field-wage base, so an 8-10% UMR hike directly inflates harvesting cost while revenue tracks the global CPO price, not domestic demand.
Net negative: same labor-heavy palm cost structure as TAPG, 41% ROE is cycle/price-driven and gets squeezed by a rising wage floor with no offsetting demand benefit.

answers.risk: The thesis breaks if the wage hike is eaten by inflation or IDR weakness: if BI is forced to hold/hike on rupiah pressure and imported input costs (wheat, dairy, packaging, CPO-linked) climb, real purchasing power is flat and staples get the cost hit without the volume lift. Two specific invalidators: (1) IDR depreciation past ~16,500-17,000/USD lifts COGS for import-reliant names (ICBP/MYOR/UNVR) faster than the demand benefit; (2) employers respond to the wage floor by cutting formal headcount or hours, so aggregate wage-bill-to-consumer transmission is muted while retailers (AMRT) still carry the full payroll increase — turning the modern-trade pick net negative.

answers.reasoning: The data flips the investor's premise: across the 3 Lebaran cycles in the dataset (2024-04-10, 2025-03-31, 2026-03-20), packaged staples and retailers do NOT rally into the event — they de-rate hard (AMRT -12.7%, INDF/MYOR ~-10.7%, ICBP -10%, KLBF -10.2% average run-up T-45 to T-5) and then re-rate AFTER (post-Lebaran T-5 to T+25 positive for most). The transmission: real THR/Ramadan volume is genuine but lands in Q1-Q2 earnings, while in Feb-March foreign flows and domestic liquidity rotate OUT of expensive defensives into the Q1 cyclical/energy-coal trade (Indonesia's index is energy/bank-heavy), and a stable BI rate plus firm IDR remove the defensive bid — so staples sag into Lebaran and snap back once volumes confirm and the rotation unwinds. The one true pre-Lebaran momentum name is TAPG (palm/CPO): positive run-up AND post in all 3 years (+13.8/+10.8/+10.7 run-up), because cooking-oil demand plus a firm CPO price feeds straight into the P&L rather than being a pure consumption-volume story. Net: the seasonality edge is real but it is a post-Lebaran mean-reversion trade in staples, plus a clean pre-Lebaran momentum trade in CPO — not a Q4 accumulate-and-hold.
tool_queries: run_query: WITH px AS (SELECT ticker,date,close,EXTRACT(year FROM date) yr,EXTRACT(month FROM date) mo FROM prices WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','UNVR','AMRT','SIDO','GGRM','ACES','TAPG','CPIN','KLBF')), mc AS (SELECT ticker,yr,mo,last(close ORDER BY date) eom,first(close ORDER BY date) som FROM px GROUP BY 1,2,3) SELECT ticker,mo,round(avg((eom/som-1)*100),2) avg_mret,count(*) n FROM mc WHERE mo BETWEEN 1 AND 5 GROUP BY ticker,mo ORDER BY ticker,mo; -- isolate the monthly seasonal shape Feb-Apr per name

run_query: -- Lebaran-anchored windows (anchors 2024-04-10, 2025-03-31, 2026-03-20). For each name take arg_max(close) at T-45, T-5, T+25 via ASOF logic, then avg run-up (T-45->T-5) vs post (T-5->T+25) across the 3 cycles to separate priced-in run-up from post-event re-rate

run_query: -- same anchored windows but year-by-year (not averaged) to check consistency / kill outliers e.g. UNVR 2025 +41% post bounce vs flat other years; require the sign to hold across all 3 cycles before trusting it

screen_stocks: sector IN ('Barang Konsumen Primer') AND mcap_tn > 5 sort by liquidity (avg daily value) desc — to confirm the seasonal candidates are tradable size and cross-check against the brief's staples list (UNVR/ICBP/INDF/MYOR/AMRT/SIDO/GGRM, +TAPG/SSMS/NSSS for CPO)

picks.rationale: Cleanest pre-Lebaran momentum name in the data: positive run-up T-45->T-5 in ALL 3 cycles (+13.8/+10.8/+10.7%) plus positive post — CPO/cooking-oil demand feeds the P&L directly; this is the one to BUY before Lebaran (entry ~Feb).

Most consistent post-Lebaran name: positive post-event return in all 3 years (+6.7/+4.5/+2.0%), high-ROE (37) defensive herbal/health staple — accumulate ~T-5 into the event for the volume-confirmation re-rate.
Textbook post-Lebaran mean-reversion: sells off hard into the event (-10.7% avg, -24% in 2026) then bounces (+18.7% post in 2025) — do NOT chase the run-up; buy the dip just before/at Lebaran.

Large-cap packaged-food proxy with the same shape (-10% run-up, +0.7% post); the -29% trailing-1y selloff in the brief means it enters this Lebaran cheap, improving the post-event risk/reward — buy weakness in March, not strength in Q4.

Direct Ramadan-consumption read-through (minimarket retail) and the deepest run-up de-rate (-12.7% avg) confirming demand is NOT bid into the event; also -29% trailing-1y — a late-March entry plays the post-Lebaran recovery, not a Q4 hold.

Pharma/consumer-health with a strong but lumpy post-Lebaran bounce (+12.7% in 2025); pairs with SIDO on the post-event theme — entry near the event, size small given the 2026 cycle was weak (-16% run-up, -0.5% post).

answers.risk: Two hard limitations. (1) Sample = only 3 Lebaran cycles (~2.25y of price history, 2024-01 to 2026-04) — this is directional, NOT statistically robust; the 'post-Lebaran bounce' in staples is heavily driven by 2025 (UNVR +41%, MYOR +18.7%) and could be a one-off rather than a repeatable seasonal. (2) The thesis depends on the Q1 rotation-OUT-of-defensives unwinding after Lebaran — if BI cuts rates or IDR weakens sharply, the defensive bid returns early and the run-up de-rate doesn't happen (staples hold into the event), breaking the 'buy-the-dip-at-Lebaran' entry. Invalidation: if pre-Lebaran run-up turns positive for the packaged-staples basket (AMRT/MYOR/ICBP) in a given year, the priced-in dynamic the investor described is back and the post-event edge evaporates. TAPG's separate risk is CPO price — a CPO selloff kills its pre-Lebaran momentum regardless of consumption demand.

answers.reasoning: In Indonesia the downtrade transmission is mechanical: when BI holds the policy rate high to defend the IDR against a soft coal/nickel/CPO export cycle (which thins the current-account buffer and foreign inflows), the elevated real rate that follows stuck-low core inflation taxes consumer credit, KTA, and paylater, so the lower-middle majority trades down to the cheapest filling calories and single-serve sachet formats. That favors mass-volume value staples whose demand is price-inelastic and whose sachet-ization gains share in a downtrade (mie instan, kopi sachet) over premium/discretionary brands and modern-retail tickets that de-rate as wallet share shifts to warung and wet markets. Tellingly, ICBP and AMRT already sit on the worst trailing-1y list (-29% each) because the market pre-priced volume/margin and imported-wheat FX fear — opportunity in the staple maker (input normalization + pricing power) but a warning on the retail intermediary carrying the squeezed basket. The cleanest expressions are low-beta, high-ROE, cash-generative staples with fat dividends (SIDO, MYOR, GGRM, INDF) that compound through the cycle while paying you to wait; premium brand only wins if you believe near-term rate cuts plus a fiscal/wage impulse reflate the middle class, which is not the base case while BI is on hold.
tool_queries: screen_stocks(sector IN ['Barang Konsumen Primer'], market_cap > 5e12, sort_by=dividend_yield desc) -- defensive staples with downside cushion: surfaces SIDO, GGRM, INDF, MYOR, TAPG, YUPI

run_query: SELECT ticker, sector, return_1y, beta, dividend_yield FROM returns r JOIN latest l USING(ticker) WHERE l.sector='Barang Konsumen Primer' ORDER BY beta ASC LIMIT 20 -- isolate low-beta value staples vs high-beta discretionary

run_query: SELECT ticker, return_on_equity, profit_margins, debt_to_equity, dividend_yield, trailing_pe FROM summary WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','INDF','MYOR','SIDO','GGRM','UNVR','AMRT','MAPI','ACES','MLBI') ORDER BY return_on_equity DESC -- contrast value-staple vs premium/discretionary fundamentals (yf ratios directional only)

run_query: SELECT ticker, price_to_book, return_1y FROM latest l JOIN returns r USING(ticker) WHERE ticker IN ('ICBP','AMRT','GGRM','SIDO','INDF','MYOR') -- confirm downtrade-priced names (ICBP/AMRT -29% 1y, GGRM 0.44 P/B) to separate cheap-maker from squeezed-retailer

picks.rationale: Indofood holdco owns the Indomie franchise plus the Bogasari flour mill — the literal value-brand noodle play, vertically integrated so wheat-FX cost is partly internalized; deepest-volume downtrade beneficiary.
Mayora is the sachet champion (kopi, biscuits, energy drinks) sold via warung at sub-Rp2k price points; single-serve formats gain share exactly when the middle class downtrades.
Sido Muncul herbal/Tolak Angin: 37% ROE, low-ticket repeat-purchase staple, near-zero debt, fat payout — classic defensive compounder while BI keeps rates high.
Gudang Garam at 0.44x P/B is the deep-value downtrade proxy: kretek is sticky low-income consumption, smokers trade down within the brand ladder rather than quit; cheap with a margin-recovery option.

Indofood CBP (Indomie maker) is on the worst-1y list at -29% — market over-priced volume/FX fear into the dominant value-brand asset; contrarian buy on input-cost normalization and pricing power, not the thesis loser.
Triputra Agro (CPO) is the input-side hedge plus 8.9% yield and 34% ROE; cooking-oil/palm demand is inelastic in a downtrade, partly offsetting the soft CPO cycle that keeps BI cautious.

answers.risk: Thesis breaks if BI pivots to cuts and a fiscal/wage impulse (minimum-wage hike, social spending, or a commodity-led rebound in coal/nickel/CPO that lifts regional incomes and the IDR) reflates middle-class purchasing power — that flips the trade toward premium/discretionary (MAPI, ACES, MLBI) and modern retail (AMRT), reversing the value-staple de-rating. Stock-specific landmines: an imported-wheat/dairy/FX cost shock with no pass-through compresses INDF/ICBP/MYOR margins despite stable volume; GGRM faces excise (CHT) and volume-decline risk that can keep it a value trap; and the brief flags yf ratios as unreliable, so verify ROE/PE/yield against IDR-native fundamentals before sizing.
